t: Driver Frontal Stage 1 Deployment Control
s: The supplemental restraint system (SRS) module has detected a fault in the deployment control circuit for the first stage of the driver frontal airbag. The SRS warning lamp illuminates and airbag deployment may be impaired in a crash event.

t: Driver Frontal Stage 2 Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the deployment control circuit for the second stage of the driver frontal airbag. Dual-stage airbags use two charges for severity-dependent deployment — this code indicates the high-severity stage cannot be triggered reliably.

t: Driver Frontal Deployment Loop Resistance Low
s: The SRS module has detected that the resistance in the driver frontal airbag deployment loop is below the expected range, suggesting a short or partial short between the squib leads. The airbag warning lamp is illuminated.

t: Driver Frontal Deployment Loop Resistance High
s: The SRS module measured a higher than expected resistance in the driver frontal airbag deployment circuit. Causes include a partially open connection, corroded contacts, or a damaged clock spring conductor.

t: Driver Frontal Deployment Loop Open
s: The SRS module detects an open circuit in the driver frontal airbag deployment loop. The airbag will not deploy in a crash and the SRS warning lamp is illuminated.

t: Driver Frontal Deployment Loop Short to Ground
s: One of the deployment leads for the driver frontal airbag is shorted to ground. The SRS module disables deployment of the affected stage to prevent unintended firing.

t: Driver Frontal Deployment Loop Short to Battery
s: The driver frontal airbag deployment loop is shorted to battery voltage. This is potentially dangerous as it could cause unintended deployment; the SRS module isolates the circuit and sets the warning lamp.

t: Right Front/Passenger Frontal Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the deployment control circuit for the front passenger airbag. The airbag warning lamp activates and crash protection on the passenger side may be reduced.

t: Right Front/Passenger Frontal Stage 2 Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the deployment control circuit for the front passenger airbag. The airbag warning lamp activates and crash protection on the passenger side may be reduced.

t: Right Front/Passenger Frontal Stage 2 Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the second-stage deployment circuit of the front passenger airbag. The dual-stage charge cannot be triggered as designed and the airbag warning lamp is illuminated.

t: Passenger Knee Bolster Deployment Control
s: Fault on the deployment control circuit of the passenger knee airbag (knee bolster). Restricts lower-body motion of the passenger in a frontal collision.

t: Driver Knee Airbag Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the driver knee airbag deployment control circuit. The knee airbag may not deploy in a crash event.

t: Passenger Knee Airbag Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the passenger knee airbag deployment control circuit. Same fault profile as B0014 but for the passenger side.

t: Right Front/Passenger Frontal Deployment Loop Short to Ground
s: The SRS module detected a short to ground in the front passenger airbag deployment circuit. Deployment of the affected stage is inhibited and the warning lamp is illuminated.

t: Right Front/Passenger Frontal Deployment Loop Short to Battery
s: The front passenger airbag deployment loop is shorted to battery voltage, creating a risk of unintended deployment. The SRS module disables the circuit and stores this fault.

t: Right Front/Passenger Frontal Deployment Loop Resistance High
s: The SRS module measures a higher than expected resistance in the passenger frontal deployment loop, indicating partial corrosion or damage. The airbag may not deploy reliably.

t: Right Front/Passenger Frontal Deployment Loop Resistance Low
s: The SRS module detects too low a resistance in the front passenger airbag deployment loop, suggesting a partial short. Deployment of that stage is inhibited.

t: Center Frontal Stage 3 Deployment Control
s: Fault on the deployment circuit of the center frontal airbag, deployment stage 3 (highest severity). Used in dual or triple-stage front airbag systems for severe crash energy.

t: Left Side Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the deployment control circuit for the left (driver) side airbag, typically located in the seat or door. The airbag warning lamp illuminates.

t: Left Side Deployment Stage 1 Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the deployment control circuit for the left side (driver) airbag stage 1. Side impact protection on the driver side may be reduced.

t: Left Side Deployment Stage 1 Loop Open
s: An open circuit has been detected in the deployment loop for the left side airbag stage 1. The airbag will not fire and the SRS lamp is illuminated.

t: Left Side Deployment Stage 1 Loop Short to Ground
s: The left side airbag stage 1 deployment loop is shorted to ground. Deployment is inhibited and the SRS warning lamp is on.

t: Right Side Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the deployment control circuit for the left (driver) side airbag, typically located in the seat or door. The airbag warning lamp illuminates.

t: Left Side Deployment Stage 2 Control
s: A fault is detected in the deployment control for the second stage of the left side airbag, used in vehicles with two-stage side restraint systems.

t: Left Side Deployment Stage 2 Loop Open
s: An open circuit is present in the second-stage deployment loop of the left side airbag.

t: Driver Side Curtain Air Bag Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the deployment control circuit for the driver side curtain (head) airbag, which deploys from the headliner during a side impact or rollover.

t: Right Side Curtain Air Bag Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the deployment control circuit for the driver side curtain (head) airbag, which deploys from the headliner during a side impact or rollover.

t: Right Curtain Deployment Control 2
s: Fault on the second deployment circuit of the right side curtain airbag. Curtain airbags deploy along the side glass to protect head and neck in a side or rollover crash.

t: Driver Side Curtain Airbag Stage 2
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the driver-side curtain airbag stage 2 deployment circuit. Two-stage curtain airbags use a second charge for severe lateral impacts.

t: Passenger Side Curtain Airbag Stage 2
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the passenger-side curtain airbag stage 2 deployment circuit. Same fault profile as B0030 but for the passenger side.

t: Right Side Deployment Stage 1 Loop Open
s: An open circuit is detected in the right side (passenger) airbag stage 1 deployment loop.

t: Right Side Deployment Stage 1 Loop Short to Ground
s: The deployment loop for the right side airbag stage 1 is shorted to ground; deployment is inhibited.

t: Right Side Deployment Stage 2 Control
s: The SRS module reports a fault in the deployment circuit of the right side airbag stage 2.

t: Right Side Deployment Stage 2 Loop Short to Ground
s: The right side airbag stage 2 deployment loop is shorted to ground; the SRS module disables that stage.

t: Second Row Right Side Airbag Deployment Control
s: Fault on the deployment circuit of the second-row right-side airbag (built into the seat or door for rear-passenger side-impact protection).

t: Second Row Right Frontal Stage 1 Deployment Control
s: Fault on the second-row right frontal airbag, stage 1 deployment (low severity). Found on vehicles with rear-passenger frontal airbags integrated in the front seatbacks.

t: Second Row Right Frontal Stage 2 Deployment Control
s: Fault on second-row right frontal airbag, stage 2 deployment (medium severity).

t: Second Row Right Frontal Stage 3 Deployment Control
s: Fault on second-row right frontal airbag, stage 3 deployment (high severity).

t: Outside Temperature Sensor Circuit
s: The HVAC or body control module has detected an implausible signal from the outside (ambient) temperature sensor. The displayed outside temperature may be wrong or missing and automatic climate control may behave incorrectly.

t: Third Row Left Frontal Stage 1 Deployment Control
s: Fault on the third-row left frontal airbag, stage 1 deployment.

t: Side Deployment Loop Open (Generic)
s: A generic open-circuit fault has been reported in a side airbag deployment loop. Refer to vehicle service data to determine the affected side and stage.

t: Third Row Left Frontal Stage 3 Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row left frontal airbag, stage 3 deployment.

t: Left Curtain Deployment Loop Open
s: An open circuit has been detected in the deployment loop of the left curtain (head) airbag. Curtain protection on the driver side will not deploy.

t: Left Curtain Deployment Loop Short to Ground
s: The left curtain airbag deployment loop is shorted to ground; the SRS module inhibits deployment.

t: Third Row Right Side Airbag Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row right-side airbag deployment circuit.

t: Third Row Right Frontal Stage 1 Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row right frontal airbag, stage 1 deployment.

t: Third Row Right Frontal Stage 2 Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row right frontal airbag, stage 2 deployment.

t: Third Row Right Frontal Stage 3 Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row right frontal airbag, stage 3 deployment.

t: Driver Pretensioner Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the deployment control circuit for the driver seat belt pretensioner. The pretensioner pulls the belt taut during a crash and may not function reliably with this fault active.

t: Driver Frontal Deployment Control Open
s: The SRS module has detected an open circuit in the driver frontal airbag deployment loop. Resistance is above the upper threshold, indicating broken wiring or a disconnected igniter.

t: Passenger Seat Belt Sensor
s: Fault on the passenger seat belt buckle/tongue sensor that detects whether the belt is fastened.

t: Passenger Pretensioner Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the deployment control circuit for the driver seat belt pretensioner. The pretensioner pulls the belt taut during a crash and may not function reliably with this fault active.

t: Second Row Center Seat Belt Sensor
s: Fault on the second-row center seat belt buckle sensor.

t: Second Row Right Seat Belt Sensor
s: Fault on the second-row right seat belt buckle sensor.

t: Right Curtain Deployment Loop Open
s: The right curtain airbag deployment circuit shows an open condition; the curtain on the passenger side cannot deploy.

t: Right Curtain Deployment Loop Short to Ground
s: The right curtain airbag deployment loop is shorted to ground.

t: Third Row Right Seat Belt Sensor
s: Fault on the third-row right seat belt buckle sensor.

t: Knee Airbag Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the deployment control circuit of a knee airbag. Knee protection in a frontal collision may be reduced.

t: Knee Airbag Deployment Loop Open
s: An open is detected in the knee airbag deployment loop.

t: Knee Airbag Deployment Loop Short to Ground
s: The knee airbag deployment loop is shorted to ground.

t: Driver Seat Belt Tension Sensor
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the driver seat belt tension sensor. This sensor measures belt load and is used by adaptive restraint systems to scale airbag deployment force based on occupant size.

t: Vehicle Rollover Sensor
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the rollover sensor circuit. The rollover sensor triggers curtain airbags and seat belt pretensioners when the vehicle approaches a tip-over angle.

t: First Row Center Seat Belt Pretensioner Deployment Control
s: Fault on the first-row center seat belt pretensioner deployment circuit. Pretensioners pull belt slack tight at the moment of impact.

t: Driver Seatbelt Pretensioner Loop Open
s: The SRS module detects an open circuit in the driver seatbelt pretensioner deployment loop. The pretensioner cannot fire and the airbag warning lamp is on.

t: Driver Seatbelt Pretensioner Loop Short to Ground
s: The driver seatbelt pretensioner deployment circuit is shorted to ground.

t: Driver Seatbelt Pretensioner Loop Short to Battery
s: The driver seatbelt pretensioner deployment circuit is shorted to battery voltage, risking unintended firing.

t: Second Row Right Seat Belt Pretensioner Deployment Control
s: Fault on the second-row right seat belt pretensioner deployment circuit.

t: Driver Seatbelt Pretensioner Resistance Low
s: The pretensioner deployment loop resistance is below the expected window, suggesting a partial short.

t: Driver Seatbelt Pretensioner Resistance High
s: The driver seatbelt pretensioner loop resistance is above the expected window. The pretensioner may not fire reliably.

t: Third Row Right Seat Belt Pretensioner Deployment Control
s: Fault on the third-row right seat belt pretensioner deployment circuit.

t: Driver Seat Belt Pretensioner B Deployment Control
s: Fault on the driver seat belt pretensioner B deployment circuit. Pretensioner B is a secondary tightening stage on systems with multi-stage pretensioners.

t: Passenger Seat Belt Pretensioner B Deployment Control
s: Fault on passenger seat belt pretensioner B deployment circuit.

t: Second Row Left Seat Belt Pretensioner B Deployment Control
s: Fault on second-row left seat belt pretensioner B deployment circuit.

t: Second Row Right Seat Belt Pretensioner B Deployment Control
s: Fault on second-row right seat belt pretensioner B deployment circuit.

t: Second Row Center Seat Belt Pretensioner B Deployment Control
s: Fault on second-row center seat belt pretensioner B deployment circuit.

t: Driver Seat Belt Pretensioner C Deployment Control
s: Fault on driver seat belt pretensioner C deployment circuit. Pretensioner C is a third-stage tightener on systems with three-stage pretensioners.

t: Passenger Seat Belt Pretensioner C Deployment Control
s: Fault on passenger seat belt pretensioner C deployment circuit.

t: Driver Seat Belt Load Limiter Deployment Control
s: Fault on the driver seat belt load limiter deployment circuit. Load limiters release belt webbing under high force to limit chest loads after the pretensioner has tightened the belt.

t: Front Passenger Belt Switch Circuit
s: The SRS or body control module has detected a fault in the front passenger seat belt buckle switch circuit. The switch reports whether the belt is fastened and is used for the belt warning lamp and for restraint logic.

t: Passenger Seatbelt Pretensioner Loop Open
s: The passenger seatbelt pretensioner deployment loop is open; the pretensioner will not fire.

t: Passenger Seatbelt Pretensioner Loop Short to Ground
s: The passenger pretensioner deployment circuit is shorted to ground.

t: Driver Belt Buckle Switch Circuit
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the driver belt buckle switch circuit. The fastened-state of the seat belt cannot be reliably read — affects belt warning and airbag deployment logic.

t: Passenger Belt Buckle Switch Circuit
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the passenger belt buckle switch circuit. Same fault profile as B0084 but for the passenger side.

t: Passenger Seatbelt Pretensioner Resistance Low
s: The passenger seatbelt pretensioner loop has below-normal resistance, indicating a partial short.

t: Passenger Seatbelt Pretensioner Resistance High
s: The passenger pretensioner loop resistance is too high, suggesting corroded contacts or partial breaks.

t: Third Row Right Seat Belt Load Limiter Deployment Control
s: Fault on the third-row right seat belt load limiter deployment circuit.

t: Driver Side Pretensioner Stage 2 Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the second-stage circuit of the driver seat belt pretensioner. Multi-stage pretensioners use additional charges for severe crashes — this stage cannot be triggered reliably.

t: Passenger Side Pretensioner Stage 2 Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the second-stage circuit of the driver seat belt pretensioner. Multi-stage pretensioners use additional charges for severe crashes — this stage cannot be triggered reliably.

t: Side Impact Sensor Left Front Malfunction
s: The SRS module reports that the left front side impact sensor is malfunctioning. Side airbag deployment may not occur correctly in a side crash.

t: Side Impact Sensor Left Front Communication Lost
s: The SRS module has lost communication with the left front side impact sensor over the dedicated bus.

t: Side Impact Sensor Right Front Malfunction
s: The right front side impact sensor reports a malfunction or fails plausibility checks against the SRS module.

t: Occupant Classification System Performance
s: The SRS module reports that the occupant classification system (OCS) on the passenger seat does not behave plausibly. The OCS detects whether the seat is empty, occupied by a small person, or by an adult — and adapts airbag deployment accordingly.

t: Side Impact Sensor Left Rear Malfunction
s: The left rear side impact sensor is reporting a fault.

t: Side Impact Sensor Right Rear Malfunction
s: The right rear side impact sensor is reporting a malfunction or implausible signal.

t: Occupant Detection Sensor
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the occupant detection sensor on the passenger seat. The sensor input is missing or out of range so passenger airbag enable/disable logic cannot be evaluated.

t: Occupant Detection Module Internal Fault
s: The occupant detection module reports an internal fault detected by self-test. The module typically processes seat sensor data and reports occupancy status to the SRS module.

t: Left Side Restraints Sensor 4
s: Fault on left-side restraints sensor 4. Side impact sensors detect lateral acceleration to trigger side and curtain airbags.

t: Left Side Restraints Sensor 5
s: Fault on left-side restraints sensor 5.

t: Left Side Restraints Sensor 6
s: Fault on left-side restraints sensor 6.

t: Right Side Restraints Sensor 4
s: Fault on right-side restraints sensor 4.

t: Right Side Restraints Sensor 5
s: Fault on right-side restraints sensor 5.

t: Right Side Restraints Sensor 6
s: Fault on right-side restraints sensor 6.

t: Occupant Classification System
s: Fault on the occupant classification system (OCS) — typically detects whether the front passenger seat is empty, holds a child seat, or has an adult, to enable/suppress passenger airbag.

t: Occupant Position System
s: Fault on the occupant position system that detects forward/rearward position of occupants, used by the airbag controller to scale deployment force.

t: Driver Seat Occupant Classification Sensor A
s: Fault on driver seat occupant classification sensor A — typically a force/weight sensing pad in the seat cushion that determines passenger weight class.

t: Driver Seat Occupant Classification Sensor B
s: Fault on driver seat occupant classification sensor B.

t: Driver Seat Occupant Classification Sensor C
s: Fault on driver seat occupant classification sensor C.

t: Driver Seat Occupant Classification Sensor D
s: Fault on driver seat occupant classification sensor D.

t: Driver Seat Occupant Classification Sensor E
s: Fault on driver seat occupant classification sensor E.

t: Driver Seat Track Position Restraints Sensor
s: Fault on the driver seat track position sensor — detects fore/aft seat position, used by the airbag controller to adapt deployment to seat distance from steering wheel.

t: Driver Seat Recline Position Restraints Sensor
s: Fault on the driver seat recline position sensor — detects backrest angle, used by the airbag controller for deployment scaling.

t: Driver Seat Occupant Position Sensor A
s: Fault on driver seat occupant position sensor A — detects occupant proximity to airbag (typically capacitive or ultrasonic).

t: Driver Seat Occupant Position Sensor B
s: Fault on driver seat occupant position sensor B.

t: Driver Seat Occupant Position Sensor C
s: Fault on driver seat occupant position sensor C.

t: Driver Seat Occupant Position Sensor D
s: Fault on driver seat occupant position sensor D.

t: Driver Seat Occupant Position Sensor E
s: Fault on driver seat occupant position sensor E.

t: Passenger Seat Occupant Classification Sensor A
s: Fault on passenger seat occupant classification sensor A — used to determine if the seat holds a child, adult or is empty (gates the passenger airbag).

t: Passenger Seat Occupant Classification Sensor B
s: Fault on passenger seat occupant classification sensor B.

t: Passenger Seat Occupant Classification Sensor C
s: Fault on passenger seat occupant classification sensor C.

t: Passenger Seat Occupant Classification Sensor D
s: Fault on passenger seat occupant classification sensor D.

t: Passenger Seat Occupant Classification Sensor E
s: Fault on passenger seat occupant classification sensor E.

t: Passenger Seat Track Position Restraints Sensor
s: Fault on the passenger seat track position sensor.

t: Passenger Seat Recline Position Restraints Sensor
s: Fault on the passenger seat recline position sensor.

t: Passenger Seat Occupant Position Sensor A
s: Fault on passenger seat occupant position sensor A.

t: Passenger Seat Occupant Position Sensor B
s: Fault on passenger seat occupant position sensor B.

t: Passenger Seat Occupant Position Sensor C
s: Fault on passenger seat occupant position sensor C.

t: Passenger Seat Occupant Position Sensor D
s: Fault on passenger seat occupant position sensor D.

t: Passenger Seat Occupant Position Sensor E
s: Fault on passenger seat occupant position sensor E.

t: Driver Seat Belt Indicator
s: Fault on the driver seat belt warning indicator (lamp/icon in cluster).

t: Passenger Seat Belt Indicator
s: Fault on the passenger seat belt warning indicator.

t: Restraint System Malfunction Indicator 1
s: Fault on the SRS (airbag) malfunction warning indicator 1 — usually the airbag warning lamp in the cluster.

t: Restraint System Malfunction Indicator 2
s: Fault on SRS malfunction warning indicator 2 (secondary indicator).

t: Restraint System Malfunction Audible Indicator
s: Fault on the audible warning (chime) for SRS malfunction.

t: Restraint System Passenger Disable Indicator
s: Fault on the indicator that shows the passenger airbag is disabled (typically a "PASSENGER AIRBAG OFF" lamp).

t: Passenger Restraints Disable Switch
s: Fault on the key-operated switch that manually disables the passenger airbag (typically used when fitting a rear-facing child seat).

t: Third Row Left Seat Belt Pretensioner B Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row left seat belt pretensioner B deployment circuit.

t: Third Row Right Seat Belt Pretensioner B Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row right seat belt pretensioner B deployment circuit.

t: Third Row Center Seat Belt Pretensioner B Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row center seat belt pretensioner B deployment circuit.

t: Second Row Left Seat Belt Pretensioner C Deployment Control
s: Fault on second-row left seat belt pretensioner C deployment circuit.

t: Second Row Right Seat Belt Pretensioner C Deployment Control
s: Fault on second-row right seat belt pretensioner C deployment circuit.

t: Second Row Center Seat Belt Pretensioner C Deployment Control
s: Fault on second-row center seat belt pretensioner C deployment circuit.

t: Third Row Right Seat Belt Pretensioner C Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row right seat belt pretensioner C deployment circuit.

t: Third Row Left Seat Belt Pretensioner C Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row left seat belt pretensioner C deployment circuit.

t: Third Row Center Seat Belt Pretensioner C Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row center seat belt pretensioner C deployment circuit.

t: Driver Inflatable Seat Belt Deployment Control
s: Fault on the deployment circuit of the driver inflatable seat belt. Inflatable belts (e.g. Ford rear-seat system) widen the belt webbing on impact to spread crash loads over a larger area.

t: Passenger Inflatable Seat Belt Deployment Control
s: Fault on the passenger inflatable seat belt deployment circuit.

t: Second Row Left Inflatable Seat Belt Deployment Control
s: Fault on second-row left inflatable seat belt deployment circuit.

t: Second Row Center Inflatable Seat Belt Deployment Control
s: Fault on second-row center inflatable seat belt deployment circuit.

t: Second Row Right Inflatable Seat Belt Deployment Control
s: Fault on second-row right inflatable seat belt deployment circuit.

t: Third Row Left Inflatable Seat Belt Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row left inflatable seat belt deployment circuit.

t: Third Row Center Inflatable Seat Belt Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row center inflatable seat belt deployment circuit.

t: Third Row Right Inflatable Seat Belt Deployment Control
s: Fault on third-row right inflatable seat belt deployment circuit.

t: Driver Door Lock Cylinder Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the driver door lock cylinder switch circuit. This switch detects key rotation in the door lock and is used for keyless functions and the alarm system.

t: Front Crash Sensor Circuit Malfunction
s: A fault has been detected in the front crash sensor circuit, used to detect frontal impacts and trigger airbags/pretensioners.

t: Power Mirror Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the power mirror switch circuit. The switch input does not match a valid pattern — mirror adjustment may be inoperative.

t: Front Crash Sensor Left Communication Lost
s: Communication with the left front crash sensor has been lost; SRS deployment logic for frontal impacts is degraded.

t: Driver Mirror Adjust Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the driver-side mirror adjust switch circuit. The mirror cannot be moved via the switch on the driver door.

t: Front Crash Sensor Right Communication Lost
s: Communication with the right front crash sensor has been lost.

t: Passenger Mirror Adjust Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the passenger-side mirror adjust switch circuit. Same fault profile as B0104 but for the passenger mirror.

t: Rollover Sensor Malfunction
s: The rollover sensor used to deploy curtain airbags or trigger fuel cutoff in a rollover event has failed plausibility checks.

t: Mirror Folder Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the mirror folder switch circuit. Power-folding mirrors cannot be folded or unfolded via the switch.

t: LIDAR Washer Actuator D
s: Fault on LIDAR washer actuator D — sprays cleaning fluid onto the LIDAR sensor lens to keep it free of dirt, snow or insects.

t: Camera Washer Actuator A
s: Fault on camera washer actuator A — sprays fluid on a camera lens (front camera, surround-view, etc.) to keep ADAS vision systems clear.

t: Camera Washer Actuator B
s: Fault on camera washer actuator B. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Camera Washer Actuator C
s: Fault on camera washer actuator C. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Camera Washer Actuator D
s: Fault on camera washer actuator D. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Camera Washer Actuator E
s: Fault on camera washer actuator E. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Camera Washer Actuator F
s: Fault on camera washer actuator F. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Driver Pelvic/Thorax Airbag Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a deployment control fault for the driver pelvic/thorax airbag commonly integrated into the seat backrest.

t: Camera Washer Actuator H
s: Fault on camera washer actuator H. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Camera Washer Actuator J
s: Fault on camera washer actuator J. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Camera Washer Actuator K
s: Fault on camera washer actuator K. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Camera Washer Actuator L
s: Fault on camera washer actuator L. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Camera Washer Actuator M
s: Fault on camera washer actuator M. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Camera Washer Actuator N
s: Fault on camera washer actuator N. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Camera Washer Actuator O
s: Fault on camera washer actuator O. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

s: Fault on camera washer actuator P. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.
