s: Fault on camera washer actuator Q. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Camera Washer Actuator R
s: Fault on camera washer actuator R. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Camera Washer Actuator S
s: Fault on camera washer actuator S. The cam phaser actuator adjusts intake/exhaust timing under load — a stuck or slow phaser kills mid-rpm torque and can throw correlation codes between cam and crank.

t: Fluid Reservoir Level Sensor A
s: Fault on fluid reservoir level sensor A. Tracks fluid level in a wash, brake or coolant reservoir.

t: Fluid Reservoir Level Sensor B
s: Fault on fluid reservoir level sensor B.

t: Fluid Reservoir Level Sensor C
s: Fault on fluid reservoir level sensor C.

t: Fluid Reservoir Level Sensor D
s: Fault on fluid reservoir level sensor D.

t: Passenger Pelvic/Thorax Airbag Deployment Control
s: The SRS module has detected a deployment control fault for the passenger pelvic/thorax airbag.

t: Fluid Reservoir Level Sensor F
s: Fault on fluid reservoir level sensor F.

t: Pedestrian Alert Speaker A
s: Fault on pedestrian alert speaker A — emits an artificial driving sound at low speed on EVs/hybrids (legally required, e.g. UNECE R138, FMVSS 141).

t: Pedestrian Alert Speaker B
s: Fault on pedestrian alert speaker B (second/redundant speaker).

t: Night Vision Camera
s: Fault on the night vision camera (typically a thermal/far-infrared camera that detects pedestrians and animals beyond headlight range).

t: Driver Side Mirror Position Sensor
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the driver-side mirror position sensor. Memory mirror positions cannot be saved or restored reliably.

t: Passenger Side Mirror Position Sensor
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the passenger-side mirror position sensor. Same fault profile as B0125 but for the passenger mirror.

t: DC/AC Converter A High Voltage Power Outlet "A" Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on the high-voltage power outlet "A" of DC/AC converter A. The outlet supplies 110/230 V AC from the HV battery on EVs/hybrids (vehicle-to-load).

t: Passenger Occupant Detection System Malfunction
s: The passenger occupant detection system (ODS), used to enable/disable the passenger airbag based on weight, has reported a malfunction.

t: DC/AC Converter A High Voltage Power Outlet "C" Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on the high-voltage power outlet "C" of DC/AC converter A.

t: DC/AC Converter A High Voltage Power Outlet "D" Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on the high-voltage power outlet "D" of DC/AC converter A.

t: DC/AC Converter A High Voltage Power Outlet "E" Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on the high-voltage power outlet "E" of DC/AC converter A.

t: DC/AC Converter A High Voltage Power Outlet Control Module "A"
s: Fault reported by control module "A" of the high-voltage power outlet on DC/AC converter A.

t: DC/AC Converter A High Voltage Power Outlet Control Module "B"
s: Fault reported by control module "B".

t: DC/AC Converter A High Voltage Power Outlet Control Module "C"
s: Fault reported by control module "C".

t: A/V Sensor Washer Fluid A Control Circuit
s: Fault on the control circuit of the audio/video sensor washer fluid pump A. Sprays cleaning fluid on ADAS audio/video sensor lenses.

t: Driver Seatbelt Tension Sensor Circuit
s: The driver seatbelt tension sensor circuit is faulty. This sensor is used by the SRS to adjust deployment based on belt usage.

t: A/V Sensor Washer Fluid C Control Circuit
s: Fault on the audio/video sensor washer fluid pump C control circuit.

t: A/V Sensor Washer Fluid D Control Circuit
s: Fault on the A/V sensor washer fluid pump D control circuit.

t: A/V Sensor Washer Fluid E Control Circuit
s: Fault on the A/V sensor washer fluid pump E control circuit.

t: A/V Sensor Washer Fluid F Control Circuit
s: Fault on the A/V sensor washer fluid pump F control circuit.

t: Passenger Seatbelt Tension Sensor Circuit
s: The passenger seatbelt tension sensor circuit is faulty.

t: HVAC Mode Door Range/Performance
s: The HVAC control module has detected that the air distribution mode door (e.g. floor/face/defrost) does not reach the commanded position within the expected time. The actuator may be stuck, the door binding, or the position feedback inaccurate.

t: Driver Buckle Switch Circuit Malfunction
s: The driver seatbelt buckle switch circuit, used to detect whether the belt is fastened, is malfunctioning. The seatbelt warning may be permanently on or off.

t: Passenger Buckle Switch Circuit Malfunction
s: The passenger seatbelt buckle switch circuit is malfunctioning.

t: Sun Load Sensor Circuit
s: The HVAC control module has detected an implausible signal from the sun load (solar) sensor, typically located on the dashboard. Automatic climate control uses this signal to bias cooling on the side facing the sun.

t: Ambient Air Temperature Sensor Circuit
s: The HVAC control module has detected an implausible signal from the ambient air temperature sensor used by the climate control system. Symptoms include incorrect outside temperature display and erratic automatic climate behavior.

t: Outside Temperature Sensor Range/Performance
s: The HVAC control module reports an out-of-range or implausible reading from the outside air temperature sensor. The displayed value may be stuck or impossibly high/low.

t: In-Vehicle Temperature Sensor Circuit
s: The HVAC control module has detected an implausible signal from the in-vehicle temperature sensor — typically an aspirated sensor in the dashboard. Automatic climate control regulates cabin temperature using this signal.

t: Sun Load Sensor 2 Circuit
s: The HVAC control module has detected an implausible signal from the second sun load sensor. Dual-zone systems use two solar sensors to detect side-asymmetric sunlight and adjust left/right airflow temperature.

t: Air Bag In-Vehicle Temperature Sensor Circuit
s: A fault is detected in the in-vehicle temperature sensor circuit used by the SRS module for deployment compensation.

t: HVAC Discharge Air Temperature Sensor
s: The HVAC control module reports an implausible signal from the discharge (vent outlet) air temperature sensor. This sensor verifies that the actual outlet air temperature matches the commanded value.

t: HVAC Air Mix Door Performance
s: The HVAC control module has detected that the air mix (blend) door does not reach the commanded position within the expected time. The blend door regulates the ratio of air through the heater core versus around it.

t: HVAC Mode Door Position Sensor
s: The HVAC control module reports an implausible position signal from the mode (air distribution) door feedback sensor. Without valid feedback the actuator cannot be regulated to the correct position.

t: HVAC Air Inlet Door
s: The HVAC control module has detected a fault on the fresh-air/recirculation inlet door — typically a stuck actuator or position feedback out of range.

t: HVAC Recirculation Door Performance
s: The HVAC control module has detected that the recirculation door does not reach the commanded position within the expected time. The recirc door switches between fresh air and recirculated cabin air.

t: Front Blower Motor Circuit Low
s: The HVAC control module has detected low voltage or a short to ground in the front blower motor circuit. The blower may run too slow or not at all.

t: Right Front/Passenger Side Buckle Tension Sensor Circuit
s: The right front buckle tension sensor circuit is reporting a malfunction.

t: Rear Blower Motor Circuit
s: The HVAC control module has detected a fault in the rear blower motor circuit, used in vehicles with separate rear climate zones. Symptoms include no rear airflow or rear blower stuck on.

t: Front Blower Motor Speed Performance
s: The HVAC control module has detected that the front blower motor does not run at the commanded speed. Possible causes include a worn motor, blocked airway, or driver module fault.

t: Rear Wiper Motor Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the rear wiper motor circuit. Possible faults include an open or shorted motor winding, a stuck mechanism, or wiring damage in the tailgate harness.

t: Front Wiper Park Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the front wiper park switch circuit. The wipers may not return to the park position when switched off.

t: Front Wiper Speed Performance
s: The body control module has detected that the front wiper does not run at the commanded speed. Possible causes include a worn motor, binding linkage, or excessive load.

t: Air Bag System Voltage Out of Range
s: The SRS module has detected that its supply voltage is outside the operating range, preventing reliable deployment readiness.

t: HVAC Defroster Door Position
s: The HVAC control module has detected that the defroster door (windshield air outlet flap) does not reach the commanded position. Defrosting performance may be reduced.

t: Rain Sensor Module
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the rain sensor module on the windshield. Automatic wiper activation based on moisture detection is no longer available.

t: Front Wiper Motor Speed Circuit
s: The body control module has detected that the front wiper motor speed feedback does not match the commanded speed. The motor may run only on one speed or not at all.

t: SRS Indicator Lamp Circuit Malfunction
s: The SRS module has detected a fault in the airbag warning lamp circuit. The lamp may be inoperative, which prevents driver notification of stored faults.

t: Headlamp Auto-Leveling Front Sensor
s: The body control module reports an implausible signal from the front ride-height sensor used by the automatic headlamp leveling system. Without valid input, the headlamp pitch cannot be corrected for vehicle load.

t: Front Fog Lamp Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the front fog lamp circuit — typically an open bulb, blown fuse, or wiring fault. One or both fog lamps may not work.

t: Front Marker Lamp Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the front side marker lamp circuit. The lamp does not match the commanded state — the bulb may be burned out or the wiring is open.

t: Rear Tail Lamp Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the rear tail lamp circuit. The lamp does not match the commanded state.

t: Brake Lamp Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the brake lamp circuit. One or more brake lamps do not match the commanded state — a safety-relevant fault.

t: Backup Lamp Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the backup (reverse) lamp circuit. The lamp does not match the commanded state when reverse gear is engaged.

t: License Plate Lamp Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the license plate lamp circuit. The lamp does not match the commanded state.

t: Headlamp Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected an implausible state at the headlamp switch — for example simultaneous low and high beam request, or no signal at all. Lighting functions may be erratic.

t: Driver Window Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the driver window switch circuit. The window may not respond to the switch in the driver door.

t: Passenger Window Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the passenger window switch circuit. Same fault profile as B0245 but for the passenger window.

t: HVAC Air Temperature Internal Sensor Circuit
s: The HVAC controller has detected a fault in the internal cabin air temperature sensor circuit, used to regulate climate control output.

t: Driver Window Motor Down Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the driver window motor down-direction control circuit. The window may not lower correctly.

t: HVAC Air Temperature External Sensor Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the external (ambient) temperature sensor circuit used by the HVAC and instrument cluster.

t: HVAC Air Mix Door 1 Circuit
s: The HVAC controller reports a fault in the actuator circuit for air mix door 1, which controls the proportion of warm and cool air in the airflow.

t: Interior Light Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the interior light circuit. The lamp does not match the commanded state — common cause is a stuck door switch causing battery drain.

t: HVAC Air Mix Door 2 Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the air mix door 2 actuator circuit, used in dual-zone HVAC systems for the passenger side.

t: Driver Door Lock/Unlock Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the driver door lock/unlock switch on the door panel. Pressing the switch may not lock or unlock the doors.

t: HVAC Mode Door 1 Circuit
s: The HVAC mode door 1 actuator circuit, which routes airflow between defrost, vents, and floor, has reported a fault.

t: Passenger Door Lock Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the driver door lock/unlock switch on the door panel. Pressing the switch may not lock or unlock the doors.

t: Power Door Lock Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the power door lock switch circuit. Central locking may not respond to the door switch.

t: HVAC Recirculation Door Circuit
s: The HVAC recirculation door actuator circuit has reported a fault. Fresh/recirculated air switching may not work.

t: Trunk Lid Release Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the trunk lid release switch circuit. The trunk may not open via the interior or remote release.

t: HVAC Defrost Door Circuit
s: A fault is detected in the HVAC defrost door actuator circuit; defrost airflow may not engage.

t: HVAC Blower Motor Circuit
s: The HVAC controller reports a fault in the blower motor control circuit. Blower may run at incorrect speed or not at all.

t: HVAC A/C Pressure Sensor Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the A/C refrigerant pressure sensor circuit. The compressor may be disabled to protect the system.

t: Rear Defrost Switch Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the rear window defroster switch circuit. The switch may be stuck or its signal does not reach the module — the rear defroster cannot be activated.

t: HVAC A/C Compressor Clutch Circuit
s: The HVAC controller has detected a fault in the A/C compressor clutch control circuit. The compressor may not engage.

t: HVAC Evaporator Temperature Sensor Circuit
s: The HVAC controller reports a fault in the evaporator temperature sensor circuit, used to prevent evaporator icing.

t: HVAC Sun Load Sensor Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the sun load sensor circuit used by automatic climate control to compensate for solar gain.

t: Driver Seat Position Sensor Circuit
s: The seat module has detected an implausible signal from the driver seat position sensor. The sensor reports the fore/aft position used for memory seat recall and for SRS occupant logic.

t: Driver Seat Forward/Back Switch Circuit
s: The seat module has detected a fault in the fore/aft adjustment switch on the driver seat. Manual adjustment of the seat position may be limited or not work at all.

t: Passenger Seat Position Sensor Circuit
s: The seat module has detected an implausible signal from the driver seat position sensor. The sensor reports the fore/aft position used for memory seat recall and for SRS occupant logic.

t: Driver Seat Tilt Position Sensor
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the driver seat tilt position sensor. Memory positions for seat back tilt cannot be reliably stored.

t: Driver Seat Memory Set Switch
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the driver seat memory set switch circuit. Seat positions cannot be saved to memory presets.

t: Passenger Seat Position Memory
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the passenger seat position memory function. Saved positions cannot be reliably restored.

t: HVAC Rear Blower Motor Circuit
s: The HVAC controller reports a fault in the rear blower motor circuit, used in vehicles with rear climate zone.

t: Driver Seat Heater Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the driver seat heater circuit. The heating element or wiring is open or shorted — the seat does not heat.

t: Passenger Seat Heater Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the passenger seat heater circuit. Same fault profile as B0335 but for the passenger seat.

t: Rear Seat Heater Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the rear seat heater circuit. The rear heating elements are open or shorted.

t: HVAC Heated Seat Driver Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the driver heated seat circuit. Seat heating may be disabled.

t: HVAC Heated Seat Passenger Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the passenger heated seat circuit.

t: Audio System Internal Failure
s: The audio head unit reports an internal self-test failure. Symptoms include silent output, frozen display, or repeated reboots of the infotainment system.

t: Audio Amplifier Internal Failure
s: The audio amplifier reports an internal self-test failure. Sound output may be distorted, missing on some channels, or the amplifier enters a protection mode.

t: Bluetooth Module Fault
s: The infotainment system has detected a fault in the Bluetooth module. Pairing with phones may fail or audio streaming drops out.

t: USB Hub Fault
s: The infotainment system has detected a fault in the internal USB hub. USB ports may not detect connected devices reliably.

t: Microphone Circuit
s: The infotainment system has detected a fault in the hands-free microphone circuit. Voice commands and phone calls may have poor audio quality.

t: Display Module Internal Failure
s: The instrument cluster or central display module reports an internal self-test failure. Pixels may be missing, the screen may stay dark, or warning indicators may not display correctly.

t: Bluetooth Antenna Circuit
s: The infotainment system has detected a fault in the Bluetooth antenna circuit. Range and connection stability may be reduced.

t: Display Backlight Circuit
s: The infotainment or instrument cluster module has detected a fault in the display backlight circuit. The display may stay dark or be unreadable in low light.

t: Driver Power Window Circuit Malfunction
s: The driver power window motor or switch circuit has reported a fault. The window may not operate.

t: Passenger Power Window Circuit Malfunction
s: A fault has been detected in the passenger power window circuit.

t: Left Rear Power Window Circuit Malfunction
s: A fault has been detected in the left rear power window circuit.

t: Right Rear Power Window Circuit Malfunction
s: A fault has been detected in the right rear power window circuit.

t: Driver Door Open Switch
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the driver door open (jamb) switch. The door open state cannot be reliably read — affects interior light, seat belt warning, and remote start interlocks.

t: Passenger Door Open Switch
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the passenger door open (jamb) switch. Same fault profile as B0510 but for the passenger door.

t: Driver Door Lock Actuator Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the driver door lock actuator circuit. Central locking may not work for the driver door.

t: Passenger Door Lock Actuator Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the passenger door lock actuator circuit.

t: Left Rear Door Lock Actuator Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the left rear door lock actuator circuit.

t: Right Rear Door Lock Actuator Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the right rear door lock actuator circuit.

t: Driver Door Ajar Switch Circuit
s: The driver door ajar switch circuit is reporting an implausible signal. The interior light may stay on or door open warning may be incorrect.

t: Passenger Door Ajar Switch Circuit
s: The passenger door ajar switch circuit is malfunctioning.

t: Driver Power Seat Forward/Back Circuit
s: A fault is detected in the forward/back motor circuit of the driver power seat.

t: Driver Power Seat Up/Down Circuit
s: A fault is detected in the up/down motor circuit of the driver power seat.

t: Driver Power Seat Recline Circuit
s: A fault is detected in the recline motor circuit of the driver power seat.

t: Passenger Power Seat Forward/Back Circuit
s: A fault is detected in the forward/back motor circuit of the passenger power seat.

t: Driver Seat Memory Module Malfunction
s: The driver seat memory module reports an internal fault. Stored seat positions cannot be recalled.

t: Mirror Adjustment Driver Side Circuit
s: The driver side mirror adjustment circuit is malfunctioning. Mirror motor or position sensor fault.

t: Mirror Adjustment Passenger Side Circuit
s: The passenger side mirror adjustment circuit is malfunctioning.

t: Mirror Heater Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the heated mirror circuit. Mirrors will not defog.

t: Sunroof Open/Close Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the sunroof open/close motor circuit.

t: Sunroof Position Sensor Circuit
s: A fault is detected in the sunroof position sensor circuit; one-touch and pinch protection may not function.

t: Headlamp Switch Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the headlamp switch circuit. Light functions may not switch properly.

t: Left Headlamp Low Beam Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the left low beam headlamp circuit. Bulb may be burnt out or wiring shorted.

t: Right Headlamp Low Beam Circuit
s: The body control module has detected a fault in the right low beam headlamp circuit.

t: Left Headlamp High Beam Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the left high beam headlamp circuit.

t: Right Headlamp High Beam Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the right high beam headlamp circuit.

t: Left Front Turn Signal Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the left front turn signal circuit.

t: Right Front Turn Signal Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the right front turn signal circuit.

t: Left Rear Turn Signal Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the left rear turn signal circuit.

t: Right Rear Turn Signal Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the right rear turn signal circuit.

t: Left Brake Lamp Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the left brake lamp circuit.

t: Right Brake Lamp Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the right brake lamp circuit.

t: License Plate Lamp Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the license plate lamp circuit.

t: Wiper Motor Front Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the front wiper motor circuit.

t: Wiper Motor Rear Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the rear wiper motor circuit.

t: Washer Pump Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the windshield washer pump circuit.

t: Heated Rear Window Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the heated rear window circuit. Defrost may not function.

t: Horn Relay Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the horn relay control circuit.

t: Interior Light Circuit
s: A fault has been detected in the interior light circuit.

t: Ignition Switch Run/Start Position Circuit
s: The body control module reports an implausible signal from the ignition switch run/start position circuit.

t: Body Control Module Internal Fault
s: The body control module (BCM) has detected an internal fault and may need to be replaced.

t: TCS Control Channel A Valve 1
s: Fault on traction control system control channel A valve 1.

t: TCS Control Channel A Valve 2
s: Fault on traction control system control channel A valve 2.

t: TCS Control Channel B Valve 1
s: Fault on traction control system control channel B valve 1.

t: TCS Control Channel B Valve 2
s: Fault on traction control system control channel B valve 2.

t: Left Front Inlet Control
s: Fault on left front ABS inlet (apply) valve.

t: Left Front Outlet Control
s: Fault on left front ABS outlet (release) valve.

t: Left Front Hydraulic Release Too Long
s: Hydraulic release on left front wheel circuit took longer than expected.

t: Right Front Inlet Control
s: Fault on right front ABS inlet (apply) valve.

t: Right Front Outlet Control
s: Fault on right front ABS outlet (release) valve.

t: Right Front Hydraulic Release Too Long
s: Hydraulic release on right front wheel circuit took longer than expected.

t: Left Rear Inlet Control
s: Fault on left rear ABS inlet (apply) valve.

t: Left Rear Outlet Control
s: Fault on left rear ABS outlet (release) valve.

t: Left Rear Hydraulic Release Too Long
s: Hydraulic release on left rear wheel circuit took longer than expected.

t: Right Rear Inlet Control
s: Fault on right rear ABS inlet (apply) valve.

t: Right Rear Outlet Control
s: Fault on right rear ABS outlet (release) valve.

t: Right Rear Hydraulic Release Too Long
s: Hydraulic release on right rear wheel circuit took longer than expected.

t: ABS Pump Motor Circuit
s: The ABS control module detects an electrical fault in the pump motor circuit. Without a working pump, the system cannot build hydraulic pressure for ABS or stability control interventions and disables those functions.

t: ABS Pump Motor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The pump motor responds, but its current draw or run time is outside the expected range. The control module suspects mechanical wear or hydraulic blockage.

t: ABS Pump Motor Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the pump motor control line is below threshold. Typical causes are a short to ground or interrupted positive supply.

t: ABS Pump Motor Circuit High
s: Voltage on the pump motor control line is above the expected range, typically caused by a short to battery voltage or a stuck relay.

t: Brake Pedal Feedback Pressure Solenoid Circuit
s: Fault on brake pedal feedback pressure solenoid — used in brake-by-wire systems to provide pedal feel.

t: ABS Pump Motor Stalled
s: The pump motor draws current but does not rotate. Self-test detects a stalled rotor and disables ABS and stability control.

t: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The signal from the left front wheel speed sensor is implausible relative to the other wheels. Typical causes are excessive air gap, contaminated tone ring or partial sensor failure.

t: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the left front wheel speed sensor circuit is below the expected range. The fault is typically caused by a short to ground or an open in the supply line.

t: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit High
s: Voltage on the left front wheel speed sensor circuit is above the expected range, typically due to a short to battery voltage.

t: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor Signal Erratic
s: The control module sees erratic pulses from the left front wheel speed sensor at speed. Common cause is metallic debris on a magnetic encoder or a damaged tone ring.

t: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor No Signal
s: No pulses are received from the left front wheel speed sensor while other wheels report motion. The fault disables ABS and stability control.

t: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Malfunction
s: The ABS control module has detected a fault in the left front wheel speed sensor circuit. Loss of wheel speed information disables ABS and stability control, and a separate ABS warning lamp is illuminated.

t: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Intermittent
s: Intermittent open or short detected on the left front wheel speed sensor circuit. Often caused by chafed wiring or a loose connector.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor
s: Generic fault on left rear wheel speed sensor. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Supply
s: Fault on left rear wheel speed sensor supply circuit. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Tone Wheel
s: Fault detected on right rear tone wheel (pulse ring).

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor
s: Generic fault on right rear wheel speed sensor. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Supply
s: Fault on right rear wheel speed sensor supply circuit. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Rear Tone Wheel
s: Fault on shared rear tone wheel (single rear sensor on solid axle).

t: Rear Wheel Speed Sensor
s: Generic fault on shared rear wheel speed sensor. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Supply
s: Fault on rear wheel speed sensor supply circuit. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Wheel Speed Sensors Rotation Direction Correlation
s: Wheel speed sensors disagree on rotation direction — typically detected when reversing. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Malfunction
s: The ABS control module has detected a fault in the right front wheel speed sensor circuit. Loss of wheel speed information disables ABS and stability control, and a separate ABS warning lamp is illuminated.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The right front wheel speed signal is implausible compared with other wheels. Typical causes mirror C0030.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Low
s: Voltage in the right front wheel speed sensor circuit is below threshold, typically a short to ground.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit High
s: Voltage in the right front wheel speed sensor circuit is above threshold, typically a short to battery.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor Signal Erratic
s: Erratic pulses on the right front wheel speed sensor at speed. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Malfunction
s: The ABS control module has detected a fault in the left rear wheel speed sensor circuit. Loss of wheel speed information disables ABS and stability control, and a separate ABS warning lamp is illuminated.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Intermittent
s: Intermittent open or short on the right front wheel speed sensor circuit. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Brake Booster Pressure Sensor
s: Generic fault on brake booster pressure sensor. Boost control tracks commanded vs. actual manifold pressure; persistent deviation means wastegate, vanes, hose or actuator are not delivering the requested pressure.

t: Brake Booster Travel Sensor
s: Generic fault on brake booster travel/position sensor. Boost control tracks commanded vs. actual manifold pressure; persistent deviation means wastegate, vanes, hose or actuator are not delivering the requested pressure.

t: Brake Fluid Level
s: Brake fluid level low — typically detected by reservoir float switch.

t: Brake Lining Wear Sensor
s: Brake lining wear sensor reports thin pads.

t: Brake Fluid Level Sensor/Switch Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on brake fluid level sensor or switch.

t: Brake Booster Motor A Position Sensor Exceeded Learning Limit
s: Brake booster motor A position sensor learned offset exceeded the allowed range. Boost control tracks commanded vs. actual manifold pressure; persistent deviation means wastegate, vanes, hose or actuator are not delivering the requested pressure.

t: Brake System Pressure Slow Response
s: Brake system pressure response slower than expected.

t: Brake System Pressure Control Valve Actuator Stuck
s: Brake system pressure control valve actuator stuck mechanically.

t: ABS Control Module Over Temperature
s: ABS control module exceeded its safe operating temperature.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Malfunction
s: The ABS control module has detected a fault in the right rear wheel speed sensor circuit. Loss of wheel speed information disables ABS and stability control, and a separate ABS warning lamp is illuminated.

t: Power Steering Position Sensor Performance
s: The chassis or power steering control module has detected that the steering position signal is implausible compared to vehicle motion. Stability control may be limited because correct yaw and slip-angle calculation depends on a valid steering angle.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Low
s: Voltage in the left rear wheel speed sensor circuit is below the expected range. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit High
s: Voltage in the left rear wheel speed sensor circuit is above the expected range. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Signal Erratic
s: Erratic pulses from the left rear wheel speed sensor. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor No Signal
s: No pulses received from the left rear wheel speed sensor. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Intermittent
s: Intermittent open or short on the left rear wheel speed sensor circuit. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit
s: The ABS control module detects a fault in the right rear wheel speed sensor circuit. ABS and stability control are deactivated.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The right rear wheel speed signal is implausible compared to the other wheels. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Low
s: Voltage in the right rear wheel speed sensor circuit is below the expected range. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit High
s: Voltage in the right rear wheel speed sensor circuit is above the expected range. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Signal Erratic
s: Erratic pulses from the right rear wheel speed sensor. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor No Signal
s: No pulses received from the right rear wheel speed sensor. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor Circuit Intermittent
s: Intermittent open or short on the right rear wheel speed sensor circuit. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Yaw Rate/Longitude Sensors
s: Generic fault on yaw rate/longitudinal acceleration sensor cluster.

t: Multi-axis Acceleration Sensor
s: Generic fault on multi-axis acceleration sensor (combines lateral, longitudinal and vertical accelerations).

t: Stability System Active Too Long
s: Electronic stability control intervened for an excessive duration.

t: Stability System
s: Generic fault in electronic stability control system.

t: Vehicle Speed Sensor Circuit
s: The chassis controller detects a fault in the dedicated vehicle speed sensor circuit (when present in addition to wheel speed sensors).

t: 2/4 Wheel Drive Status Input
s: Fault on 2WD/4WD status signal input.

t: Brake Temperature Too High
s: Brake temperature exceeded safe operating limit — risk of brake fade.

t: Delivered Driving Torque
s: Engine-delivered driving torque does not match TCS request.

t: Requested Driving Torque
s: Driving torque request from TCS rejected or out of range.

t: Brake Pedal Position Performance
s: Brake pedal position sensor reading implausible.

t: PWM for Traction Control
s: PWM communication signal for traction control faulty.

t: Low Tire Pressure
s: Tire pressure monitoring system detected low tire pressure.

t: Tire Diameter
s: Detected wheel speed implies tire diameter outside expected range — typically wrong tire size or mismatched tires.

t: Variable Effort Steering
s: Generic fault on variable effort steering (VES) system.

t: Wheel Speed Mismatch (Front Axle)
s: Front axle wheel speeds differ beyond the plausibility threshold over time. Often caused by mismatched tyre rolling circumference or a partially failing sensor.

t: Wheel Speed Mismatch (Rear Axle)
s: Rear axle wheel speeds differ beyond the plausibility threshold. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Brake System Malfunction Indicator
s: Brake system malfunction indicator circuit fault.

t: Tire Pressure Monitor Malfunction Indicator
s: TPMS malfunction indicator circuit fault.

t: Traction Active Indicator
s: Traction control active indicator lamp circuit fault.

t: Traction Disable Indicator
s: Traction control disable indicator lamp circuit fault.

t: Vehicle Dynamics Indicator
s: Vehicle dynamics control indicator lamp circuit fault.

t: TCS Disable Switch
s: Fault on TCS disable switch.

t: TCS Mode Control
s: Fault on TCS mode control input (offroad/sport mode etc.).

t: Hydraulic Brake Pressure Sensor Circuit
s: The brake pressure sensor in the master cylinder or hydraulic block reports an implausible signal or no signal. The fault disables ABS, stability control and brake assist.

t: Hydraulic Brake Pressure Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The brake pressure sensor signal is outside the plausibility band relative to pedal application. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: 4WD/AWD Power Transfer Unit Position Sensor B
s: Fault on 4WD/AWD power transfer unit (PTU) position sensor B.

t: 4WD/AWD Power Transfer Unit Temperature Sensor
s: Fault on PTU temperature sensor.

t: 4WD/AWD Rear Differential Unit Temperature Sensor
s: Fault on rear differential unit temperature sensor.

t: 4WD/AWD Rear Differential Unit Actuator A
s: Fault on rear differential unit actuator A.

t: 4WD/AWD Rear Differential Unit Actuator A Data
s: Rear differential actuator A data implausible or out of range.

t: 4WD/AWD Rear Differential Unit Actuator B
s: Fault on rear differential unit actuator B.

t: 4WD/AWD Rear Differential Unit Actuator B Data
s: Rear differential actuator B data implausible or out of range.

t: ABS Control Module Internal Fault
s: The ABS control module fails its internal self-test (RAM, ROM or processor watchdog). ABS and stability control are disabled.

t: 4WD/AWD Rear Differential Unit Actuator A Position Sensor B
s: Fault on rear differential actuator A position sensor B.

t: 4WD/AWD Rear Differential Unit Actuator A Position Sensor C
s: Fault on rear differential actuator A position sensor C.

t: 4WD/AWD Rear Differential Unit Actuator A Position Sensor Correlation
s: Position sensors on rear differential actuator A disagree.

t: 4WD/AWD Rear Differential Unit Actuator B Position Sensor A
s: Fault on rear differential actuator B position sensor A.

t: 4WD/AWD Rear Differential Unit Actuator B Position Sensor B
s: Fault on rear differential actuator B position sensor B.

t: 4WD/AWD Rear Differential Unit Actuator B Position Sensor C
s: Fault on rear differential actuator B position sensor C.

t: 4WD/AWD Rear Differential Unit Actuator B Position Sensor Correlation
s: Position sensors on rear differential actuator B disagree.

t: Driveline Control Module
s: Generic fault on driveline control module.

t: Driveline Control Module Power Transfer Unit Control Circuit Driver
s: Fault on driveline control module driver for PTU control circuit.

t: Driveline Control Module Rear Differential Unit A Control Circuit Driver
s: Fault on driveline control module driver for rear differential unit A.

t: Driveline Control Module Rear Differential Unit B Control Circuit Driver
s: Fault on driveline control module driver for rear differential unit B.

t: Driveline Control Module Transfer Case Unit Control Circuit Driver
s: Fault on driveline control module driver for transfer case unit.

t: 4WD/AWD Transfer Case Unit Actuator
s: Fault on transfer case unit actuator.

t: 4WD/AWD Transfer Case Unit Actuator Position Sensor A
s: Fault on transfer case actuator position sensor A.

t: 4WD/AWD Transfer Case Unit Actuator Position Sensor B
s: Fault on transfer case actuator position sensor B.

t: ABS Pump Motor Circuit Malfunction
s: The ABS control module has detected a fault in the hydraulic pump motor circuit. The pump rebuilds brake pressure during ABS regulation — without it, ABS and stability control are disabled.

t: ABS Activation Excessively Long
s: ABS regulation has been active longer than the allowed duration during a single brake event, suggesting a sensor or hydraulic problem.

t: ABS Activation Frequency Too High
s: ABS regulation cycles activate more frequently than the plausibility model allows.

t: Valve Relay Circuit Malfunction
s: The ABS control module has detected a fault in the valve relay circuit, which supplies power to the ABS solenoid valves. With this fault, the module cannot energize the valves and ABS is disabled.

t: Low Brake Fluid Indicated
s: The brake fluid level switch in the master cylinder reservoir reports a low level. ABS and stability control may be deactivated as a precaution.

t: Master Cylinder Pressure Sensor Range/Performance
s: The ABS or stability control module has detected an implausible signal from the master cylinder brake pressure sensor. This sensor reports driver brake input and is used by ABS and ESP for control decisions.

t: Stop Lamp Switch Circuit
s: The ABS or stability control module sees an implausible brake light switch signal. Stability and brake assist functions can be limited until the fault is cleared.

t: Left Front ABS Inlet Solenoid Circuit
s: The ABS control module has detected an electrical fault in the inlet solenoid for the left front brake circuit. The solenoid isolates the wheel circuit from master cylinder pressure during ABS regulation.

t: Left Front ABS Outlet Solenoid Circuit
s: The ABS control module has detected an electrical fault in the outlet solenoid for the left front brake circuit. The outlet valve releases brake pressure to the low-pressure accumulator during ABS regulation.

t: Right Front ABS Outlet Solenoid B Circuit Malfunction
s: The ABS module has detected an electrical fault in the secondary outlet solenoid for the right front wheel. ABS and stability control are disabled while the fault is active.

t: Right Front ABS Inlet Solenoid Circuit
s: The ABS control module has detected an electrical fault in the inlet solenoid for the left front brake circuit. The solenoid isolates the wheel circuit from master cylinder pressure during ABS regulation.

t: Right Front ABS Inlet Solenoid B Circuit Malfunction
s: Electrical fault in the secondary inlet solenoid for the right front wheel. Pressure modulation for that wheel is no longer possible and ABS is disabled.

t: Right Front ABS Outlet Solenoid Circuit
s: The ABS control module has detected an electrical fault in the outlet solenoid for the left front brake circuit. The outlet valve releases brake pressure to the low-pressure accumulator during ABS regulation.

t: Left Rear ABS Inlet Solenoid Circuit Malfunction
s: Electrical fault in the inlet solenoid for the left rear wheel. ABS is disabled and the warning lamp is illuminated.

t: ABS/TCS Brake Switch Circuit
s: The ABS or traction control module has detected an implausible signal from the brake light switch. The switch tells the module whether the driver is braking and is used to inhibit traction control during braking.

t: Left Rear ABS Outlet Solenoid Circuit Malfunction
s: Electrical fault in the outlet solenoid for the left rear wheel. ABS pressure release for this wheel is no longer possible.

t: Right Rear ABS Inlet Solenoid Circuit Malfunction
s: Electrical fault in the inlet solenoid for the right rear wheel. ABS is disabled while the fault is active.

t: Right Rear ABS Outlet Solenoid Circuit Malfunction
s: Electrical fault in the outlet solenoid for the right rear wheel. ABS pressure release for this wheel is no longer possible.

t: Lateral Acceleration Sensor Range/Performance
s: The stability control module has detected that the lateral acceleration sensor signal is out of the expected range or does not match other inertial sensors. ESP and roll-stability functions may be disabled.

t: Lateral Acceleration Sensor Circuit
s: The stability control module has detected an implausible signal from the lateral acceleration sensor. ESP uses lateral g-force together with yaw rate and steering angle to detect skid conditions — without it, ESP is disabled.

t: Yaw Rate Sensor Circuit
s: The stability control module has detected an implausible signal from the yaw rate sensor. The yaw sensor measures rotation around the vertical axis and is essential for ESP — a fault disables stability control.

t: ABS Solenoid Valve Front Left Circuit
s: The ABS control module detects a fault in the inlet/outlet solenoid for the front left brake circuit. The valve is integrated in the hydraulic block; replacement is generally a workshop task.

t: Brake Fluid Level Switch Circuit
s: The ABS or brake control module has detected a fault in the brake fluid level switch circuit. A low fluid level indicates a potential leak in the hydraulic system and disables ABS while a separate brake warning lamp is illuminated.

t: Brake Pedal Position Sensor
s: The chassis or brake control module has detected an implausible signal from the brake pedal position sensor. Modern brake systems use this sensor for brake assist, regenerative braking and adaptive cruise control functions.

t: ABS Solenoid Valve Front Right Circuit
s: Fault in the front right ABS solenoid (mirror of C0200).

t: Stop Lamp Switch Circuit
s: The ABS or stability control module has detected a fault in the stop lamp switch circuit. The brake light switch tells the module whether the driver is braking and is used to coordinate ABS, traction control and cruise control.

t: ABS Solenoid Valve Rear Left Circuit
s: Fault in the rear left ABS solenoid valve.

t: ABS Solenoid Valve Rear Right Circuit
s: Fault in the rear right ABS solenoid valve.

t: ABS Valve Block Supply Voltage
s: Supply voltage to the ABS valve block is out of range, disabling all valve control.

t: ABS Valve Relay Circuit
s: The valve relay supplying the ABS solenoids does not respond as commanded.

t: Brake Pedal Position Sensor Circuit
s: The brake pedal position sensor reports an implausible signal. Required for brake-by-wire and stability control.

t: Wheel Speed Sensor Frequency Error
s: The ABS control module has detected that one of the wheel speed sensor signals has a frequency outside the plausible range, e.g. unrealistically high pulses or signal dropouts at speed. Stability control may be limited.

t: Wheel Speed Sensor Signal Erratic
s: The ABS control module has detected an erratic wheel speed sensor signal — sudden jumps or dropouts compared to the other wheels. Typical causes are a damaged tone ring or a loose sensor.

t: Loss of Speed Sensor Signal Front Left
s: The ABS control module has lost the wheel speed signal from the front left wheel during driving. ABS and stability control are disabled because wheel-individual speed information is required for slip detection.

t: Speed Wheel-to-Wheel Mismatch
s: The ABS control module has detected an implausible speed difference between wheels that cannot be explained by cornering. This typically points to one suspect sensor, mismatched tyre sizes or a damaged tone ring.

t: Brake Pressure Sensor
s: The ABS or stability control module has detected a fault in a brake pressure sensor. Pressure information is used by ESP and brake assist to determine driver intent and modulation pressure during regulation.

t: ABS Warning Lamp Control Circuit
s: The ABS warning lamp control circuit is shorted or open. The driver may see a permanently lit or non-functional ABS warning lamp.

t: PCM Indicated ABS Malfunction
s: The powertrain control module reports a fault that the ABS module must consider (e.g. traction control engine torque request fault).

t: PWM Brake Pressure Modulation Circuit
s: The PWM control of the brake pressure modulation valves is faulty.

t: Wheel Speed Sensor Frequency Mismatch
s: The ABS control module has detected that the frequency of one wheel speed sensor signal does not match the others under stable driving conditions. This often indicates a tone ring with extra or missing teeth or a sensor with degraded signal quality.

t: ABS/TCS Control Module Internal Performance
s: Internal plausibility check of the combined ABS/TCS control module fails.

t: Power Steering Pump Pressure
s: The power steering control module has detected an implausible pressure or load signal from the steering pump. On hydraulic systems this may indicate low fluid or a worn pump; on electric systems it may indicate excessive motor load.

t: Pump Motor Ground Circuit Open
s: The pump motor ground return is open. The motor cannot run, and the system disables ABS and stability control.

t: Brake Booster Vacuum Sensor Circuit
s: The brake booster vacuum sensor reports an implausible signal; brake assist functions are reduced. Boost control tracks commanded vs. actual manifold pressure; persistent deviation means wastegate, vanes, hose or actuator are not delivering the requested pressure.

t: EBCM Relay Circuit
s: The Electronic Brake Control Module has detected a fault in its main supply relay circuit. Without proper relay supply the EBCM cannot operate the hydraulic unit and ABS as well as stability control are disabled.

t: Active Brake Booster
s: The brake control module has detected a fault in the active (electric or vacuum-controlled) brake booster. Active boosters are used by emergency brake assist and adaptive cruise control to autonomously build brake pressure.

t: Brake Pump Motor Circuit
s: The ABS or brake control module has detected an electrical fault in the brake pump motor circuit. The pump is used to build brake pressure during ABS, ESP and brake-by-wire operations and a fault disables these functions.

t: Brake Pump Run Time Exceeded
s: The brake control module has detected that the brake pump has run longer than the allowed maximum time. This typically indicates the pump cannot reach target pressure due to a hydraulic leak, air in the system or a worn pump.

t: Brake Vacuum Sensor
s: The brake control module has detected an implausible signal from the vacuum sensor on the brake booster. Modern systems monitor booster vacuum to ensure brake assist is available, especially with stop-start engines and electric vacuum pumps.

t: Brake Booster Pressure Sensor Circuit Malfunction
s: The brake control module has detected an out-of-range or implausible signal from the brake booster pressure sensor. Brake assist and electronic boost functions may be limited.

t: Brake Booster Vacuum Sensor Circuit Malfunction
s: The brake control module has detected an implausible signal from the booster vacuum sensor. Loss of vacuum signal can cause hard pedal and reduced power assist.

t: Brake Booster Boost Sensor Circuit Malfunction
s: The brake control module has detected an electrical fault in the booster boost (assist pressure) sensor circuit. Power-assist regulation may default to a backup strategy.

t: ESP Performance
s: The stability control module has detected that overall ESP performance is degraded — typically a plausibility check between yaw rate, lateral acceleration, steering angle and wheel speeds has failed. ESP is deactivated until the root cause is resolved.

t: Yaw Rate Sensor Range
s: The stability control module has detected that the yaw rate sensor signal is outside the plausible range during stable driving. This typically indicates a sensor offset, an incorrect mounting or a degraded sensor element.

t: Brake Pedal Stroke Sensor Circuit Malfunction
s: The brake control module has detected an out-of-range signal from the brake pedal stroke sensor. Pedal travel information is required for brake assist and electronically boosted braking.

t: Brake Pedal Apply Sensor Circuit Malfunction
s: Electrical fault in the brake pedal apply sensor circuit. The module cannot reliably detect whether the brake pedal is pressed, which affects ABS, ESP and brake assist functions.

t: Steering Sensor Indicates Bias
s: The stability control module has detected that the steering wheel position sensor reports a non-zero angle when the vehicle is driving straight. This bias makes ESP intervene incorrectly and the system requires recalibration.

t: Inlet Valve Front Left Performance
s: The front left inlet solenoid valve does not respond hydraulically as expected during ABS self-test.

t: Air Suspension Compressor Relay
s: The air suspension control module has detected a fault in the compressor relay circuit. The compressor cannot be energized which prevents the system from refilling the air struts and ride height drops over time.

t: Outlet Valve Front Left Performance
s: The front left outlet solenoid valve does not respond hydraulically as expected.

t: Inlet Valve Front Right Performance
s: The front right inlet solenoid valve does not respond hydraulically as expected.

t: Outlet Valve Front Right Performance
s: The front right outlet solenoid valve does not respond hydraulically as expected.

t: Inlet Valve Rear Left Performance
s: The rear left inlet solenoid valve does not respond hydraulically as expected.

t: Ride Height Sensor Front Left
s: The air suspension control module has detected a fault in the front left ride height sensor circuit. Without this signal the module cannot regulate suspension height and self-levelling is disabled.

t: Inlet Valve Rear Right Performance
s: The rear right inlet solenoid valve does not respond hydraulically as expected.

t: Ride Height Sensor Front Right
s: The air suspension control module has detected a fault in the front left ride height sensor circuit. Without this signal the module cannot regulate suspension height and self-levelling is disabled.

t: Outlet Valve Rear Right Performance
s: The rear right outlet solenoid valve does not respond hydraulically as expected.

t: Ride Height Sensor Rear Left
s: The air suspension control module has detected a fault in the front left ride height sensor circuit. Without this signal the module cannot regulate suspension height and self-levelling is disabled.

t: Ride Height Sensor Rear Right
s: The air suspension control module has detected a fault in the front left ride height sensor circuit. Without this signal the module cannot regulate suspension height and self-levelling is disabled.

t: Compressor Relay Circuit
s: The air suspension control module has detected a circuit fault on the compressor relay output — typically open circuit, short to ground or short to power. Self-levelling is disabled and the vehicle may sit lower than normal.

t: Encoder Motor
s: The transfer case control module has detected a fault in the encoder motor used to shift between 2WD and 4WD ranges. A fault prevents range changes and may leave the transfer case in an undefined position.

t: 4WD Drive Coupling
s: The 4WD or AWD control module has detected a fault on the electronic drive coupling that distributes torque between axles. Reduced torque transfer or a complete loss of all-wheel drive function may result.

t: Front Axle Disconnect
s: The 4WD control module has detected a fault on the front axle disconnect actuator. On part-time AWD vehicles this actuator decouples the front axle for fuel saving, and a fault prevents engagement or disengagement of front-wheel drive.

t: Transfer Case Position Sensor
s: The transfer case control module has detected an implausible signal from the position sensor that reports the current shift position (2HI, 4HI, 4LO, neutral). Without a valid position the system cannot complete a range change.

t: Transfer Case High Range Switch Circuit Malfunction
s: The transfer case control module has detected an implausible state from the high range (4H) position switch. Range selection in 4WD mode may be limited.

t: Transfer Case Low Range Switch Circuit Malfunction
s: The transfer case control module has detected an implausible state from the low range (4L) position switch. Engagement of low range may be inhibited.

t: 4WD Mode Switch Circuit Malfunction
s: The transfer case or 4WD control module has detected an invalid signal from the driver-operated 4WD mode selector. Mode requests from the dashboard may be ignored.

t: Traction Control Solenoid Valve Circuit
s: The dedicated traction control isolation valve in the hydraulic block reports an electrical fault.

t: Traction Control Switch Circuit
s: The TCS on/off switch reports an implausible state.

t: Traction Control Lamp Circuit
s: The TCS indicator lamp control circuit is shorted or open.

t: Traction Control Active Too Long
s: TCS regulation has been continuously active longer than allowed, suggesting persistent low-friction conditions or a sensor fault.

t: Adaptive Cruise Control Sensor Circuit Malfunction
s: The adaptive cruise control module has detected a fault in its forward-looking radar or lidar sensor circuit. ACC and forward collision warning are unavailable while the fault is active.

t: Adaptive Cruise Control Module Internal Performance
s: The adaptive cruise control module has reported an internal performance fault — for example self-test failure, supply voltage out of range or invalid configuration. ACC is disabled.

t: Steering Wheel Position Sensor Circuit
s: The steering angle sensor reports an electrical fault. ESC and lane keeping assistance functions are typically disabled.

t: Steering Wheel Position Sensor Calibration
s: The steering angle sensor has not been calibrated after a service operation, or the calibration value is implausible.

t: Rear Wheel Steering Position Sensor Circuit
s: On vehicles with active rear wheel steering, the rear position sensor reports an electrical fault. The system disables rear steering.

t: Steering Wheel Position Sensor Circuit
s: The chassis or stability control module has detected a fault in the steering wheel position sensor circuit. ESP and lane assist systems require an accurate steering angle and may be deactivated.

t: Steering Position Signal Implausible
s: Steering angle signal is implausible compared with yaw rate, lateral acceleration and wheel speeds.

t: Yaw Rate Sensor Circuit
s: The yaw rate sensor reports an electrical fault. ESC is disabled while the fault is active.

t: Yaw Rate Sensor Range/Performance
s: The yaw rate sensor signal is implausible relative to steering angle and lateral acceleration.

t: Lateral Acceleration Sensor Circuit
s: The lateral acceleration sensor reports an electrical fault.

t: Lateral Acceleration Sensor Range/Performance
s: The lateral acceleration sensor signal is outside the plausibility range.

t: Anti-lock Brake System Disabled
s: A general status code indicating that ABS function has been disabled by the brake control module. It is set in addition to the underlying root-cause code that disabled ABS.

t: ESC Active Performance
s: ESC interventions occur but do not produce the expected vehicle response. May indicate hydraulic or mechanical limitation.

t: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor A Circuit Low
s: Left-front wheel speed sensor A signal voltage below expected range. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor A Circuit High
s: Left-front wheel speed sensor A signal voltage above expected range. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor A Intermittent/Erratic
s: Intermittent or erratic signal from left-front wheel speed sensor A. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: ESC Switch Circuit
s: The ESC on/off switch reports an implausible state.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor A Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on right-front wheel speed sensor A. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor A Range/Performance
s: Right-front wheel speed sensor A reading implausible. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor A Circuit Low
s: Right-front WSS A signal voltage below expected range. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor A Circuit High
s: Right-front WSS A signal voltage above expected range. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor A Intermittent/Erratic
s: Intermittent signal from right-front WSS A. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor A Correlation
s: Right-front WSS A reading does not correlate with the other wheel speed sensors during straight-line driving. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor A Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on left-rear WSS A. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor A Range/Performance
s: Left-rear WSS A reading implausible. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor A Circuit Low
s: Left-rear WSS A signal voltage below expected range. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor A Circuit High
s: Left-rear WSS A signal voltage above expected range. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Hill Hold Function Disabled
s: The brake or stability control module has disabled the hill hold (auto-hold) function due to a related fault. Hill start assist is unavailable until the underlying fault is repaired.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor A Correlation
s: Left-rear WSS A correlation fault with other wheel speed sensors. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor A Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on right-rear WSS A. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor A Range/Performance
s: Right-rear WSS A reading implausible. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor A Circuit Low
s: Right-rear WSS A signal voltage below expected range. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor A Circuit High
s: Right-rear WSS A signal voltage above expected range. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor A Intermittent/Erratic
s: Intermittent signal from right-rear WSS A. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor A Correlation
s: Right-rear WSS A correlation fault. Wheel-speed signals feed ABS, ESC, traction control, transmission and odometer — a single dropped signal disables all of these safety functions until repaired.

t: Left Front Wheel Speed Sensor - Mechanical
s: Mechanical fault at left-front wheel speed sensor — typically damaged tone ring, excessive air gap or loose sensor mount.

t: Right Front Wheel Speed Sensor - Mechanical
s: Mechanical fault at left-front wheel speed sensor — typically damaged tone ring, excessive air gap or loose sensor mount.

t: Left Rear Wheel Speed Sensor - Mechanical
s: Mechanical fault at left-front wheel speed sensor — typically damaged tone ring, excessive air gap or loose sensor mount.

t: Right Rear Wheel Speed Sensor - Mechanical
s: Mechanical fault at left-front wheel speed sensor — typically damaged tone ring, excessive air gap or loose sensor mount.

t: Multi-axis Acceleration Sensor Module A Range/Performance
s: Multi-axis acceleration sensor module A reading implausible.

t: Multi-axis Acceleration Sensor Module A Missing Calibration
s: Multi-axis acceleration module A is not calibrated — required after replacement or chassis adjustment.

t: Multi-axis Acceleration Sensor Module A Intermittent/Erratic
s: Intermittent signal from multi-axis accel module A.

t: Multi-axis Acceleration Sensor Module A Correlation
s: Acceleration data from module A does not correlate with wheel speed and steering inputs.

t: Brake Pedal Force Sensor Circuit
s: On systems with brake force sensing the dedicated sensor reports an implausible signal.

t: Multi-axis Acceleration Sensor Module B Range/Performance
s: Multi-axis acceleration sensor module B reading implausible.

t: Multi-axis Acceleration Sensor Module B Missing Calibration
s: Multi-axis acceleration module B is not calibrated — required after replacement or chassis adjustment.

t: Multi-axis Acceleration Sensor Module B Intermittent/Erratic
s: Intermittent signal from multi-axis accel module B.

t: Multi-axis Acceleration Sensor Module B Correlation
s: Acceleration data from module B does not correlate with wheel speed and steering inputs.

t: Multi-axis Acceleration Sensor Module B
s: Generic fault on multi-axis acceleration sensor module B.

t: Steering Angle Sensor Module
s: Generic fault on the steering angle sensor module.

t: Steering Angle Sensor Module Mechanical
s: Mechanical fault on the steering angle sensor — encoder disc damaged or sensor housing loose on the column.

t: Steering Angle Sensor Module Range/Performance
s: Steering angle sensor reading implausible compared to expected angle.

t: Steering Angle Sensor Module Missing Calibration
s: Steering angle sensor not calibrated — required after wheel alignment, suspension service or sensor replacement.

t: Steering Angle Sensor Module Correlation
s: Steering angle does not correlate with vehicle yaw and lateral acceleration during driving.

t: ABS Pump Motor Control Range/Performance
s: ABS pump motor does not respond to control signals as expected — motor may be sluggish, locked or drawing abnormal current.

t: ABS Pump Motor Control Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on ABS pump motor control line.

t: ABS Pump Motor Control Circuit High

t: Brake Master Cylinder Piston Position Sensor Calibration Not Learned
s: Master cylinder piston position sensor calibration not learned.

t: Brake Master Cylinder Cut Off Valve
s: Fault on master cylinder cut-off valve — used in brake-by-wire systems to isolate the master cylinder during normal electric boost.

t: Power Steering Control Module Processor Performance
s: Power steering control module processor reports an internal performance fault.

t: Brake Transmission Shift Interlock (BTSI) Control Circuit
s: Fault on the brake transmission shift interlock (BTSI) circuit — prevents shifting from Park unless brake pedal is depressed.

t: 4WD/AWD Clutch A Actuator Control Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on 4WD/AWD clutch A actuator control. The clutch engages drive to a secondary axle or transfer case.

t: 4WD/AWD Clutch A Actuator Control Circuit Performance
s: AWD clutch A actuator does not respond as expected.

t: 4WD/AWD Clutch A Actuator Control Circuit Low
s: AWD clutch A actuator control voltage below expected range. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: 4WD/AWD Clutch A Actuator Control Circuit High
s: AWD clutch A actuator control voltage above expected range. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: 4WD/AWD Clutch A Temperature Too High
s: AWD clutch A overheated — typically from extended slip during off-road driving or repeated traction events.

t: 4WD/AWD Clutch A Performance/Stuck Disengaged
s: AWD clutch A does not engage when commanded — stuck disengaged.

t: 4WD/AWD Clutch A Stuck Engaged
s: AWD clutch A stays engaged when commanded off — drivetrain not disconnected, may cause transmission wind-up on tight turns.

t: 4WD/AWD Clutch A Engagement Fault
s: AWD clutch A engagement fault — sluggish or partial engagement detected.

t: 4WD/AWD Clutch A Position Sensor Circuit
s: Generic fault on AWD clutch A position sensor.

t: Service/Park Brake Chamber Pressure Sensor
s: Generic fault on service/park brake chamber pressure sensor — used on air-brake systems with combined service and spring-applied parking brake chamber.

t: Service/Park Brake Chamber Pressure Too High
s: Pressure in the service/park brake chamber exceeded safe limit.

t: Service/Park Brake Chamber Pressure Too Low
s: Pressure in the service/park brake chamber dropped below required minimum — risk of insufficient braking force.

t: Electric Park Brake Actuator Left Circuit
s: The left electric park brake caliper actuator does not respond as commanded.

t: Electric Park Brake Actuator Right Circuit
s: The right electric park brake caliper actuator does not respond as commanded.

t: Electric Park Brake Module Internal Fault
s: The EPB control module fails its internal self-test and the park brake function may be limited.

t: Active Steering Module Communication
s: A communication fault between the chassis control module and the active steering module has been detected. Active steering varies the steering ratio with speed, and a fault disables this assistance and may store secondary codes in other modules.

t: Steering Position Signal Malfunction
s: The chassis or stability control module has received an invalid or missing steering position signal — often via the communication bus from the steering control module. ESP, lane assist and electric power steering may be limited.

t: Power Steering Position Sensor Range/Performance
s: The power steering control module has detected that the rack or column position signal is out of range or implausible. Steering assist may be reduced.

t: Power Steering Pressure Sensor Range/Performance
s: The power steering module has detected that the hydraulic pressure sensor signal is out of range — relevant for hydraulic and electro-hydraulic systems. Assist regulation may run in fallback mode.

t: Electric Power Steering Torque Sensor Circuit
s: The torque sensor in the EPS column or rack reports an electrical fault. Power assist is reduced or disabled.

t: Electric Power Steering Motor Circuit
s: The EPS assist motor circuit reports an electrical fault.

t: Trailer Connector Circuit Malfunction
s: The chassis or trailer brake module has detected a fault in the trailer wiring connector — for example open lighting circuit or short to ground. Trailer brake and lighting functions may be unavailable.

t: Electric Power Steering Motor Position Sensor Circuit
s: The EPS motor position sensor (resolver/Hall) reports an implausible or missing signal.

t: Electric Power Steering Module Internal Fault
s: The EPS control module fails its internal self-test. Power assist is disabled.

t: Electric Power Steering Voltage Supply
s: The supply voltage to the EPS module is out of range, reducing or disabling power assist.

t: Electric Power Steering Overtemperature Protection
s: The EPS module has reduced or disabled assistance to protect the motor from overheating, typically after extended low-speed manoeuvring.

t: Cruise Control Brake Switch
s: The cruise control or chassis module has detected an implausible signal from the dedicated cruise control brake switch. This switch is independent from the stop lamp switch and disengages cruise control when the driver brakes.

t: Trailer Brake Force Sensor Circuit Malfunction
s: The integrated trailer brake controller has detected an electrical fault in the brake force sensor circuit. Proportional trailer brake control is disabled — the controller defaults to a manual or fixed-gain mode.

t: Drive System Voltage Low
s: The chassis control module has detected that the supply voltage is below the operating threshold required for ABS, ESP or steering systems. Many chassis functions are limited or disabled until the voltage returns to a normal range.

t: Steering Angle Sensor Calibration
s: The chassis or stability control module reports that the steering angle sensor has not been calibrated or that the calibration data has been lost. ESP requires a calibrated zero position and is disabled until calibration is performed.

t: Air Suspension Compressor Circuit
s: The air suspension compressor motor circuit reports an electrical fault. Levelling is disabled.

t: Air Suspension Compressor Overtemperature
s: The air suspension compressor has been disabled to protect against overheating, typically after a slow leak.

t: Air Suspension Height Sensor Front Left
s: The front left ride height sensor reports an implausible or missing signal.

t: Air Suspension Height Sensor Front Right
s: The front right ride height sensor reports an implausible or missing signal.

t: Air Suspension Height Sensor Rear Left
s: The rear left ride height sensor reports an implausible or missing signal.

t: Air Suspension Height Sensor Rear Right
s: The rear right ride height sensor reports an implausible or missing signal.

t: Steering Effort Sensor Range/Performance
s: The electric power steering module has detected that the torque (effort) sensor signal is out of the expected range. Steering assist is reduced or disabled.

t: Steering Effort Sensor Stuck
s: The power steering module has detected that the torque sensor signal does not change despite steering input — the sensor appears stuck. Assist is disabled to avoid uncontrolled steering torque.

t: Steering Wheel Angle Sensor Center Position Not Learned
s: The chassis control module has not stored a valid center (zero) position for the steering wheel angle sensor. ESP, lane assist and electric power steering may be limited until calibration is performed.

t: Air Suspension Solenoid Valve Circuit
s: One of the level control solenoid valves reports an electrical fault. The corresponding axle cannot be levelled.

t: Active Damping Control Front Left
s: The front left adjustable damper reports an electrical fault.

t: Active Damping Control Front Right
s: The front right adjustable damper reports an electrical fault.

t: Active Damping Control Rear Left
s: The rear left adjustable damper reports an electrical fault.

t: Active Damping Control Rear Right
s: The rear right adjustable damper reports an electrical fault.

t: Trailer Stability Assist Performance
s: The trailer stability assist function detects implausible trailer sway response or sensor input.

t: Adaptive Cruise Control Brake Request Performance
s: A brake request from adaptive cruise control or AEB cannot be executed within the expected response time.

t: TPMS Sensor 1 Battery
s: The tyre pressure monitoring system has detected that the battery in tyre pressure sensor 1 is low or depleted. TPMS sensors are battery-powered with a typical life of 5 to 10 years and must be replaced as a unit when the battery fails.

t: TPMS Sensor 1 Position Performance
s: The tire pressure monitoring receiver cannot reliably localize sensor 1 to a wheel position. Pressure values may be assigned to the wrong wheel until relearn is completed.

t: TPMS Sensor 2 Battery
s: The tyre pressure monitoring system has detected that the battery in tyre pressure sensor 2 is low or depleted. TPMS sensors are battery-powered with a typical life of 5 to 10 years and must be replaced as a unit when the battery fails.

t: TPMS Sensor 2 Position Performance
s: The tire pressure monitoring receiver cannot reliably localize sensor 2 to a wheel position. Pressure values may be assigned to the wrong wheel until relearn is completed.

t: TPMS Sensor 3 Battery Low
s: The TPMS receiver reports that the internal battery of sensor 3 is at end of life. The sensor must be replaced as the battery is sealed inside the housing.

t: TPMS Sensor 4 Battery Low
