t: EGR Cooler Bypass Control Stuck Closed Bank 1
s: The EGR cooler bypass valve on bank 1 is stuck in the closed position. EGR gas always passes through the cooler, which slows engine warm-up and can degrade cold-start emissions.

t: EGR Cooler Bypass Control Stuck Closed Bank 2
s: The EGR cooler bypass valve on bank 2 is stuck in the closed position. EGR gas always passes through the cooler, slowing engine warm-up and degrading cold-start emissions.

t: A/C Compressor Internal Control Module Performance
s: The internal control module of an electrically driven A/C compressor (typical on hybrid and BEV vehicles) reports an internal performance fault. Compressor capacity may be reduced or shut down.

t: Exhaust Flow Control Valve A Control Performance
s: The exhaust flow control valve A responds to commands but the resulting flow or back-pressure does not match expectations. Worn flap or carbon coking on the bore.

t: Auto Configuration Throttle Input Not Present Bank 2
s: During controller auto-configuration the expected throttle input on bank 2 was not detected. Configuration cannot complete and the second throttle path cannot be used.

t: Transmission Friction Element A Apply Time Range/Performance
s: The transmission control module measured an apply time for friction element A (clutch or band) outside the calibrated window. Indicates worn clutch material, low line pressure, or a degrading shift solenoid.

t: Transmission Friction Element B Apply Time Range/Performance
s: The time required to apply friction element B (a clutch or band inside the automatic transmission) is outside the expected window. Either too long (worn clutch, low pressure) or too short (sticky valve, mis-calibration).

t: Transmission Friction Element C Apply Time Range/Performance
s: The time required to apply friction element C is outside the expected window. Worn clutch, low pressure, or sticky valve.

t: Transmission Friction Element D Apply Time Range/Performance
s: The time required to apply friction element D is outside the expected window.

t: Transmission Friction Element E Apply Time Range/Performance
s: The time required to apply friction element E is outside the expected window.

t: Transmission Friction Element F Apply Time Range/Performance
s: The time required to apply friction element F is outside the expected window.

t: Shift Solenoid F
s: A general fault in shift solenoid F (the sixth gear-selection solenoid in 6-speed and higher transmissions). Detected through electrical resistance, hydraulic response, or downstream gear engagement check.

t: Shift Solenoid F Performance/Stuck Off
s: Shift solenoid F is commanded on but does not produce the expected hydraulic response. Spool stuck closed or hydraulic apply circuit blocked.

t: Shift Solenoid F Stuck On
s: Shift solenoid F stays in the activated state when commanded off. Affected gear stays applied so the transmission cannot shift correctly.

t: Shift Solenoid F Electrical
s: Electrical fault in shift solenoid F — open or shorted coil, or short to ground/battery in the harness.

t: Transmission Friction Element A Temperature Too High
s: Friction element A inside the automatic transmission has reached over-temperature. Excessive slip during shifts or trailer towing burns the clutch.

t: Transmission Friction Element B Temperature Too High
s: Friction element B inside the automatic transmission has reached over-temperature.

t: Transmission Friction Element C Temperature Too High
s: Friction element C inside the automatic transmission has reached over-temperature.

t: Transmission Friction Element D Temperature Too High
s: Friction element D inside the automatic transmission has reached over-temperature.

t: Transmission Friction Element E Temperature Too High
s: Friction element E inside the automatic transmission has reached over-temperature.

t: Transmission Friction Element F Temperature Too High
s: Friction element F inside the automatic transmission has reached over-temperature.

t: Shift Solenoid F Intermittent
s: Shift solenoid F shows intermittent electrical or hydraulic faults that come and go. Loose connector or marginal coil.

t: Unexpected Mechanical Gear Disengagement
s: The transmission disengaged mechanically without a corresponding command. The vehicle can free-wheel, which is a safety concern. Often a hydraulic or hardware fault inside the gearbox.

t: Hydraulic Power Unit Leakage
s: The transmission hydraulic power unit (pump + accumulator) has detected a pressure drop that suggests internal leakage. Pressure cannot be held during shifts.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid D
s: A general fault in pressure control solenoid D. Detected via electrical resistance, response to commanded duty cycle, or downstream pressure measurement.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid D Performance/Stuck Off
s: Pressure control solenoid D is commanded on but does not produce the expected pressure. Spool stuck closed or hydraulic feed restricted.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid D Stuck On
s: Pressure control solenoid D stays in the activated state when commanded off. Affected hydraulic circuit stays pressurised.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid D Electrical
s: The TCM detects an electrical fault on transmission pressure control solenoid D. Hydraulic pressure cannot be modulated as expected, causing harsh or slipping shifts.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid D Intermittent
s: Pressure control solenoid D shows intermittent electrical or hydraulic faults. Loose connector or marginal coil.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid D Control Circuit / Open
s: The control line to pressure control solenoid D is open. The solenoid cannot be driven.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid D Control Circuit Range/Performance
s: The current through pressure control solenoid D does not match the commanded value within tolerance. Coil resistance drift or high-resistance contact.

t: Park Pawl Position Sensor A Circuit Low
s: The position sensor that confirms the park pawl A engagement state reads below the lower diagnostic threshold. Without confirmed park engagement the shift-by-wire controller cannot release the brake hold.

t: Park Pawl Position Sensor A Circuit High
s: The position sensor for park pawl A reads above the upper diagnostic threshold. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Park Pawl Position Sensor B Circuit Low
s: The redundant park pawl position sensor B reads below the lower diagnostic threshold. Redundancy for the park-engagement check is lost.

t: Park Pawl Position Sensor B Circuit High
s: The redundant park pawl position sensor B reads above the upper diagnostic threshold. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Performance
s: A general transmission performance fault. The transmission controller has detected behaviour outside acceptable bounds (slip, shift quality, ratio error) without a more specific cause.

t: Electronic Gear Shift - Position Not Learned
s: The shift-by-wire controller did not complete the position learning routine for the gear selector. Without learned end stops the controller cannot map selector positions to gear requests.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid D Control Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the pressure control solenoid D control line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid D Control Circuit High
s: Voltage on the pressure control solenoid D control line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid E
s: A general fault in pressure control solenoid E.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid E Stuck Off
s: The TCM detects that pressure control solenoid E is stuck in the de-energised position. The corresponding clutch or band cannot be applied, leading to gear slip or loss of certain gears.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid E Stuck On
s: Pressure control solenoid E stays in the activated state when commanded off.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid E Electrical
s: Electrical fault in pressure control solenoid E — open or shorted coil, or short to ground/battery in the harness.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid E Intermittent
s: Pressure control solenoid E shows intermittent electrical or hydraulic faults.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid E Control Circuit / Open
s: The control line to pressure control solenoid E is open. The solenoid cannot be driven.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid E Control Circuit Range/Performance
s: The current through pressure control solenoid E does not match the commanded value within tolerance.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid E Control Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the pressure control solenoid E control line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Range Select Motor Control Circuit
s: A general fault in the transmission range select motor control circuit. The motor that physically moves the gear range selector (P-R-N-D) does not respond correctly.

t: Transmission Range Select Motor Control Circuit Current Too High
s: The current through the range select motor exceeds the limit. Mechanism is binding or shorted.

t: Park Pawl Motor Control Circuit
s: A general fault in the park pawl motor control circuit. The motor that engages or releases the park pawl does not respond correctly.

t: Park Pawl Motor Control Circuit Current Too High
s: The current through the park pawl motor exceeds the limit. Park pawl mechanism binding or motor shorted.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid E Control Circuit High
s: Voltage on the pressure control solenoid E control line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid F
s: A general fault in pressure control solenoid F.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid F Performance/Stuck Off
s: Pressure control solenoid F is commanded on but does not produce the expected pressure.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid F Stuck On
s: Pressure control solenoid F stays in the activated state when commanded off.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid F Electrical
s: Electrical fault in pressure control solenoid F.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid F Intermittent
s: Pressure control solenoid F shows intermittent electrical or hydraulic faults.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid F Control Circuit / Open
s: The control line to pressure control solenoid F is open.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid F Control Circuit Range/Performance
s: The current through pressure control solenoid F does not match the commanded value within tolerance.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid F Control Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the pressure control solenoid F control line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid F Control Circuit High
s: Voltage on the pressure control solenoid F control line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Friction Element G Apply Time Range/Performance
s: The time required to apply friction element G is outside the expected window.

t: Transmission Friction Element H Apply Time Range/Performance
s: The time required to apply friction element H is outside the expected window.

t: Gear Shift Control Module A Supply Voltage Low
s: The shift-by-wire gear shift control module A reports a supply voltage below the operating range.

t: Gear Shift Control Module A Supply Voltage High
s: The gear shift control module A reports a supply voltage above the operating range.

t: Gear Shift Control Module A Over Temperature
s: The gear shift control module A has reached over-temperature. Excessive switching activity, sustained high motor load, or insufficient cooling.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor B Over Temperature Condition
s: The second transmission fluid temperature sensor reports an over-temperature condition. Transmission fluid is too hot in the area monitored by sensor B.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor B Circuit
s: The second transmission fluid temperature sensor returns no usable signal.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor B Circuit Range/Performance
s: The second transmission fluid temperature sensor reading is implausible against engine load and ambient temperature. Drift or stuck signal.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor B Circuit Low
s: The second transmission fluid temperature sensor signal is below the lower diagnostic threshold. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor B Circuit High
s: The second transmission fluid temperature sensor signal is above the upper diagnostic threshold. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor B Circuit Intermittent
s: The second transmission fluid temperature sensor signal jumps or drops out intermittently. Loose connector or chafed wire.

t: Intermediate Shaft Speed Sensor B Circuit
s: The intermediate shaft speed sensor B (used for closed-loop ratio control on planetary or DCT gearboxes) returns no usable signal. The controller cannot verify ratio change.

t: Intermediate Shaft Speed Sensor B Circuit Range/Performance
s: The intermediate shaft speed sensor B reading is implausible against expected speed from gear ratio and input shaft speed.

t: Intermediate Shaft Speed Sensor B Circuit No Signal
s: The intermediate shaft speed sensor B produces no pulses while the shaft is known to be turning.

t: Intermediate Shaft Speed Sensor B Circuit Intermittent
s: The intermediate shaft speed sensor B signal jumps or drops out intermittently. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Intermediate Shaft Speed Sensor C Circuit
s: The third intermediate shaft speed sensor returns no usable signal. Used on transmissions with multiple intermediate planetary stages.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor C Circuit
s: The third transmission fluid temperature sensor returns no usable signal.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor C Circuit Range/Performance
s: The third transmission fluid temperature sensor reading is implausible. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor C Circuit Low
s: The third transmission fluid temperature sensor signal is below the lower diagnostic threshold. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor C Circuit High
s: The third transmission fluid temperature sensor signal is above the upper diagnostic threshold. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor C Circuit Intermittent
s: The third transmission fluid temperature sensor signal jumps or drops out intermittently. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor C Over Temperature Condition
s: The third transmission fluid temperature sensor reports an over-temperature condition.

t: Intermediate Shaft Speed Sensor C Circuit Range/Performance
s: The third intermediate shaft speed sensor reading is implausible against expected speed from gear ratio and input shaft speed.

t: Intermediate Shaft Speed Sensor C Circuit No Signal
s: The third intermediate shaft speed sensor produces no pulses while the shaft is known to be turning.

t: Intermediate Shaft Speed Sensor C Circuit Intermittent
s: The third intermediate shaft speed sensor signal jumps or drops out intermittently. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Fluid Cooler Control Circuit / Open
s: The controller cannot drive the transmission fluid cooler control valve or fan. Active cooling cannot be commanded so transmission fluid temperature can rise during heavy use.

t: Transmission Fluid Cooler Control Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the transmission fluid cooler control line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Fluid Cooler Control Circuit High
s: Voltage on the transmission fluid cooler control line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid
s: A general fault in the torque converter clutch (TCC) pressure control solenoid. Lockup feel and slip control are degraded.

t: Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit Performance/Stuck Off
s: The TCC pressure control solenoid is commanded on but does not produce the expected lockup pressure. TCC stays open or partially engaged.

t: Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit Stuck On
s: The TCC pressure control solenoid stays in the activated state when commanded off. TCC stays locked which can stall the engine on low-speed stops.

t: Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit Electrical
s: Electrical fault in the TCC pressure control solenoid — open or shorted coil, or short to ground/battery in the harness.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor D Circuit
s: The fourth transmission fluid temperature sensor returns no usable signal.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor D Circuit Range/Performance
s: The fourth transmission fluid temperature sensor reading is implausible. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor D Circuit Low
s: The fourth transmission fluid temperature sensor signal is below the lower diagnostic threshold. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor D Circuit High
s: The fourth transmission fluid temperature sensor signal is above the upper diagnostic threshold. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor D Circuit Intermittent
s: The fourth transmission fluid temperature sensor signal jumps or drops out intermittently. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor D Over Temperature Condition
s: The fourth transmission fluid temperature sensor reports an over-temperature condition.

t: Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit Intermittent
s: The TCC pressure control solenoid shows intermittent electrical or hydraulic faults. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit / Open
s: The control line to the TCC pressure control solenoid is open. The solenoid cannot be driven so TCC lockup is unavailable.

t: Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit Range/Performance
s: The current through the TCC pressure control solenoid does not match the commanded value within tolerance.

t: Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit High
s: Voltage on the TCC pressure control solenoid control line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Torque Converter Clutch Pressure Control Solenoid Control Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the TCC pressure control solenoid control line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Input/Turbine Shaft Speed Sensor B Circuit
s: The second input or turbine shaft speed sensor returns no usable signal. Used on transmissions with redundant input speed sensing for closed-loop ratio control.

t: Input/Turbine Shaft Speed Sensor B Circuit Range/Performance
s: The second input shaft speed sensor reading is implausible against the primary sensor. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Input/Turbine Shaft Speed Sensor B Circuit No Signal
s: The second input shaft speed sensor produces no pulses while the shaft is known to be turning.

t: Input/Turbine Shaft Speed Sensor B Circuit Intermittent
s: The second input shaft speed sensor signal jumps or drops out intermittently. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Torque Converter Clutch Solenoid Circuit Low
s: The TCM reads a voltage below threshold on the torque converter clutch (TCC) solenoid control circuit. The TCC may not engage, increasing fuel consumption and converter heat.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Motor Supply Voltage Circuit / Open
s: The motor supply line of the second electric or auxiliary transmission fluid pump is open. Pump cannot run, hydraulic pressure is lost during stop-start engine-off events.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Motor Supply Voltage Circuit Low
s: The motor supply voltage of the second auxiliary transmission fluid pump is below the operating range.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Motor Supply Voltage Circuit High
s: The motor supply voltage of the second auxiliary transmission fluid pump is above the operating range.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Motor Current
s: A general fault in the motor current measurement of the second auxiliary transmission fluid pump.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Motor Current Low
s: The measured current of the second auxiliary transmission fluid pump motor is below the lower threshold. Pump may be free-spinning or motor partially open.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Motor Current High
s: The measured current of the second auxiliary transmission fluid pump motor is above the upper threshold. Pump binding or motor shorted.

t: Torque Converter Clutch Solenoid Circuit High
s: The voltage on the TCC solenoid control circuit is above the calibrated maximum. Either the wire is shorted to battery voltage or the solenoid coil is open.

t: Four Wheel Drive (4WD) Low Switch Circuit
s: The 4WD-Low engagement switch circuit is faulty. The transmission and chassis controllers cannot tell whether the transfer case is in low range.

t: Four Wheel Drive (4WD) Low Switch Circuit Range/Performance
s: The 4WD-Low switch state is implausible against transfer case position feedback. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Four Wheel Drive (4WD) Low Switch Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the 4WD-Low switch line is below the expected window. Usually a short to ground or a stuck-closed switch.

t: Four Wheel Drive (4WD) Low Switch Circuit High
s: Voltage on the 4WD-Low switch line is above the expected window. Usually an open switch or a short to battery.

t: Upshift Switch Circuit Range/Performance
s: The upshift switch (paddle or steering-wheel button on Tiptronic-style controls) signal is implausible. Stuck or noisy contacts.

t: Upshift Switch Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the upshift switch line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Upshift Switch Circuit High
s: Voltage on the upshift switch line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Upshift Switch Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The upshift switch signal jumps or drops out intermittently. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Downshift Switch Circuit Range/Performance
s: The downshift switch signal is implausible. Stuck or noisy contacts. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Hydraulic Leakage
s: The second auxiliary transmission fluid pump cannot maintain pressure even at high duty cycle. Indicates internal hydraulic leakage downstream of the pump.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Driver Circuit Performance
s: The driver stage for the second auxiliary transmission fluid pump does not respond to commands within tolerance.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Motor Stalled
s: The second auxiliary transmission fluid pump motor is commanded on but the rotor does not turn.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Motor Over Temperature
s: The second auxiliary transmission fluid pump motor has reached over-temperature. Sustained high duty or insufficient cooling.

t: Clutch A Unexpected Mechanical Gear Disengagement
s: Clutch A inside the dual-clutch transmission disengaged mechanically without a corresponding command. The vehicle can free-wheel briefly during the affected gear.

t: Clutch B Unexpected Mechanical Gear Disengagement
s: Clutch B inside the dual-clutch transmission disengaged mechanically without a corresponding command.

t: Downshift Switch Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the downshift switch line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Downshift Switch Circuit High
s: Voltage on the downshift switch line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Downshift Switch Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The downshift switch signal jumps or drops out intermittently. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Torque Converter Temperature Too High
s: The TCM detects that torque converter temperature exceeds the calibrated upper limit. Continuous slipping or insufficient cooling causes overheating and risks transmission damage.

t: Input/Turbine Speed Sensor A/B Correlation
s: The two input or turbine shaft speed sensors A and B do not agree within tolerance. Either sensor may be drifting or missing pulses.

t: Clutch Actuator Temperature Too High
s: The clutch actuator (electromechanical or hydraulic) on an automated manual or DCT transmission has reached over-temperature. Excessive engagement activity (slow clutch driver, hill creeping) overloads the actuator.

t: Gear Shift Actuator Temperature Too High
s: The gear shift actuator on an automated manual or DCT transmission has reached over-temperature. Frequent shifting in stop-and-go traffic overloads the actuator.

t: Clutch A Temperature Too High
s: Clutch A in the dual-clutch transmission has reached over-temperature. Sustained slip during launch or hill hold burns the clutch.

t: Auto Shift Manual Adaptive Learning at Limit
s: The adaptive learning that compensates for clutch wear or hydraulic drift in an automated manual transmission has reached its limit. No more compensation is available.

t: Clutch A Adaptive Learning at Limit
s: The adaptive learning that compensates for clutch A engagement point drift in a DCT has reached its limit. Clutch wear is at end of life.

t: Kick Down Switch Circuit
s: The kick-down switch (under the accelerator pedal at full travel) circuit is faulty. The transmission cannot detect the driver's full-throttle downshift request.

t: Kick Down Switch Circuit Range/Performance
s: The kick-down switch state is implausible against accelerator pedal position. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Kick Down Switch Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the kick-down switch line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Kick Down Switch Circuit High
s: Voltage on the kick-down switch line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Kick Down Switch Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The kick-down switch signal jumps or drops out intermittently. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Clutch B Adaptive Learning at Limit
s: The adaptive learning that compensates for clutch B engagement point drift in a DCT has reached its limit.

t: Gate Select Direction Circuit
s: The gate-select direction circuit on an automated manual or sequential transmission cannot detect which direction the selector is being moved.

t: Gate Select Direction Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the gate-select direction line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Gate Select Direction Circuit High
s: Voltage on the gate-select direction line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Gear Shift Direction Circuit
s: The gear-shift direction circuit cannot detect the commanded shift direction (up or down) on an automated manual or sequential transmission.

t: Gear Shift Direction Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the gear-shift direction line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Gear Shift Direction Circuit High
s: Voltage on the gear-shift direction line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump A Control Circuit / Open
s: The controller cannot drive the first auxiliary transmission fluid pump. Pressure is lost during stop-start engine-off events.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump A Performance/Stuck Off
s: The first auxiliary transmission fluid pump is commanded on but does not run.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump A Control Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the first auxiliary transmission fluid pump control line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump A Control Circuit High
s: Voltage on the first auxiliary transmission fluid pump control line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Transfer Case Gear High Incorrect Ratio
s: When the transfer case is in High range the measured input/output speed ratio does not match the expected value. Worn chain or planetary set.

t: Transfer Case Gear Low Incorrect Ratio
s: When the transfer case is in Low range the measured input/output speed ratio does not match the expected value.

t: Transfer Case Gear Neutral Incorrect Ratio
s: When the transfer case is in Neutral the measured output speed is not zero or shows unexpected drag.

t: Four Wheel Drive (4WD) Range Signal Circuit
s: The 4WD range signal circuit (reporting whether 2H, 4H, 4L is engaged) returns no usable signal.

t: Four Wheel Drive (4WD) Range Signal Circuit Range/Performance
s: The 4WD range signal does not match expected value for the commanded transfer case position. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Four Wheel Drive (4WD) Range Signal Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the 4WD range signal line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Four Wheel Drive (4WD) Range Signal Circuit High
s: Voltage on the 4WD range signal line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump A Stuck On
s: The first auxiliary transmission fluid pump runs even when no command is active.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Control Circuit / Open
s: The controller cannot drive the second auxiliary transmission fluid pump.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Performance/Stuck Off
s: The second auxiliary transmission fluid pump is commanded on but does not run.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Control Circuit High
s: Voltage on the second auxiliary transmission fluid pump control line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Stuck On
s: The second auxiliary transmission fluid pump runs even when no command is active.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump A Pressure Too Low
s: The auxiliary transmission fluid pump A produces less pressure than expected for the commanded duty. Pump aging, leakage, or fluid loss.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Pressure Too Low
s: The auxiliary transmission fluid pump B produces less pressure than expected for the commanded duty. Pump aging, leakage, or fluid loss.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump Control Valve Circuit/Open
s: The control circuit for the auxiliary transmission fluid pump control valve reports a circuit/open condition. Pump pressure regulation cannot be commanded as expected.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump Control Valve Circuit Range/Performance
s: The control circuit for the auxiliary transmission fluid pump control valve reports a circuit range/performance condition. Pump pressure regulation cannot be commanded as expected.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump Control Valve Circuit Low
s: The control circuit for the auxiliary transmission fluid pump control valve reports a circuit low condition. Pump pressure regulation cannot be commanded as expected.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump Control Valve Circuit High
s: The control circuit for the auxiliary transmission fluid pump control valve reports a circuit high condition. Pump pressure regulation cannot be commanded as expected.

t: Transmission Range Control B Circuit/Open
s: The B-channel actuator of the transmission range control reports a circuit/open condition. Gear-selector mechanical engagement cannot be requested as expected.

t: Transmission Range Control B Circuit Performance
s: The B-channel actuator of the transmission range control reports a circuit performance condition. Gear-selector mechanical engagement cannot be requested as expected.

t: Transmission Range Control B Circuit Low
s: The B-channel actuator of the transmission range control reports a circuit low condition. Gear-selector mechanical engagement cannot be requested as expected.

t: Transmission Range Control B Circuit High
s: The B-channel actuator of the transmission range control reports a circuit high condition. Gear-selector mechanical engagement cannot be requested as expected.

t: Transmission Range Control A Position Sensor/Switch Circuit/Open
s: The position sensor or switch of transmission range control channel A reports a circuit/open condition. The TCM cannot verify the actuator position reliably.

t: Transmission Range Control A Position Sensor/Switch Circuit Range/Performance
s: The position sensor or switch of transmission range control channel A reports a circuit range/performance condition. The TCM cannot verify the actuator position reliably.

t: Transmission Range Control A Position Sensor/Switch Circuit Low
s: The position sensor or switch of transmission range control channel A reports a circuit low condition. The TCM cannot verify the actuator position reliably.

t: Transmission Range Control A Position Sensor/Switch Circuit High
s: The position sensor or switch of transmission range control channel A reports a circuit high condition. The TCM cannot verify the actuator position reliably.

t: Transmission Range Control B Position Sensor/Switch Circuit/Open
s: The position sensor or switch of transmission range control channel B reports a circuit/open condition. The TCM cannot verify the actuator position reliably.

s: The position sensor or switch of transmission range control channel B reports a circuit range/performance condition. The TCM cannot verify the actuator position reliably.

t: Transmission Range Control B Position Sensor/Switch Circuit Low
s: The position sensor or switch of transmission range control channel B reports a circuit low condition. The TCM cannot verify the actuator position reliably.

t: Transmission Range Control B Position Sensor/Switch Circuit High
s: The position sensor or switch of transmission range control channel B reports a circuit high condition. The TCM cannot verify the actuator position reliably.

t: Clutch B Temperature Too High
s: The transmission control unit measures clutch B temperature above the safe operating threshold. Repeated slip events or excessive load have heated the clutch pack.

t: Gear Shift Lock Solenoid/Actuator A Performance/Stuck On
s: The shift lock solenoid for selector lever A stays engaged when release is commanded. Driver cannot move the selector out of Park or away from the locked gate.

t: Gear Shift Lock Solenoid/Actuator A Stuck Off
s: The shift lock solenoid for selector lever A does not engage when commanded. The selector can be moved without the safety interlock active.

t: Transmission Range Sensor B Circuit (PRNDL Input)
s: The TCM detects an invalid PRNDL signal pattern from transmission range sensor B. The selected gear position cannot be determined reliably from the B-channel.

t: Transmission Range Sensor B Circuit Range/Performance
s: Range sensor B reports a position that does not match other plausibility checks. Mechanical alignment off or sensor contacts intermittent.

t: Transmission Range Sensor B Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the range sensor B signal line is below the expected window. Wire shorted to ground or sensor internal short.

t: Transmission Range Sensor B Circuit High
s: Voltage on the range sensor B signal line is above the expected window. Wire shorted to B+ or sensor open.

t: Transmission Range Sensor B Circuit Intermittent
s: The range sensor B signal drops out intermittently. Loose connector or vibration-sensitive solder joint.

t: Transmission Range Sensor A/B Correlation
s: The two range sensor channels A and B do not agree on the same gear selector position. Plausibility check fails after debounce.

t: Transmission Range Sensor Alignment
s: The TCM detects that the transmission range sensor mechanical alignment is outside the calibration window. Selector positions read with offset across the entire travel.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid H

t: Pressure Control Solenoid H Performance/Stuck Off

s: Pressure control solenoid H stays in the activated state when commanded off. Affected hydraulic circuit stays pressurised.

t: Pressure Control Solenoid H Electrical
s: The TCM detects an electrical fault on transmission pressure control solenoid H. Hydraulic pressure cannot be modulated as expected, causing harsh or slipping shifts.

t: Shift Fork A Position Sensor Incorrect Neutral Position Indicated

t: Shift Fork B Position Sensor Incorrect Neutral Position Indicated
s: The position sensor for shift fork B indicates the neutral position when the fork is mechanically engaged in a gear, or vice versa. Sensor reference offset.

s: The position sensor for shift fork C indicates the neutral position when the fork is mechanically engaged in a gear, or vice versa. Sensor reference offset.

s: The position sensor for shift fork D indicates the neutral position when the fork is mechanically engaged in a gear, or vice versa. Sensor reference offset.

t: Shift Fork A Stuck
s: Shift fork A cannot leave its current engagement position when the actuator commands movement. Mechanical jam or actuator without travel.

t: Shift Fork B Stuck
s: Shift fork B cannot leave its current engagement position when the actuator commands movement. Mechanical jam or actuator without travel.

t: Shift Fork C Stuck
s: Shift fork C cannot leave its current engagement position when the actuator commands movement. Mechanical jam or actuator without travel.

t: Shift Fork B Unrequested Movement
s: Shift fork B moves without an actuator command. Hydraulic leakage at the actuator or detent spring failure.

t: Shift Fork C Unrequested Movement
s: Shift fork C moves without an actuator command. Hydraulic leakage at the actuator or detent spring failure.

t: Shift Fork Position Sensor A/B Correlation
s: The position signals of shift fork sensors A and B do not correlate as expected for the active gear pre-selection. Plausibility check fails.

t: Shift Fork Position Sensor C/D Correlation
s: The position signals of shift fork sensors C and D do not correlate as expected for the active gear pre-selection. Plausibility check fails.

t: Clutch A Pressure Discharge Performance
s: Clutch A hydraulic pressure does not bleed off as expected when commanded to release. Excessive residual pressure delays disengagement.

t: Clutch B Pressure Discharge Performance
s: Clutch B hydraulic pressure does not bleed off as expected when commanded to release. Excessive residual pressure delays disengagement.

t: Clutch A Pressure Charge Performance
s: Clutch A hydraulic pressure does not build up as expected when commanded to engage. Engagement is delayed or weakened.

t: Clutch B Pressure Charge Performance
s: Clutch B hydraulic pressure does not build up as expected when commanded to engage. Engagement is delayed or weakened.

t: Clutch A Pressure Engagement Performance/Too Low
s: Clutch A engagement pressure is below the calibrated minimum. Slip during launch and torque transfer reduced.

t: Clutch B Pressure Engagement Performance/Too Low
s: Clutch B engagement pressure is below the calibrated minimum. Slip during launch and torque transfer reduced.

t: Clutch A Pressure Disengagement Performance/Too Low
s: Clutch A disengagement pressure is below the calibrated minimum. Clutch may not separate cleanly under load.

t: Clutch B Pressure Disengagement Performance/Too Low
s: Clutch B disengagement pressure is below the calibrated minimum. Clutch may not separate cleanly under load.

t: Shift Fork A Actuator Circuit/Open
s: The control circuit for shift fork A actuator reports a actuator circuit/open condition. Fork engagement cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Shift Fork A Actuator Performance
s: The control circuit for shift fork A actuator reports a actuator performance condition. Fork engagement cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Shift Fork A Actuator Circuit Low
s: The control circuit for shift fork A actuator reports a actuator circuit low condition. Fork engagement cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Shift Fork A Actuator Circuit High
s: The control circuit for shift fork A actuator reports a actuator circuit high condition. Fork engagement cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Shift Fork B Actuator Circuit/Open
s: The control circuit for shift fork B actuator reports a actuator circuit/open condition. Fork engagement cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Shift Fork B Actuator Performance
s: The control circuit for shift fork B actuator reports a actuator performance condition. Fork engagement cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Shift Fork B Actuator Circuit Low
s: The control circuit for shift fork B actuator reports a actuator circuit low condition. Fork engagement cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Shift Fork B Actuator Circuit High

t: Shift Fork E Position Sensor Incorrect Neutral Position Indicated

t: Shift Fork E Unrequested Movement
s: Shift fork E moves without an actuator command. Hydraulic leakage at the actuator or detent spring failure.

t: Clutch A Pressure Engagement Too High
s: Clutch A engagement pressure exceeds the calibrated maximum. Risk of harsh engagement and accelerated wear.

t: Clutch A Pressure Disengagement Too High
s: Clutch A disengagement pressure exceeds the calibrated maximum. The clutch may not separate cleanly.

t: Clutch A Engagement Time Performance/Too Slow
s: Clutch A takes longer than calibrated to reach the engaged state. Hydraulic flow restricted or pressure rise too slow.

t: Clutch A Engagement Time Too Fast
s: Clutch A reaches the engaged state faster than calibrated. Risk of harsh engagement and shock loads.

t: Clutch A Disengagement Time Performance/Too Slow
s: Clutch A takes longer than calibrated to release. Pressure does not bleed off quickly enough.

t: Clutch A Disengagement Time Too Fast
s: Clutch A releases faster than calibrated. Risk of torque interruption and driveline shock.

t: Clutch A Performance/Stuck Disengaged
s: Clutch A cannot leave the disengaged state when engagement is commanded. No torque transfer through this clutch.

t: Clutch A Stuck Engaged
s: Clutch A cannot leave the engaged state when release is commanded. Torque transfer continues unwanted.

t: Clutch B Pressure Engagement Too High
s: Clutch B engagement pressure exceeds the calibrated maximum. Risk of harsh engagement and accelerated wear.

t: Clutch B Pressure Disengagement Too High
s: Clutch B disengagement pressure exceeds the calibrated maximum. The clutch may not separate cleanly.

t: Clutch B Engagement Time Performance/Too Slow
s: Clutch B takes longer than calibrated to reach the engaged state. Hydraulic flow restricted or pressure rise too slow.

t: Clutch B Engagement Time Too Fast
s: Clutch B reaches the engaged state faster than calibrated. Risk of harsh engagement and shock loads.

t: Clutch B Disengagement Time Performance/Too Slow
s: Clutch B takes longer than calibrated to release. Pressure does not bleed off quickly enough.

t: Clutch B Disengagement Time Too Fast
s: Clutch B releases faster than calibrated. Risk of torque interruption and driveline shock.

t: Clutch B Performance/Stuck Disengaged
s: Clutch B cannot leave the disengaged state when engagement is commanded. No torque transfer through this clutch.

t: Clutch B Stuck Engaged
s: Clutch B cannot leave the engaged state when release is commanded. Torque transfer continues unwanted.

t: Shift Fork A Calibration Not Learned
s: The TCM has not learned the end-stop calibration for shift fork A. Engagement positions cannot be commanded with full precision until adaptation completes.

t: Transmission Adaptive Values Not Learned
s: The TCM has not learned the adaptive shift values needed for refined gear changes. The transmission operates with default values until adaptation completes.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump Motor Torque Out Of Range
s: The motor torque required by the auxiliary transmission fluid pump is outside the calibrated window for the commanded duty. Pump load too high or too low for expected operation.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Actuator Circuit/Open
s: The control circuit for the engine disconnect clutch actuator reports a circuit/open condition. Hybrid driveline coupling cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Actuator Circuit Range/Performance
s: The control circuit for the engine disconnect clutch actuator reports a circuit range/performance condition. Hybrid driveline coupling cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Actuator Circuit Low
s: The control circuit for the engine disconnect clutch actuator reports a circuit low condition. Hybrid driveline coupling cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Actuator Circuit High
s: The control circuit for the engine disconnect clutch actuator reports a circuit high condition. Hybrid driveline coupling cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Excessive Slippage
s: The hybrid control module detects more slip across the engine disconnect clutch than the calibration allows for the requested torque transfer. Friction surfaces may be glazed or contaminated.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Temperature Too High
s: The hybrid control module measures engine disconnect clutch temperature above the safe operating threshold. Repeated slip events or insufficient cooling have heated the friction pack.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Stuck Open
s: The engine disconnect clutch cannot leave the open state when engagement is commanded. The combustion engine cannot be coupled to the hybrid drivetrain.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Engagement Fault
s: The hybrid control module detects an engagement fault on the engine disconnect clutch. The engagement profile or end position deviates from calibration.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Stuck Closed
s: The engine disconnect clutch cannot leave the closed state when release is commanded. Pure electric driving is not available because the combustion engine stays coupled.

t: Park Lock/Pawl Actuator Circuit
s: The TCM detects a generic fault on the park lock or pawl actuator circuit. Park engagement command path is impaired.

t: Park Lock/Pawl Actuator Circuit/Open
s: The control wiring of the park lock or pawl actuator is open. No current flows when the actuator is commanded.

t: Park Lock/Pawl Actuator Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the park lock or pawl actuator control line is below the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Park Lock/Pawl Actuator Circuit High
s: Voltage on the park lock or pawl actuator control line is above the expected window. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Park Lock/Pawl Actuator Circuit Performance
s: The park lock or pawl actuator does not engage or release within the calibrated time window. Mechanical resistance or weakened actuator.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Actuator Control Circuit Driver Current/Temperature Too High
s: The output driver for the engine disconnect clutch actuator reports current or junction temperature above its protection threshold. Sustained high load on the actuator or short-circuit fault.

t: Shift Fork B Calibration Not Learned
s: The TCM has not learned the end-stop calibration for shift fork B. Engagement positions cannot be commanded with full precision until adaptation completes.

t: Shift Fork "A" Actuator Control Circuit Driver Current/Temperature Too High
s: The output driver for shift fork A actuator reports current or junction temperature above its protection threshold. Stalled actuator or short-circuit fault.

t: Shift Fork "B" Actuator Control Circuit Driver Current/Temperature Too High
s: The output driver for shift fork B actuator reports current or junction temperature above its protection threshold. Stalled actuator or short-circuit fault.

t: Ion Sense Module Supply Voltage Circuit
s: The ECM detects a generic fault on the supply voltage circuit of the ion sense module. The module can no longer reliably bias the spark plug for ion current measurement.

t: Ion Sense Module Supply Voltage Low
s: Supply voltage on the ion sense module is below the expected window. Wiring shorted to ground, blown fuse, or upstream regulator fault.

t: Ion Sense Module Supply Voltage High
s: Supply voltage on the ion sense module is above the expected window. Charging system overvoltage or upstream regulator fault.

t: EVAP System High Load Purge Line B Performance
s: The ECM detects insufficient flow or pressure in the high-load purge line B of the evaporative emissions system. Purge cannot be commanded effectively under high engine load.

t: Clutch A Phase U-V-W Circuit Low
s: The output stage for the clutch A BLDC motor reports a low phase voltage on at least one of the U/V/W phases. Short to ground or interrupted half-bridge.

t: Clutch A Phase U-V-W Circuit High
s: The output stage for the clutch A BLDC motor reports a high phase voltage on at least one of the U/V/W phases. Short to B+ or interrupted load.

t: Clutch A Phase U-V-W Circuit/Open
s: The output stage for the clutch A BLDC motor cannot drive current through at least one of the U/V/W phases. Open wire or open winding.

t: Clutch A Phase U-V-W Performance
s: The phase currents of the clutch A BLDC motor do not match the commanded commutation pattern within tolerance. Sensor or driver synchronisation off.

t: Clutch A Pump Motor Performance
s: The clutch A hydraulic pump motor does not produce the expected flow for the commanded speed. Worn pump, leakage, or aged motor characteristic.

t: Clutch A Motor Overspeed
s: The clutch A actuator motor exceeds the calibrated maximum speed. Load on the motor unexpectedly low or rotor position offset.

t: Clutch A Motor Underspeed
s: The clutch A actuator motor falls below the calibrated minimum speed for the commanded duty. High mechanical load or supply voltage low.

t: Clutch A Motor Position Sensor Circuit/Open
s: The TCM cannot read a valid signal from the clutch A motor rotor position sensor. Open wiring or sensor failure.

t: Clutch A Motor Position Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The clutch A motor position signal does not correlate with the commanded commutation. Sensor offset or signal integrity degraded.

t: Clutch A Pressure Sensor Circuit/Open
s: The TCM cannot read a valid signal from the clutch A pressure sensor. Open wiring or sensor failure.

t: Clutch A Pressure Sensor Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the clutch A pressure sensor signal line is below the expected window. Wire shorted to ground or sensor short.

t: Clutch A Pressure Sensor Circuit High
s: Voltage on the clutch A pressure sensor signal line is above the expected window. Wire shorted to B+ or sensor open.

t: Clutch A Pressure Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The clutch A pressure signal does not match plausibility checks against actuator commands. Sensor drift or installation offset.

t: Clutch A Motor Driver Current/Temperature Too High
s: The output driver for the clutch A BLDC motor reports current or junction temperature above its protection threshold. Sustained high load or short-circuit fault.

t: Clutch B Phase U-V-W Circuit Low
s: The output stage for the clutch B BLDC motor reports a low phase voltage on at least one of the U/V/W phases. Short to ground or interrupted half-bridge.

t: Clutch B Phase U-V-W Circuit High
s: The output stage for the clutch B BLDC motor reports a high phase voltage on at least one of the U/V/W phases. Short to B+ or interrupted load.

t: Clutch B Phase U-V-W Circuit/Open
s: The output stage for the clutch B BLDC motor cannot drive current through at least one of the U/V/W phases. Open wire or open winding.

t: Clutch B Phase U-V-W Performance
s: The phase currents of the clutch B BLDC motor do not match the commanded commutation pattern within tolerance. Sensor or driver synchronisation off.

t: Clutch B Motor Driver Current/Temperature Too High
s: The output driver for the clutch B BLDC motor reports current or junction temperature above its protection threshold. Sustained high load or short-circuit fault.

t: Clutch B Pump Motor Performance
s: The clutch B hydraulic pump motor does not produce the expected flow for the commanded speed. Worn pump, leakage, or aged motor characteristic.

t: Clutch B Motor Overspeed
s: The clutch B actuator motor exceeds the calibrated maximum speed. Load on the motor unexpectedly low or rotor position offset.

t: Clutch B Motor Underspeed
s: The clutch B actuator motor falls below the calibrated minimum speed for the commanded duty. High mechanical load or supply voltage low.

t: Clutch B Motor Position Sensor Circuit/Open
s: The TCM cannot read a valid signal from the clutch B motor rotor position sensor. Open wiring or sensor failure.

t: Clutch B Motor Position Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The clutch B motor position signal does not correlate with the commanded commutation. Sensor offset or signal integrity degraded.

t: Clutch B Pressure Sensor Circuit/Open
s: The TCM cannot read a valid signal from the clutch B pressure sensor. Open wiring or sensor failure.

t: Clutch B Pressure Sensor Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the clutch B pressure sensor signal line is below the expected window. Wire shorted to ground or sensor short.

t: Clutch B Pressure Sensor Circuit High
s: Voltage on the clutch B pressure sensor signal line is above the expected window. Wire shorted to B+ or sensor open.

t: Clutch B Pressure Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The clutch B pressure signal does not match plausibility checks against actuator commands. Sensor drift or installation offset.

t: Shift Motor Phase U-V-W Circuit Low
s: The output stage for the shift motor reports a low phase voltage on at least one of the U/V/W phases. Short to ground or interrupted half-bridge.

t: Shift Motor Phase U-V-W Circuit High
s: The output stage for the shift motor reports a high phase voltage on at least one of the U/V/W phases. Short to B+ or interrupted load.

t: Shift Motor Phase U-V-W Circuit/Open
s: The output stage for the shift motor cannot drive current through at least one of the U/V/W phases. Open wire or open winding.

t: Shift Motor Phase U-V-W Performance
s: The phase currents of the shift motor do not match the commanded commutation pattern within tolerance. Sensor or driver synchronisation off.

t: Shift Motor Driver Current/Temperature Too High
s: The output driver for the shift motor reports current or junction temperature above its protection threshold. Sustained high load or short-circuit fault.

t: Shift Motor Performance
s: The shift motor does not move the gear actuator within the calibrated time window. Mechanical resistance, weakened motor, or supply voltage low.

t: Shift Motor Overspeed
s: The shift motor exceeds the calibrated maximum speed. Mechanical load lost or rotor position offset.

t: Shift Motor Position Sensor Circuit/Open
s: The TCM cannot read a valid signal from the shift motor rotor position sensor. Open wiring or sensor failure.

t: Shift Motor Position Sensor Circuit High
s: Voltage on the shift motor position sensor signal line is above the expected window. Wire shorted to B+ or sensor open.

t: Shift Motor Position Sensor Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the shift motor position sensor signal line is below the expected window. Wire shorted to ground or sensor short.

t: Shift Motor Position Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The shift motor position signal does not correlate with the commanded commutation. Sensor offset or signal integrity degraded.

t: Shift Motor Position Not Learned
s: The TCM has not learned the end-stop calibration for the shift motor. Gear positions cannot be commanded with full precision until adaptation completes.

t: Shift Motor Position Sensor Exceeded Learning Limit
s: The required adaptation offset for the shift motor position sensor exceeds the calibrated learning window. Sensor or mechanism out of nominal range.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Overspeed
s: The auxiliary transmission fluid pump B exceeds the calibrated maximum speed for the commanded duty. Mechanical load on the pump unexpectedly low.

t: Electric/Auxiliary Transmission Fluid Pump B Underspeed
s: The auxiliary transmission fluid pump B falls below the calibrated minimum speed for the commanded duty. Mechanical resistance high or supply voltage low.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Actuator Performance/Stuck Off
s: The engine disconnect clutch actuator is commanded to engage but does not produce the expected motion. Actuator stuck in disengaged position.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Actuator Stuck On
s: The engine disconnect clutch actuator stays in the engaged state when release is commanded. Pure electric driving is not available.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Pressure Sensor Circuit
s: The hybrid control module detects a generic fault on the engine disconnect clutch pressure sensor circuit. Engagement pressure cannot be monitored reliably.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Pressure Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The engine disconnect clutch pressure signal does not match plausibility checks against actuator commands. Sensor drift or installation offset.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Pressure Sensor Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the engine disconnect clutch pressure sensor signal line is below the expected window. Wire shorted to ground or sensor short.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Pressure Sensor Circuit High
s: Voltage on the engine disconnect clutch pressure sensor signal line is above the expected window. Wire shorted to B+ or sensor open.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Pressure Sensor Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The engine disconnect clutch pressure sensor signal drops out or jumps intermittently. Loose connector or noisy ground reference.

t: Engine Disconnect Clutch Actuator Circuit Intermittent
s: The control circuit of the engine disconnect clutch actuator drops out intermittently. Loose connector or vibration-sensitive wiring.

t: Exhaust Pressure Too High Bank 1
s: The exhaust pressure measured on bank 1 exceeds the calibrated maximum. Common causes are clogged DPF, restricted exhaust path, or stuck exhaust brake/back-pressure valve.

t: Exhaust Pressure Too High Bank 2
s: The exhaust pressure measured on bank 2 exceeds the calibrated maximum. Common causes are clogged DPF, restricted exhaust path, or stuck exhaust brake/back-pressure valve.

t: Exhaust Pressure Too Low Bank 1
s: The exhaust pressure measured on bank 1 falls below the calibrated minimum. Common causes are leaks upstream of the sensor, missing DPF substrate, or sensor calibration drift.

t: Exhaust Pressure Too Low Bank 2
s: The exhaust pressure measured on bank 2 falls below the calibrated minimum. Common causes are leaks upstream of the sensor, missing DPF substrate, or sensor calibration drift.

t: O2 Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1 Sensor 1
s: Long-term plausibility checks show that the front oxygen sensor on bank 1 reacts too slowly or stays too close to a fixed value. The signal does not show normal cross-counts during closed-loop operation.

t: O2 Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1 Sensor 2
s: The rear oxygen sensor on bank 1 does not show the expected slow steady-state behaviour after the catalyst. Either the sensor is degraded or the catalyst itself behaves abnormally.

t: O2 Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1 Sensor 3
s: The optional third oxygen sensor on bank 1 (typically downstream of a second catalyst) returns implausible values during long-term monitoring.

t: O2 Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 2 Sensor 1
s: The front oxygen sensor on bank 2 reacts too slowly or shows insufficient signal swing during closed-loop fuel control.

t: O2 Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 2 Sensor 2
s: The rear oxygen sensor on bank 2 shows abnormal behaviour during long-term plausibility evaluation. Often a sign of an aged sensor or weakened catalyst.

t: O2 Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 2 Sensor 3
s: The third oxygen sensor on bank 2 (after a second catalyst stage on some vehicles) returns implausible values. Often related to sensor aging or mechanical exhaust leak upstream.

t: O2 Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1 Sensor 4
s: The fourth oxygen sensor on bank 1, where present, returns implausible values during long-term plausibility evaluation. Used on multi-stage aftertreatment systems.

t: O2 Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 2 Sensor 4
s: The fourth oxygen sensor on bank 2 returns values outside the expected long-term performance window during plausibility checks.

t: O2 Sensor Negative Voltage Bank 1 Sensor 3
s: The ECM detects a negative voltage on the signal line of oxygen sensor 3 on bank 1. This is unusual for narrow-band sensors and often indicates a short to a negative reference, ground offset, or a wide-band sensor wired incorrectly.

t: O2 Sensor Negative Voltage Bank 2 Sensor 1
s: The ECM detects a negative voltage on the signal line of oxygen sensor 1 on bank 2. This is unusual for narrow-band sensors and often indicates a short to a negative reference, ground offset, or a wide-band sensor wired incorrectly.

t: Manifold Absolute Pressure Sensor B Circuit
s: The ECM detects a generic fault on the secondary manifold absolute pressure sensor (MAP B). Air load estimation cannot be cross-checked.

t: Manifold Absolute Pressure Sensor B Circuit Range/Performance
s: The MAP B signal does not correlate with TPS, MAF, or RPM as expected. Vacuum leak, restricted port, or sensor drift.

t: Manifold Absolute Pressure Sensor B Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the MAP B signal line is below the expected window. Wire shorted to ground or sensor short.

t: Manifold Absolute Pressure Sensor B Circuit High
s: Voltage on the MAP B signal line is above the expected window. Wire shorted to B+ or sensor open. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Manifold Absolute Pressure Sensor B Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The MAP B signal drops out or jumps intermittently. Loose connector or noisy ground reference. Wiring faults of this kind typically come from chafing, water ingress at connector seals, or pin push-back — a wiggle test on the harness while the code is active often confirms the location.

t: Fuel Supply Heater Performance
s: The fuel supply heater does not raise fuel temperature within the calibrated time window. Common in diesel and biofuel installations to prevent paraffin or fat crystallisation in cold weather.

t: O2 Sensor Negative Voltage Bank 2 Sensor 2
s: The ECM detects a negative voltage on the signal line of oxygen sensor 2 on bank 2. This is unusual for narrow-band sensors and often indicates a short to a negative reference, ground offset, or a wide-band sensor wired incorrectly.

t: O2 Sensor Negative Voltage Bank 2 Sensor 3
s: The ECM detects a negative voltage on the signal line of oxygen sensor 3 on bank 2. This is unusual for narrow-band sensors and often indicates a short to a negative reference, ground offset, or a wide-band sensor wired incorrectly.

t: AFCM Power Relay Control Circuit/Open
s: The control circuit of the alternative fuel control module (AFCM) power relay is open. The relay coil cannot be energised on command.

t: AFCM Power Relay Control Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the AFCM power relay control line is below the expected window. Wire shorted to ground or coil short.

t: AFCM Power Relay Control Circuit High
s: Voltage on the AFCM power relay control line is above the expected window. Wire shorted to B+ or coil open.

t: AFCM Power Relay Sense Circuit
s: The AFCM detects a generic fault on the sense feedback line of its power relay. Relay state cannot be confirmed reliably.

t: AFCM Power Relay Sense Circuit Low
s: The sense feedback voltage from the AFCM power relay is below the expected window. Indicates the relay output stays low when the relay is commanded on.

t: AFCM Power Relay Sense Circuit High
s: The sense feedback voltage from the AFCM power relay is above the expected window. Indicates relay output is energised when the relay should be off, or wire shorted to B+.

t: O2 Sensor Positive Current Control Circuit/Open Bank 1 Sensor 3
s: The control circuit for the wide-band oxygen sensor positive current pump on bank 1 sensor 3 is open. Pumping current cannot be applied for lambda regulation.

t: O2 Sensor Positive Current Control Circuit Low Bank 1 Sensor 3
s: Voltage on the wide-band oxygen sensor positive pump current line of bank 1 sensor 3 is below the expected window. Wire shorted to ground or pump cell short.

t: O2 Sensor Positive Current Control Circuit High Bank 1 Sensor 3
s: Voltage on the wide-band oxygen sensor positive pump current line of bank 1 sensor 3 is above the expected window. Wire shorted to B+ or pump cell open.

t: O2 Sensor Negative Current Control Circuit/Open Bank 1 Sensor 3
s: The control circuit for the wide-band oxygen sensor negative current pump on bank 1 sensor 3 is open. Pumping current cannot be applied for lambda regulation.

t: O2 Sensor Negative Current Control Circuit Low Bank 1 Sensor 3
s: Voltage on the wide-band oxygen sensor negative pump current line of bank 1 sensor 3 is below the expected window. Wire shorted to ground or pump cell short.

t: O2 Sensor Negative Current Control Circuit High Bank 1 Sensor 3
s: Voltage on the wide-band oxygen sensor negative pump current line of bank 1 sensor 3 is above the expected window. Wire shorted to B+ or pump cell open.

t: Alternative Fuel Delivery Error
s: The AFCM detects that alternative fuel delivery to the engine deviates from the commanded amount. Pump output, valve actuation, or injector flow does not match expectations.

t: Excessive Time to Enter Alternative Fuel Control
s: On bi-fuel vehicles, the AFCM cannot transition into alternative fuel control mode within the calibrated time window. Tank pressure low, vaporizer cold, or fuel quality limits the change-over.

t: Alternative Fuel System - Injector Leakage to Rail Detected
s: The AFCM detects pressure decay in the alternative fuel rail consistent with injector leakage. Even with all injectors commanded closed, fuel flows back into the rail.

t: Alternative Fuel Tank B Pressure Sensor Circuit
s: The AFCM detects a generic fault on the pressure sensor of alternative fuel tank B. Tank pressure cannot be monitored reliably.

t: Alternative Fuel Tank B Pressure Sensor Range/Performance
s: The pressure signal of alternative fuel tank B does not match plausibility checks against tank temperature and fill level. Sensor drift.

t: Alternative Fuel Tank B Pressure Sensor Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the pressure sensor signal line of alternative fuel tank B is below the expected window. Wire shorted to ground or sensor short.

t: Alternative Fuel Tank B Pressure Sensor Circuit High
s: Voltage on the pressure sensor signal line of alternative fuel tank B is above the expected window. Wire shorted to B+ or sensor open.

t: Alternative Fuel Tank B Pressure Sensor Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The pressure sensor signal of alternative fuel tank B drops out or jumps intermittently. Loose connector or noisy ground.

t: Alternative Fuel Tank C Pressure Sensor Circuit
s: The AFCM detects a generic fault on the pressure sensor of alternative fuel tank C. Tank pressure cannot be monitored reliably.

t: Alternative Fuel Tank C Pressure Sensor Range/Performance
s: The pressure signal of alternative fuel tank C does not match plausibility checks against tank temperature and fill level. Sensor drift.

t: Alternative Fuel Tank C Pressure Sensor Circuit Low
s: Voltage on the pressure sensor signal line of alternative fuel tank C is below the expected window. Wire shorted to ground or sensor short.

t: Alternative Fuel Temperature Sensor Circuit
s: The AFCM detects a generic fault on the alternative fuel temperature sensor. Fuel viscosity and density compensation cannot be applied reliably.

t: Alternative Fuel Temperature Sensor Range/Performance

t: Alternative Fuel Temperature Sensor Circuit Low

s: Voltage on the alternative fuel temperature sensor signal line is above the expected window. Wire shorted to B+ or sensor open.

t: Alternative Fuel Vaporizer A Outlet Temperature Sensor Circuit
s: The AFCM detects a generic fault on the outlet temperature sensor of alternative fuel vaporizer A. Vaporization control cannot be tuned reliably.

s: The outlet temperature signal of alternative fuel vaporizer A does not match plausibility checks against fuel rail temperature and engine coolant temperature. Sensor drift.

s: The AFCM detects a generic fault on the outlet temperature sensor of alternative fuel vaporizer C. Vaporization control cannot be tuned reliably.

t: Alternative Fuel Vaporizer C Outlet Temperature Sensor Range/Performance
s: The outlet temperature signal of alternative fuel vaporizer C does not match plausibility checks against fuel rail temperature and engine coolant temperature. Sensor drift.

t: Cylinder 7 Injection Pulse Offset Exceeded Learning Limit
s: The injection pulse offset adaptation for cylinder 7 exceeds the calibrated learning window. Injector aging or fuel pressure drift beyond compensation.

t: Cylinder 8 Injection Pulse Offset Exceeded Learning Limit
s: The injection pulse offset adaptation for cylinder 8 exceeds the calibrated learning window. Injector aging or fuel pressure drift beyond compensation.

t: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 5 Circuit High

s: The control circuit of coolant heater B reports a control circuit performance condition. Auxiliary preheating of the coolant cannot be commanded as expected.

t: EVAP System Pressure Sensor/Switch C Circuit
s: The EVAP pressure sensor/switch C reports a circuit condition. Tank pressure plausibility for leak detection on this branch is impaired.

t: EVAP System Pressure Sensor/Switch C Circuit Range/Performance
s: The EVAP pressure sensor/switch C reports a circuit range/performance condition. Tank pressure plausibility for leak detection on this branch is impaired.

t: EVAP System Pressure Sensor/Switch C Circuit Low
s: The EVAP pressure sensor/switch C reports a circuit low condition. Tank pressure plausibility for leak detection on this branch is impaired.

s: The EVAP pressure sensor/switch C reports a circuit high condition. Tank pressure plausibility for leak detection on this branch is impaired.
