s: The EVAP pressure sensor/switch C reports a circuit intermittent condition. Tank pressure plausibility for leak detection on this branch is impaired.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost Control Module A Calibration Not Learned/Programmed
s: The boost control module A reports that its calibration data is not present or not learned. Boost regulation falls back to default values until programming or adaptation completes.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost Control Module B Calibration Not Learned/Programmed
s: The boost control module B reports that its calibration data is not present or not learned. Boost regulation falls back to default values until programming or adaptation completes.

t: Wastegate Position Sensor A Circuit Performance
s: The wastegate position sensor A signal does not correlate with the commanded actuator position within tolerance. Sensor offset, mechanical wear, or signal integrity degraded.

t: Wastegate Position Sensor B Circuit Performance
s: The wastegate position sensor B signal does not correlate with the commanded actuator position within tolerance. Sensor offset, mechanical wear, or signal integrity degraded.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump A Overspeed/Air In System
s: The charge air cooler coolant pump A runs above the calibrated maximum speed without producing the expected flow. Indicates air pocket in the coolant loop.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump A Underspeed
s: The charge air cooler coolant pump A runs below the calibrated minimum speed for the commanded duty. Mechanical resistance high or supply voltage low.

t: Engine Coolant Pump Underspeed
s: The engine coolant pump runs below the calibrated minimum speed for the commanded duty. Mechanical resistance high or supply voltage low.

t: Coolant Pump A Overspeed/Air in System
s: The coolant pump A runs above the calibrated maximum speed without producing the expected flow. Indicates air pocket in the coolant loop.

t: Coolant Pump A Underspeed
s: The coolant pump A runs below the calibrated minimum speed for the commanded duty. Mechanical resistance high or supply voltage low.

t: Intake Air O2 Sensor Circuit Bank 1
s: The ECM detects a generic fault on the intake air oxygen sensor signal circuit on bank 1. A drifted or sluggish lambda sensor lets the mixture run lean or rich without correction — fuel economy and emissions suffer immediately.

t: Intake Air O2 Sensor Circuit Low Voltage Bank 1
s: Voltage on the intake air oxygen sensor signal line on bank 1 is below the expected window. A drifted or sluggish lambda sensor lets the mixture run lean or rich without correction — fuel economy and emissions suffer immediately.

t: Intake Air O2 Sensor Circuit High Voltage Bank 1
s: Voltage on the intake air oxygen sensor signal line on bank 1 is above the expected window. A drifted or sluggish lambda sensor lets the mixture run lean or rich without correction — fuel economy and emissions suffer immediately.

t: Intake Air O2 Sensor Heater Control Circuit/Open Bank 1
s: The control circuit for the intake air O2 sensor heater on bank 1 is open. Sensor cannot reach operating temperature.

t: Intake Air O2 Sensor Heater Control Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1
s: The heater current of the intake air O2 sensor on bank 1 does not match the commanded value within tolerance.

t: Intake Air O2 Sensor Heater Control Circuit Low Bank 1
s: Voltage on the intake air O2 sensor heater control line on bank 1 is below the expected window. Wire shorted to ground or heater element shorted.

t: Intake Air O2 Sensor Heater Control Circuit High Bank 1
s: Voltage on the intake air O2 sensor heater control line on bank 1 is above the expected window. Wire shorted to B+ or heater element open.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger B Temperature Sensor Circuit
s: The ECM detects a circuit fault on the turbocharger/supercharger b temperature sensor. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger B Temperature Sensor Circuit Low
s: The ECM detects a circuit low fault on the turbocharger/supercharger b temperature sensor. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger B Temperature Sensor Circuit High
s: The ECM detects a circuit high fault on the turbocharger/supercharger b temperature sensor. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger B Temperature Sensor Range/Performance
s: The ECM detects a range/performance fault on the turbocharger/supercharger b temperature sensor. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Wastegate Position Sensor A Exceeded Learning Limit
s: The required adaptation offset for the wastegate position sensor A exceeds the calibrated learning window. Sensor or mechanism out of nominal range.

t: Wastegate Position Sensor B Exceeded Learning Limit
s: The required adaptation offset for the wastegate position sensor B exceeds the calibrated learning window. Sensor or mechanism out of nominal range.

t: Cold Start Injection Pulse Performance
s: During cold start, the ECM detects that the overall injection pulse performance does not match the calibrated cold-start strategy. Combustion stability is reduced during warm-up.

t: Injection Pulse Performance
s: The ECM detects that the overall injection pulse performance does not match the calibrated values. Combustion result deviates from expected fuelling behaviour.

t: Exhaust Pressure Control Valve A Position Exceeded Learning Limit
s: The required adaptation offset for the exhaust pressure control valve A position exceeds the calibrated learning window. Mechanical wear or sensor drift beyond compensation.

t: Exhaust Pressure Control Valve A Supply Low
s: The supply voltage to the exhaust pressure control valve A actuator is below the expected window. Fuse blown, supply wire shorted, or upstream regulator fault.

t: Exhaust Pressure Control Valve A Driver Current/Temperature Too High
s: The output driver for the exhaust pressure control valve A reports current or junction temperature above its protection threshold. Stalled actuator or short-circuit fault.

t: Reductant Quality Sensor Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The reductant (DEF/AdBlue) quality sensor signal drops out or jumps intermittently. Loose connector, dilution noise, or ground reference offset.

t: Vehicle Conditions Incorrect for Particulate Matter Sensor Regeneration
s: The ECM detects that the vehicle is not operating in conditions suitable for the periodic PM sensor regeneration cycle. The sensor element cannot be cleaned and soot-mass measurement is paused.

t: Coolant Pump A Stuck/Stalled
s: Coolant pump A reports a stuck/stalled condition. Coolant circulation through the affected loop cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Coolant Pump A Supply Voltage Circuit
s: Coolant pump A reports a supply voltage circuit condition. Coolant circulation through the affected loop cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Coolant Pump A Control Module Overtemperature
s: Coolant pump A reports a control module overtemperature condition. Coolant circulation through the affected loop cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Coolant Pump B Underspeed
s: Coolant pump B reports a underspeed condition. Coolant circulation through the affected loop cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Coolant Pump C Underspeed
s: Coolant pump C reports a underspeed condition. Coolant circulation through the affected loop cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Coolant Pump D Underspeed
s: Coolant pump D reports a underspeed condition. Coolant circulation through the affected loop cannot be commanded reliably.

t: Engine Coolant Pump Stuck/Stalled
s: The engine coolant pump is mechanically stuck or has stalled. Coolant circulation through the engine is severely reduced or absent.

t: Starter/Generator Mechanical Performance
s: The hybrid control module detects that the integrated starter/generator does not deliver the expected mechanical torque or speed for the commanded operation. Belt slippage, bearing wear, or coupling fault.

t: Catalyst Feedgas Performance
s: The ECM detects that the exhaust gas composition reaching the catalyst (feedgas) deviates from the calibrated profile. Pre-cat O2 measurement and air-fuel control are inconsistent with engine operation.

t: Particulate Filter Feedgas Performance
s: The ECM detects that the exhaust gas composition reaching the particulate filter (feedgas) deviates from the calibrated profile. Soot input rate cannot be modelled correctly, affecting regen scheduling.

t: NOx Exceedance - SCR NOx Catalyst Performance
s: NOx emissions are above the legal threshold and the ECU isolates the cause to the SCR NOx catalyst. Conversion efficiency is degraded — aged substrate, sulphur poisoning, or thermal damage.

t: NOx Exceedence - Empty Reagent Tank
s: NOx emissions are above the legal threshold and the ECU isolates the cause to an empty reductant tank. SCR cannot dose AdBlue/DEF.

t: NOx Exceedence - Interruption of Reagent Dosing Activity
s: NOx emissions are above the legal threshold and the ECU isolates the cause to interruption of reductant dosing. The injection valve, pump, or supply line is impaired.

t: NOx Exceedence — Insufficient Reagent
s: The SCR system has detected that NOx emissions exceed the legal limit and the cause is traced to insufficient AdBlue/DEF dosing. Modern emission regulations require this fault to be reported separately so that operator-induced misuse (running an empty tank, diluting the fluid) is recorded.

t: NOx Sensor Range/Performance (Bank 1, Sensor 1)
s: The upstream NOx sensor on bank 1 returns values that are not plausible for the current operating point. NOx sensors are slow to warm up, sensitive to soot and water, and are a frequent failure on diesel SCR systems.

t: NOx Exceedence — Reagent System Performance
s: The SCR system has detected that NOx emissions exceed the legal limit and the cause is traced to a performance fault in the reagent dosing system — for example a slow dosing pump, a clogged injector, or a frozen line that is not yet caught by another DTC.

t: NOx Exceedence - Insufficient Reagent Quality
s: The diesel exhaust aftertreatment system detects elevated NOx levels combined with abnormal reagent (AdBlue/DEF) consumption, indicating that the reagent quality is insufficient or contaminated. Many regions trigger driver inducement (limited restarts).

t: NOx Exceedence - Insufficient Reagent Consumption
s: The aftertreatment system detects elevated NOx emissions while reagent (AdBlue/DEF) consumption is below the expected level. Causes include clogged dosing injector, incorrect dosing rate or tampering.

t: NOx Exceedence - EGR System
s: NOx emissions are above the legal threshold and the ECU isolates the cause to the EGR system. EGR flow is too low or absent under operating conditions where NOx reduction is required.

t: NOx Sensor Heater Sense Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1 Sensor 1
s: The heater sense feedback of NOx sensor 1 on bank 1 reports values that do not match expected operation. The sensor warm-up cycle does not behave as the diagnostic expects.

t: Alternative Fuel Consumption Too Low
s: The AFCM detects that alternative fuel consumption falls below the expected value for the active operating mode. Indicates injector clogging, leaks before injectors, or incorrect tank-fill correction.

t: Alternative Fuel Consumption Too High
s: The AFCM detects that alternative fuel consumption exceeds the expected value for the active operating mode. Indicates injector leakage, fuel pressure too high, or unmetered fuel loss.

t: Alternative Fuel Tank Empty
s: The AFCM detects that the alternative fuel tank is empty. Bi-fuel vehicles may switch to gasoline operation; mono-fuel vehicles cannot continue without refuel.

t: Fuel Pump Secondary Circuit Performance
s: The ECM detects that the secondary fuel pump control circuit (typically used for high-load enrichment) does not respond as expected. Pump output, relay, or driver fault.

t: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 4 Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The signal of engine coolant temperature sensor 4 drops out or jumps intermittently. Loose connector or noisy ground reference.

t: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 5 Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The signal of engine coolant temperature sensor 5 drops out or jumps intermittently. Loose connector or noisy ground reference.

t: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 6 Circuit
s: The ECM detects a circuit fault on the engine coolant temperature sensor 6. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 6 Range/Performance
s: The ECM detects a range/performance fault on the engine coolant temperature sensor 6. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 6 Circuit Low
s: The ECM detects a circuit low fault on the engine coolant temperature sensor 6. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 6 Circuit High
s: The ECM detects a circuit high fault on the engine coolant temperature sensor 6. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor 6 Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The ECM detects a circuit intermittent/erratic fault on the engine coolant temperature sensor 6. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Cold Start Fuel Pressure Regulator A Performance
s: During cold start, fuel pressure regulator A does not maintain the calibrated pressure profile. Pressure rise or hold deviates from the cold-start strategy.

s: During cold start, fuel pressure regulator A cannot maintain commanded pressure - actual rail pressure drops below the calibrated lower limit.

s: During cold start, fuel pressure regulator A overshoots target pressure - actual rail pressure rises above the calibrated upper limit.

t: O2 Sensor Pumping Current Trim Circuit/Open Bank 1 Sensor 3
s: The wide-band oxygen sensor on bank 1 sensor 3 reports a pumping current trim circuit/open condition. Lambda regulation accuracy is impaired.

t: O2 Sensor Pumping Current Trim Circuit Low Bank 1 Sensor 3
s: The wide-band oxygen sensor on bank 1 sensor 3 reports a pumping current trim circuit low condition. Lambda regulation accuracy is impaired.

t: O2 Sensor Pumping Current Trim Circuit High Bank 1 Sensor 3
s: The wide-band oxygen sensor on bank 1 sensor 3 reports a pumping current trim circuit high condition. Lambda regulation accuracy is impaired.

t: O2 Sensor Out of Range During Deceleration Bank 1 Sensor 3
s: The wide-band oxygen sensor on bank 1 sensor 3 reports a out of range during deceleration condition. Lambda regulation accuracy is impaired.

t: Hybrid/EV Electronics Coolant Pump A Underspeed
s: The hybrid electronics coolant pump A runs below the calibrated minimum speed for the commanded duty. Mechanical resistance high or supply voltage low.

t: Hybrid/EV Electronics Coolant Pump A Overspeed
s: The hybrid electronics coolant pump A exceeds the calibrated maximum speed. Mechanical load lost or rotor position offset.

t: Hybrid/EV Electronics Coolant Pump A Stuck/Stalled
s: The hybrid electronics coolant pump A is mechanically stuck or has stalled. Power electronics cooling is severely reduced or absent.

t: Drive Motor "A" Phase X Current
s: The hybrid control module detects a generic fault on phase X current measurement of drive motor A. The drive motor's position, current and temperature are watched continuously; a fault here downgrades the inverter to a safe operating point or triggers torque reduction.

t: Drive Motor "A" Phase X Current Low
s: Phase X current of drive motor A is below the expected window. Indicates open winding, short, or sensor offset.

t: Drive Motor "A" Phase X Current High
s: Phase X current of drive motor A exceeds the expected window. Indicates phase short, stalled rotor, or sensor offset.

t: Drive Motor "A" Phase Y Current
s: The hybrid control module detects a generic fault on phase Y current measurement of drive motor A. The drive motor's position, current and temperature are watched continuously; a fault here downgrades the inverter to a safe operating point or triggers torque reduction.

t: Drive Motor "A" Phase Y Current Low
s: Phase Y current of drive motor A is below the expected window. Indicates open winding, short, or sensor offset.

t: Drive Motor "A" Phase Y Current High
s: Phase Y current of drive motor A exceeds the expected window. Indicates phase short, stalled rotor, or sensor offset.

t: Drive Motor "A" Phase Z Current
s: The hybrid control module detects a generic fault on phase Z current measurement of drive motor A. The drive motor's position, current and temperature are watched continuously; a fault here downgrades the inverter to a safe operating point or triggers torque reduction.

t: Drive Motor "A" Phase Z Current Low
s: Phase Z current of drive motor A is below the expected window. Indicates open winding, short, or sensor offset.

t: Drive Motor "A" Phase Z Current High
s: Phase Z current of drive motor A exceeds the expected window. Indicates phase short, stalled rotor, or sensor offset.

t: Hybrid/EV Electronics Cooling System Performance
s: The hybrid control module detects that the power electronics cooling system does not maintain target temperatures under load. Coolant flow, radiator fouling, or pump output reduced.

t: Active Grille Air Shutter C Stuck On
s: Active grille air shutter C stays in the open position when commanded to close. Aerodynamic drag and warm-up time are affected.

t: Active Grille Air Shutter C Control Circuit Range/Performance
s: The control circuit of active grille air shutter C reports a range/performance condition. Actuator response or current draw outside calibration.

t: Active Grille Air Shutter Module C Over Temperature
s: The control module of active grille air shutter C reports junction temperature above its protection threshold. Thermal protection has triggered.

t: Active Grille Air Shutter D Stuck On
s: Active grille air shutter D stays in the open position when commanded to close. Aerodynamic drag and warm-up time are affected.

t: Active Grille Air Shutter D Control Circuit Range/Performance
s: The control circuit of active grille air shutter D reports a range/performance condition. Actuator response or current draw outside calibration.

t: Active Grille Air Shutter Module D Over Temperature
s: The control module of active grille air shutter D reports junction temperature above its protection threshold. Thermal protection has triggered.

t: NOx Adsorber - Sulfur Limit Exceeded
s: The ECM detects that the cumulative sulfur load on the NOx adsorber catalyst exceeds the calibrated threshold. Desulfation regeneration is required to restore catalyst function.

t: SCR NOx Catalyst Efficiency Below Threshold Bank 1 Catalyst 2
s: The ECM detects that the conversion efficiency of the second SCR catalyst on bank 1 is below the calibrated threshold. NOx slip past catalyst 2 is too high.

t: Turbocharger Turbine Inlet Valve Control Performance
s: The turbocharger turbine inlet valve does not respond to commands within the expected time window. Mechanical resistance or actuator weakened.

t: Turbocharger Turbine Inlet Valve Supply Circuit Low
s: The supply voltage to the turbocharger turbine inlet valve actuator is below the expected window. Boost control tracks commanded vs. actual manifold pressure; persistent deviation means wastegate, vanes, hose or actuator are not delivering the requested pressure.

t: Turbocharger Turbine Inlet Valve Motor Driver High Current
s: The output driver for the turbocharger turbine inlet valve motor reports current above the protection threshold. Stalled motor or short-circuit fault.

t: Turbocharger Turbine Inlet Valve Motor Driver Overtemperature
s: The output driver for the turbocharger turbine inlet valve motor reports junction temperature above the protection threshold.

t: Turbocharger Turbine Inlet Valve Position Sensor Exceeded Learning Limit
s: The required adaptation offset for the turbocharger turbine inlet valve position sensor exceeds the calibrated learning window.

t: AIR System Pump Overspeed Bank 1
s: The secondary air injection (AIR) pump on bank 1 exceeds the calibrated maximum speed. Indicates load lost or supply voltage too high.

t: AIR System Pump Overtemperature Bank 1
s: The AIR pump on bank 1 reports temperature above the protection threshold. Sustained operation, blocked outlet, or cooling path obstruction.

t: AIR System Pump Supply Voltage Circuit Bank 1
s: The AIR pump supply voltage circuit on bank 1 reports a fault. Pump may not start when commanded.

t: Cold Start Shift Time Too Short
s: During cold start, the transmission shift time is shorter than calibrated. Risk of harsh shifts before fluid reaches operating temperature.

t: Cold Start Shift Time Too Long
s: During cold start, the transmission shift time exceeds the calibrated maximum. Cold fluid viscosity, valve body restriction, or solenoid lazy at low temperature.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage External Isolation Fault
s: The hybrid control module detects an isolation fault between the high-voltage battery and the vehicle chassis. Indicates degraded HV insulation.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage Internal Isolation Warning
s: The hybrid control module detects an internal isolation warning inside the high-voltage battery pack. Pre-failure indicator for cell or busbar insulation.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage External Isolation Warning
s: The hybrid control module detects an early-warning external isolation degradation. Pre-failure indicator for HV cable insulation against chassis.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Voltage System Isolation Warning
s: The hybrid control module detects a system-wide isolation warning across multiple HV components. General insulation degradation.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery System Precharge Current Too High
s: During the HV battery precharge sequence, the inrush current exceeds the calibrated maximum. Indicates short on the DC-link side or insufficient precharge resistance.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Interface Control Module A Performance
s: The hybrid battery interface control module A reports a performance fault. Internal self-tests fail or response time outside calibration.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Interface Control Module B Performance
s: The hybrid battery interface control module B reports a performance fault. Internal self-tests fail or response time outside calibration.

t: DC/DC Converter Temperature Sensor D Circuit
s: The ECM detects a circuit fault on the dc/dc converter temperature sensor d circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: DC/DC Converter Temperature Sensor D Circuit Range/Performance
s: The ECM detects a range/performance fault on the dc/dc converter temperature sensor d circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: DC/DC Converter Temperature Sensor D Circuit Low
s: The ECM detects a circuit low fault on the dc/dc converter temperature sensor d circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: DC/DC Converter Temperature Sensor D Circuit High
s: The ECM detects a circuit high fault on the dc/dc converter temperature sensor d circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Crankcase Ventilation System - Hose A Disconnected
s: The ECM detects that crankcase ventilation hose A is disconnected based on pressure or vacuum signals. Unmetered air ingress and oil mist escape are possible.

t: Crankcase Ventilation System - Hose B Disconnected
s: The ECM detects that crankcase ventilation hose B is disconnected based on pressure or vacuum signals. Unmetered air ingress and oil mist escape are possible.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Contactor Control Supply Circuit/Open
s: The supply circuit for the hybrid battery contactor control reports a open condition. Contactor coils may not energise as expected.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Contactor Control Supply Circuit Range/Performance
s: The supply circuit for the hybrid battery contactor control reports a range/performance condition. Contactor coils may not energise as expected.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Contactor Control Supply Circuit/Low
s: The supply circuit for the hybrid battery contactor control reports a low condition. Contactor coils may not energise as expected.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Contactor Control Supply Circuit/High
s: The supply circuit for the hybrid battery contactor control reports a high condition. Contactor coils may not energise as expected.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Disable Circuit/Open
s: The hybrid battery disable circuit reports a circuit/open condition. Service disconnect signal cannot be read reliably.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Disable Circuit Low
s: The hybrid battery disable circuit reports a circuit low condition. Service disconnect signal cannot be read reliably.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Disable Circuit High
s: The hybrid battery disable circuit reports a circuit high condition. Service disconnect signal cannot be read reliably.

t: Throttle Actuator A Control Exceeded Learning Limit
s: The required adaptation offset for throttle actuator A exceeds the calibrated learning window. Mechanical wear, deposit build-up, or sensor drift beyond compensation.

t: Throttle Actuator B Control Exceeded Learning Limit
s: The required adaptation offset for throttle actuator B exceeds the calibrated learning window. Mechanical wear, deposit build-up, or sensor drift beyond compensation.

t: Cold Start Turbocharger/Supercharger Wastegate Actuator A Range/Performance
s: During cold start, the wastegate actuator A response does not match the cold-start calibration. Cold viscosity, ice formation, or actuator force degraded at low temperature.

t: Cold Start Turbocharger/Supercharger Wastegate Actuator B Range/Performance
s: During cold start, the wastegate actuator B response does not match the cold-start calibration. Cold viscosity, ice formation, or actuator force degraded at low temperature.

t: Fuel Pressure Regulator C Exceeded Control Limits - Pressure Too High
s: Fuel pressure regulator C cannot maintain commanded pressure - actual rail pressure rises above the calibrated upper limit.

t: Fuel Pressure Regulator C Exceeded Control Limits - Pressure Too Low
s: Fuel pressure regulator C cannot maintain commanded pressure - actual rail pressure drops below the calibrated lower limit.

t: Fuel Pressure Regulator D Exceeded Control Limits - Pressure Too High
s: Fuel pressure regulator D cannot maintain commanded pressure - actual rail pressure rises above the calibrated upper limit.

t: Fuel Pressure Regulator D Exceeded Control Limits - Pressure Too Low
s: Fuel pressure regulator D cannot maintain commanded pressure - actual rail pressure drops below the calibrated lower limit.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump Sense Circuit High
s: The sense feedback circuit for the charge air cooler coolant pump reports a high condition. Pump output verification is impaired.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump Sense Circuit Low
s: The sense feedback circuit for the charge air cooler coolant pump reports a low condition. Pump output verification is impaired.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump Sense Circuit Performance
s: The sense feedback circuit for the charge air cooler coolant pump reports a performance condition. Pump output verification is impaired.

t: Particulate Filter Pressure Sensor Signals Swapped A/B
s: The ECM detects that the particulate filter pressure sensor signals A and B are reversed - low-pressure side reads as high-pressure side and vice versa. Most likely a wiring swap during service.

t: DC/DC Converter Temperature Sensor E Circuit
s: The ECM detects a circuit fault on the dc/dc converter temperature sensor e circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: DC/DC Converter Temperature Sensor E Circuit Low
s: The ECM detects a circuit low fault on the dc/dc converter temperature sensor e circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: DC/DC Converter Temperature Sensor E Circuit High
s: The ECM detects a circuit high fault on the dc/dc converter temperature sensor e circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: DC/DC Converter Temperature Sensor E Circuit Range/Performance
s: The ECM detects a range/performance fault on the dc/dc converter temperature sensor e circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: DC/DC Converter Temperature Sensor F Circuit
s: The ECM detects a circuit fault on the dc/dc converter temperature sensor f circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: DC/DC Converter Temperature Sensor F Circuit Low
s: The ECM detects a circuit low fault on the dc/dc converter temperature sensor f circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: DC/DC Converter Temperature Sensor F Circuit High
s: The ECM detects a circuit high fault on the dc/dc converter temperature sensor f circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: DC/DC Converter Temperature Sensor F Circuit Range/Performance
s: The ECM detects a range/performance fault on the dc/dc converter temperature sensor f circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump B Control Circuit/Open
s: Charge air cooler coolant pump B reports a control circuit/open condition. CAC coolant loop circulation is impaired.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump B Control Circuit Low
s: Charge air cooler coolant pump B reports a control circuit low condition. CAC coolant loop circulation is impaired.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump B Control Circuit High
s: Charge air cooler coolant pump B reports a control circuit high condition. CAC coolant loop circulation is impaired.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump B Performance
s: Charge air cooler coolant pump B reports a performance condition. CAC coolant loop circulation is impaired.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump B Overspeed/Air In System
s: Charge air cooler coolant pump B reports a overspeed/air in system condition. CAC coolant loop circulation is impaired.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump B Underspeed
s: Charge air cooler coolant pump B reports a underspeed condition. CAC coolant loop circulation is impaired.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump B Stuck/Stalled
s: Charge air cooler coolant pump B reports a stuck/stalled condition. CAC coolant loop circulation is impaired.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump B Supply Voltage Circuit
s: Charge air cooler coolant pump B reports a supply voltage circuit condition. CAC coolant loop circulation is impaired.

t: Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump B Control Module Overtemperature
s: Charge air cooler coolant pump B reports a control module overtemperature condition. CAC coolant loop circulation is impaired.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost System Performance
s: The turbocharger/supercharger boost system on the B branch does not deliver the expected boost pressure. Multiple components possible.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost Pressure B Not Detected – Mechanical
s: The boost pressure on branch B is not detected during operation. Mechanical fault — turbo seized, charge pipe disconnected, or wastegate fully open.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost Pressure B Too High – Mechanical
s: The boost pressure on branch B exceeds the calibrated maximum. Mechanical fault — wastegate stuck closed or boost control valve jammed.

t: Fan 2 Underspeed
s: Cooling fan 2 runs below the calibrated minimum speed for the commanded duty. Mechanical resistance high or motor weakened.

t: Fan 2 Overspeed
s: Cooling fan 2 exceeds the calibrated maximum speed. Indicates load lost (broken fan blade) or supply voltage above nominal.

t: Fan 3 Underspeed
s: Cooling fan 3 runs below the calibrated minimum speed for the commanded duty. Mechanical resistance high or motor weakened.

t: Fan 3 Overspeed
s: Cooling fan 3 exceeds the calibrated maximum speed. Indicates load lost (broken fan blade) or supply voltage above nominal.

t: PCV Heater "B" Control Circuit /Open
s: The control circuit of PCV (positive crankcase ventilation) heater B reports a control circuit /open condition. PCV line heater prevents oil mist freezing in cold weather.

t: PCV Heater "B" Control Circuit High
s: The control circuit of PCV (positive crankcase ventilation) heater B reports a control circuit high condition. PCV line heater prevents oil mist freezing in cold weather.

t: PCV Heater "B" Control Circuit Low
s: The control circuit of PCV (positive crankcase ventilation) heater B reports a control circuit low condition. PCV line heater prevents oil mist freezing in cold weather.

t: PCV Heater "B" Performance
s: The control circuit of PCV (positive crankcase ventilation) heater B reports a performance condition. PCV line heater prevents oil mist freezing in cold weather.

t: Generator Voltage Sense Circuit B
s: The voltage sense circuit B for the generator reports a circuit b condition. Generator output regulation may be impaired.

t: Generator Voltage Sense Circuit B Range/Performance
s: The voltage sense circuit B for the generator reports a circuit b range/performance condition. Generator output regulation may be impaired.

t: Generator Voltage Sense Circuit B Low
s: The voltage sense circuit B for the generator reports a circuit b low condition. Generator output regulation may be impaired.

t: Generator Voltage Sense Circuit B High
s: The voltage sense circuit B for the generator reports a circuit b high condition. Generator output regulation may be impaired.

t: Generator Inverter Power Supply B Circuit/Open
s: The power supply circuit B for the generator inverter reports a circuit/open condition. Inverter operation may be impaired.

t: Generator Inverter Power Supply B Circuit Low
s: The power supply circuit B for the generator inverter reports a circuit low condition. Inverter operation may be impaired.

t: Generator Inverter Power Supply B Circuit High
s: The power supply circuit B for the generator inverter reports a circuit high condition. Inverter operation may be impaired.

t: Coolant Pump Speed Sensor Circuit
s: The ECM detects a circuit fault on the coolant pump speed sensor circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Coolant Pump Speed Sensor Range/Performance
s: The ECM detects a range/performance fault on the coolant pump speed sensor. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Coolant Pump Speed Sensor Circuit Low
s: The ECM detects a circuit low fault on the coolant pump speed sensor circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Coolant Pump Speed Sensor Circuit High
s: The ECM detects a circuit high fault on the coolant pump speed sensor circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack G Deterioration
s: The BMS detects accelerated deterioration in battery pack subsection G. Cell voltage delta, internal resistance, or capacity has dropped below threshold for the affected sub-pack.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack H Deterioration
s: The BMS detects accelerated deterioration in battery pack subsection H. Cell voltage delta, internal resistance, or capacity has dropped below threshold for the affected sub-pack.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack I Deterioration
s: The BMS detects accelerated deterioration in battery pack subsection I. Cell voltage delta, internal resistance, or capacity has dropped below threshold for the affected sub-pack.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack J Deterioration
s: The BMS detects accelerated deterioration in battery pack subsection J. Cell voltage delta, internal resistance, or capacity has dropped below threshold for the affected sub-pack.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack K Deterioration
s: The BMS detects accelerated deterioration in battery pack subsection K. Cell voltage delta, internal resistance, or capacity has dropped below threshold for the affected sub-pack.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack L Deterioration
s: The BMS detects accelerated deterioration in battery pack subsection L. Cell voltage delta, internal resistance, or capacity has dropped below threshold for the affected sub-pack.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack M Deterioration
s: The BMS detects accelerated deterioration in battery pack subsection M. Cell voltage delta, internal resistance, or capacity has dropped below threshold for the affected sub-pack.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack N Deterioration
s: The BMS detects accelerated deterioration in battery pack subsection N. Cell voltage delta, internal resistance, or capacity has dropped below threshold for the affected sub-pack.

t: A/C Refrigerant Pressure Sensor A/B Correlation
s: The signals of A/C refrigerant pressure sensors A and B do not correlate within the calibrated tolerance. One sensor drifts or installation offset between the two channels.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger Bypass Valve C Stuck
s: The turbocharger/supercharger bypass valve C is mechanically stuck. Boost recirculation on this branch cannot be commanded.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost Sensor D Circuit
s: The ECM detects a circuit fault on the turbocharger/supercharger boost sensor d circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost Sensor D Circuit Range/Performance
s: The ECM detects a range/performance fault on the turbocharger/supercharger boost sensor d circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost Sensor D Circuit Low
s: The ECM detects a circuit low fault on the turbocharger/supercharger boost sensor d circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost Sensor D Circuit High
s: The ECM detects a circuit high fault on the turbocharger/supercharger boost sensor d circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Alternative Fuel Tank A Vacuum Insulation Deteriorated
s: The AFCM detects that the vacuum insulation of alternative fuel tank A has lost effectiveness. Common in cryogenic LNG tanks where loss of vacuum increases boil-off rate.

t: Cold Start Cylinder 1 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Performance
s: During cold start, the cylinder 1 deactivation or intake valve control does not respond as expected. Cold oil viscosity, lifter slow to actuate, or solenoid response sluggish.

t: Cold Start Cylinder 2 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Performance
s: During cold start, the cylinder 2 deactivation or intake valve control does not respond as expected. Cold oil viscosity, lifter slow to actuate, or solenoid response sluggish.

t: Cold Start Cylinder 3 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Performance
s: During cold start, the cylinder 3 deactivation or intake valve control does not respond as expected. Cold oil viscosity, lifter slow to actuate, or solenoid response sluggish.

t: Cold Start Cylinder 4 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Performance
s: During cold start, the cylinder 4 deactivation or intake valve control does not respond as expected. Cold oil viscosity, lifter slow to actuate, or solenoid response sluggish.

t: Cold Start Cylinder 5 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Performance
s: During cold start, the cylinder 5 deactivation or intake valve control does not respond as expected. Cold oil viscosity, lifter slow to actuate, or solenoid response sluggish.

t: Cold Start Cylinder 6 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Performance
s: During cold start, the cylinder 6 deactivation or intake valve control does not respond as expected. Cold oil viscosity, lifter slow to actuate, or solenoid response sluggish.

t: Intake Air Heater C Circuit/Open
s: The control circuit of intake air heater C reports a circuit/open condition. Cold-start preheating cannot be commanded as expected.

t: Intake Air Heater C Circuit Range/Performance
s: The control circuit of intake air heater C reports a circuit range/performance condition. Cold-start preheating cannot be commanded as expected.

t: Intake Air Heater C Circuit Low
s: The control circuit of intake air heater C reports a circuit low condition. Cold-start preheating cannot be commanded as expected.

t: Intake Air Heater C Circuit High
s: The control circuit of intake air heater C reports a circuit high condition. Cold-start preheating cannot be commanded as expected.

t: Particulate Filter Pressure Sensor C Circuit
s: The ECM detects a circuit fault on the particulate filter pressure sensor c. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Particulate Filter Pressure Sensor C Range/Performance
s: The ECM detects a range/performance fault on the particulate filter pressure sensor c. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

s: The ECM detects a circuit intermittent/erratic fault on the particulate filter pressure sensor c. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

s: The ECM detects a circuit fault on the particulate filter pressure sensor d. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Particulate Filter Pressure Sensor D Range/Performance
s: The ECM detects a range/performance fault on the particulate filter pressure sensor d. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Particulate Filter Pressure Sensor D Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The ECM detects a circuit intermittent/erratic fault on the particulate filter pressure sensor d. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Auxiliary Thermostat A Stuck Open
s: The auxiliary thermostat A is stuck open. Coolant flow bypasses the thermal regulation, leading to slow warm-up and reduced engine efficiency.

t: Auxiliary Thermostat A Stuck Closed
s: The auxiliary thermostat A is stuck closed. Coolant cannot flow through the affected branch, risking overheating in the bypassed path.

t: Auxiliary Thermostat B Stuck Open
s: The auxiliary thermostat B is stuck open. Coolant flow bypasses the thermal regulation, leading to slow warm-up and reduced engine efficiency.

t: Auxiliary Thermostat B Stuck Closed
s: The auxiliary thermostat B is stuck closed. Coolant cannot flow through the affected branch, risking overheating in the bypassed path.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Pump A Underspeed
s: The HV battery pack coolant pump A runs below the calibrated minimum speed for the commanded duty. Mechanical resistance high, supply voltage low, or pump aged.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Pump B Supply Voltage Circuit
s: The HV battery pack coolant pump B reports a supply voltage circuit condition. Battery thermal management may be impaired.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Pump B Stuck/Stalled
s: The HV battery pack coolant pump B reports a stuck/stalled condition. Battery thermal management may be impaired.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Pump B Overspeed /Air in System
s: The HV battery pack coolant pump B reports a overspeed /air in system condition. Battery thermal management may be impaired.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Pump B Underspeed
s: The HV battery pack coolant pump B reports a underspeed condition. Battery thermal management may be impaired.

t: Engine Coolant Bypass Valve D Control Circuit Driver Current/Temperature Too High
s: The output driver for the engine coolant bypass valve D actuator reports current or junction temperature above its protection threshold. Stalled actuator or short to ground.

t: Belt Integrated Starter Generator Belt Tensioner Control Circuit/Open
s: The control circuit of the belt-integrated starter/generator (BISG) belt tensioner reports a control circuit/open condition. Belt tension cannot be modulated for assist/regen modes.

t: Belt Integrated Starter Generator Belt Tensioner Control Circuit Low
s: The control circuit of the belt-integrated starter/generator (BISG) belt tensioner reports a control circuit low condition. Belt tension cannot be modulated for assist/regen modes.

t: Belt Integrated Starter Generator Belt Tensioner Control Circuit High
s: The control circuit of the belt-integrated starter/generator (BISG) belt tensioner reports a control circuit high condition. Belt tension cannot be modulated for assist/regen modes.

t: A/C Clutch Control Circuit
s: The A/C compressor clutch control circuit reports a control circuit condition. Clutch engagement cannot be commanded reliably.

t: A/C Clutch Control Circuit Low
s: The A/C compressor clutch control circuit reports a control circuit low condition. Clutch engagement cannot be commanded reliably.

t: A/C Clutch Control Circuit High
s: The A/C compressor clutch control circuit reports a control circuit high condition. Clutch engagement cannot be commanded reliably.

t: A/C Clutch Control Circuit Driver Current/Temperature Too High
s: The A/C compressor clutch control circuit reports a control circuit driver current/temperature too high condition. Clutch engagement cannot be commanded reliably.

s: Motor electronics coolant pump B reports a supply voltage circuit condition. Power electronics cooling is impaired.

t: Motor Electronics Coolant Pump C Control Module Overtemperature

t: Engine Pre-Ignition Above Threshold
s: The ECM detects pre-ignition events (combustion before spark) above the calibrated threshold. Risk of severe engine damage from uncontrolled combustion peaks.

t: Engine Pre-Ignition Above Threshold - Forced Limited Power
s: Pre-ignition events exceed a critical threshold. The ECM forces limited engine power to protect pistons, rings, and connecting rods from further damage.

t: Exhaust Flow Control Valve A Position Not Learned
s: The ECM has not learned the end-stop calibration for exhaust flow control valve A. Position cannot be commanded with full precision until adaptation completes.

t: Exhaust Flow Control Valve B Position Not Learned
s: The ECM has not learned the end-stop calibration for exhaust flow control valve B. Position cannot be commanded with full precision until adaptation completes.

t: Exhaust Flow Control Valve C Control Circuit/Open

t: Cold Start EGR A/B Ratio Performance
s: During cold start, the ratio between EGR channel A and B flow does not match the calibrated cold-start strategy. One channel responds slower or sticks at low temperature.

t: Reductant System Leak Detected – Small Leak
s: The SCR module detects a small leak in the reductant system based on pressure decay rate after dosing pause. Slow loss of DEF/AdBlue.

t: Reductant System Leak Detected – Large Leak
s: The SCR module detects a large leak in the reductant system. Pressure cannot be maintained for dosing operations.

t: Reductant System Flow – Restricted
s: The SCR module detects restricted flow in the reductant system. Pressure rises above expected for the commanded dosing rate.

t: Reductant System Flow – Blocked
s: The SCR module detects fully blocked flow in the reductant system. No reductant reaches the injector despite commanded dosing.

t: Turbocharger Interstage Pressure Sensor A Circuit
s: The ECM detects a circuit fault on the turbocharger interstage pressure sensor a circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Coolant Pump E Control Circuit Performance/Stuck Off
s: Coolant pump E is commanded on but does not respond as expected. Pump motor stuck, relay weld, or driver fault.

t: Coolant Pump F Control Circuit Performance/Stuck Off
s: Coolant pump F is commanded on but does not respond as expected. Pump motor stuck, relay weld, or driver fault.

t: Cold Start Propulsion Torque Performance
s: During cold start, the propulsion torque output does not match the calibrated cold-start torque profile. Cold engine response, hybrid blend, or torque modelling deviates from expected.

t: A/C Compressor Motor Stalled
s: The electric A/C compressor motor is stalled. High mechanical resistance, internal seizure, or refrigerant overpressure prevents rotation.

t: Hybrid/EV Electronics Coolant Temperature Sensor A/B Correlation
s: The signals of the hybrid electronics coolant temperature sensors A and B do not correlate within the calibrated tolerance. One sensor drifts or installation offset between channels.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Coolant Temperature Sensor A/B Correlation
s: The signals of the HV battery pack coolant temperature sensors A and B do not correlate within the calibrated tolerance. One sensor drifts or installation offset between channels.

t: Engine Coolant Temperature Measurement System - Multiple Sensor Correlation
s: The signals of multiple engine coolant temperature sensors do not correlate within the calibrated tolerance. One sensor drifts or installation offset between channels.

t: Cold Start Intake Manifold Runner Performance Bank 1
s: During cold start, the intake manifold runner control on bank 1 does not produce the expected tumble/swirl effect. Linkage stiff, actuator sluggish, or runner alignment off.

t: Cold Start Intake Manifold Runner Performance Bank 2
s: During cold start, the intake manifold runner control on bank 2 does not produce the expected tumble/swirl effect. Linkage stiff, actuator sluggish, or runner alignment off.

t: Cold Start Turbocharger/Supercharger A Underboost
s: During cold start, turbocharger/supercharger A does not produce the expected boost pressure. Cold lubricant, lazy wastegate, or turbine spool-up delayed.

t: Cold Start Turbocharger/Supercharger A Overboost
s: During cold start, turbocharger/supercharger A produces boost pressure above the cold-start target. Wastegate sticking closed or boost control overshoot.

t: Intake Manifold Tuning Valve Exceeded Learning Limit Bank 1
s: The required adaptation offset for the intake manifold tuning valve on bank 1 exceeds the calibrated learning window. Mechanism wear or sensor drift beyond compensation.

t: Intake Manifold Tuning Valve Control Circuit Driver Current/Temperature Too High Bank 1
s: The output driver for the intake manifold tuning valve actuator on bank 1 reports current or junction temperature above its protection threshold.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Inductive Charger Control Module Processor Performance
s: The hybrid battery inductive charger control module reports a processor performance fault. Internal self-tests fail or response time outside calibration.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Inductive Charger Control Circuit Range/Performance
s: The hybrid battery inductive charger control circuit reports a range/performance condition. Charger response or feedback outside calibration.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Inductive Charging System High Voltage Interlock Circuit Performance
s: The high-voltage interlock circuit of the inductive charging system reports a performance condition. Safety interlock chain may be compromised.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Inductive Charger Output Voltage Performance
s: The output voltage of the inductive charger does not match the commanded value within tolerance. Air-gap variation, load mismatch, or coil aging.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Inductive Charger Input Circuit Range/Performance
s: The input circuit of the inductive charger reports a range/performance condition. Mains input power outside calibration or rectifier drift.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Heater Performance
s: The HV battery pack heater does not raise battery temperature within the calibrated time window. Heater element aged or supply current insufficient.

t: Forced Hybrid/EV Battery Disconnect
s: The BMS has forced a disconnect of the high-voltage battery from the vehicle bus. Triggered by safety condition such as overcurrent, isolation fault, or thermal runaway.

t: Forced Hybrid/EV System Shutdown
s: The hybrid control module has forced a complete system shutdown. Triggered by critical fault such as multiple isolation warnings or contactor failure.

t: Hybrid/EV Discharge System Fault
s: The hybrid control module detects a fault in the active discharge system used to safely drain the DC-link capacitors after shutdown. Discharge resistor or switch may have failed.

t: Cold Start Hybrid/EV Drive Motor Performance
s: During cold start, the hybrid drive motor does not deliver the expected torque or speed. Cold lubricant viscosity or magnet temperature affects motor characteristic.

t: Cold Start Hybrid/EV Drive Motor Inverter Torque Performance
s: During cold start, the inverter cannot deliver commanded torque to the drive motor within the expected window. Cold capacitor characteristic or switching loss elevated.

t: Cold Start Hybrid/EV Battery Performance
s: During cold start, the HV battery cannot deliver the expected power. Cold cell impedance reduces available current.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Cooling System B Performance
s: The hybrid battery cooling system B does not maintain target temperatures under load. Coolant flow, radiator fouling, or pump output reduced on this branch.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Cooling System C Performance
s: The hybrid battery cooling system C does not maintain target temperatures under load. Coolant flow, radiator fouling, or pump output reduced on this branch.

t: A/C Compressor Motor Voltage B High
s: The voltage B feedback of the electric A/C compressor motor is above the expected window. Phase wire shorted to B+ or driver fault.

t: A/C Compressor Motor Performance
s: The electric A/C compressor motor does not deliver the expected speed or torque for the commanded duty. Bearing wear, magnet aging, or load mismatch.

t: Reductant Temperature Too Low
s: The reductant temperature is below the calibrated minimum. DEF/AdBlue may not flow correctly through the dosing system - heater fault or extreme cold weather.

t: Starter/Generator Excitation Current Intermittent
s: The starter/generator excitation current drops out or jumps intermittently. Loose connector or unstable driver.

t: Starter/Generator Phase X-Y-Z Current Sensor Correlation
s: The phase X, Y, and Z current sensors of the starter/generator do not correlate within the calibrated tolerance. Sum of phase currents should be near zero.

t: Starter/Generator Position Sensor Correlation
s: The starter/generator rotor position sensors do not correlate with the calculated electrical angle within tolerance. Sensor offset or pole alignment off.

t: Particulate Filter B Incorrect
s: The ECM detects that the installed particulate filter B does not match the expected type or specification. Wrong replacement part or substrate damage.

t: Control Pilot Charging Switch B Circuit/Open
s: The control pilot charging switch B reports a circuit/open condition. SAE J1772 charging signal handshake may be compromised.

t: Control Pilot Charging Switch B Range/Performance
s: The control pilot charging switch B reports a range/performance condition. SAE J1772 charging signal handshake may be compromised.

t: Control Pilot Charging Switch B Circuit Low
s: The control pilot charging switch B reports a circuit low condition. SAE J1772 charging signal handshake may be compromised.

t: Control Pilot Charging Switch B Circuit High
s: The control pilot charging switch B reports a circuit high condition. SAE J1772 charging signal handshake may be compromised.

t: Control Pilot Charging Switch B Intermittent/Erratic
s: The control pilot charging switch B reports a intermittent/erratic condition. SAE J1772 charging signal handshake may be compromised.

t: Control Pilot Charging Switch B Stuck Off
s: The control pilot charging switch B reports a stuck off condition. SAE J1772 charging signal handshake may be compromised.

t: Control Pilot Charging Switch B Stuck On
s: The control pilot charging switch B reports a stuck on condition. SAE J1772 charging signal handshake may be compromised.

t: Fuel Injection Quantity B Lower Than Expected
s: The measured fuel injection quantity for group B is below the calibrated window. Injector flow drift, rail pressure off, or measurement bias.

t: Fuel Injection Quantity B Higher Than Expected
s: The measured fuel injection quantity for group B is above the calibrated window. Injector flow drift, rail pressure off, or measurement bias.

t: Cold Start Fuel Injection Quantity B Lower Than Expected
s: During cold start, the measured fuel injection quantity for group B is below the calibrated cold-start window. Cold-start strategy or injector flow drift.

t: Cold Start Fuel Injection Quantity B Higher Than Expected
s: During cold start, the measured fuel injection quantity for group B is above the calibrated cold-start window. Cold-start strategy or injector flow drift.

t: EVAP System Leak Detection Pump Control Circuit Performance
s: The EVAP leak detection pump control circuit reports a performance condition. Pump response or current draw outside calibration.

t: EVAP System Leak Detection Pump Performance/Stuck Off
s: The EVAP leak detection pump does not start when commanded. Motor seized, relay failure, or driver fault.

t: EVAP System Leak Detection Pump Stuck On
s: The EVAP leak detection pump stays running when commanded off. Relay welded or driver stuck on. The evaporative system captures fuel vapor from the tank and purges it into intake; leaks or stuck purge valves leak hydrocarbons and cause emissions failures.

t: Fuel Injection Quantity C Lower Than Expected
s: The measured fuel injection quantity for group C is below the calibrated window. Injector flow drift, rail pressure off, or measurement bias.

t: Fuel Injection Quantity C Higher Than Expected
s: The measured fuel injection quantity for group C is above the calibrated window. Injector flow drift, rail pressure off, or measurement bias.

t: Fuel Injection Quantity D Lower Than Expected
s: The measured fuel injection quantity for group D is below the calibrated window. Injector flow drift, rail pressure off, or measurement bias.

t: Fuel Injection Quantity D Higher Than Expected
s: The measured fuel injection quantity for group D is above the calibrated window. Injector flow drift, rail pressure off, or measurement bias.

t: Cold Start Fuel Injection Quantity C Lower Than Expected
s: During cold start, the measured fuel injection quantity for group C is below the calibrated cold-start window. Cold-start strategy or injector flow drift.

t: Cold Start Fuel Injection Quantity C Higher Than Expected
s: During cold start, the measured fuel injection quantity for group C is above the calibrated cold-start window. Cold-start strategy or injector flow drift.

t: Cold Start Fuel Injection Quantity D Lower Than Expected
s: During cold start, the measured fuel injection quantity for group D is below the calibrated cold-start window. Cold-start strategy or injector flow drift.

t: Cold Start Fuel Injection Quantity D Higher Than Expected
s: During cold start, the measured fuel injection quantity for group D is above the calibrated cold-start window. Cold-start strategy or injector flow drift.

t: Drive Motor A Clutch Stuck Engaged
s: The drive motor A clutch cannot leave the engaged state when release is commanded. The drive motor's position, current and temperature are watched continuously; a fault here downgrades the inverter to a safe operating point or triggers torque reduction.

t: Drive Motor A Clutch Stuck Disengaged
s: The drive motor A clutch cannot leave the disengaged state when engagement is commanded. The drive motor's position, current and temperature are watched continuously; a fault here downgrades the inverter to a safe operating point or triggers torque reduction.

t: Drive Motor A Clutch Actuator Performance
s: The drive motor A clutch actuator does not respond within the calibrated time window. The drive motor's position, current and temperature are watched continuously; a fault here downgrades the inverter to a safe operating point or triggers torque reduction.

t: Cold Start Engine Coolant Bypass Valve C Range/Performance
s: During cold start, the engine coolant bypass valve C response does not match the cold-start calibration.

t: Cold Start Engine Coolant Bypass Valve D Stuck Open
s: During cold start, the engine coolant bypass valve D is stuck in the open position. Coolant bypasses the warm-up loop.

t: Cold Start Engine Coolant Pump Underspeed
s: During cold start, the engine coolant pump runs below the calibrated minimum speed. Cold viscosity load too high or pump weak.

t: Cylinder 1 Intake Valve Lift Range/Performance

t: System Too Lean During Cylinder Deactivation Bank 1

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack A Cell Thickness
s: The BMS detects abnormal cell thickness in HV battery pack A. Cell swelling indicates aging, thermal stress, or imminent failure.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack A Cell Thickness Sensor Circuit High
s: The cell thickness sensor of HV battery pack A reports voltage above the expected window. Cell-group voltage monitoring is the foundation of HV battery safety — drift here triggers contactor opening, charge inhibit, and reduced power to protect the pack.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack A Cell Thickness Sensor Circuit Low
s: The cell thickness sensor of HV battery pack A reports voltage below the expected window. Cell-group voltage monitoring is the foundation of HV battery safety — drift here triggers contactor opening, charge inhibit, and reduced power to protect the pack.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Pack A Cell Thickness Sensor Circuit Performance
s: The cell thickness sensor of HV battery pack A signal does not match plausibility checks. Cell-group voltage monitoring is the foundation of HV battery safety — drift here triggers contactor opening, charge inhibit, and reduced power to protect the pack.
