t: A/C Compressor Control Module Internal Temperature Sensor B Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The ECM detects a circuit intermittent/erratic fault on the a/c compressor control module internal temperature sensor b circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Cold Start Engine Coolant Bypass Valve D Stuck Closed
s: During cold start, engine coolant bypass valve D is stuck closed. Coolant cannot flow through the affected branch.

t: Cold Start Engine Coolant Pump Overspeed
s: During cold start, the engine coolant pump exceeds the calibrated maximum speed. Indicates load lost or supply voltage too high.

t: Fuel Rail Pressure Performance - Engine Off
s: After engine shutdown, the fuel rail pressure does not maintain the calibrated decay profile. Indicates injector leakage or check valve fault.

t: Battery Charger B Input Voltage Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The input voltage sensor of battery charger B reports a range/performance fault. Mains input voltage measurement is unreliable.

t: Battery Charger B Input Voltage Sensor Circuit Low
s: The input voltage sensor of battery charger B reports a low fault. Mains input voltage measurement is unreliable.

t: Battery Charger B Input Voltage Sensor Circuit High
s: The input voltage sensor of battery charger B reports a high fault. Mains input voltage measurement is unreliable.

t: Proximity Detection Circuit B
s: The proximity detection circuit B (SAE J1772 PD signal) reports a circuit condition. Charging cable plug detection is impaired.

t: Proximity Detection Circuit B Range/Performance
s: The proximity detection circuit B (SAE J1772 PD signal) reports a range/performance condition. Charging cable plug detection is impaired.

t: Proximity Detection Circuit B Low
s: The proximity detection circuit B (SAE J1772 PD signal) reports a low condition. Charging cable plug detection is impaired.

t: Proximity Detection Circuit B High
s: The proximity detection circuit B (SAE J1772 PD signal) reports a high condition. Charging cable plug detection is impaired.

t: Proximity Detection Circuit B Intermittent/Erratic
s: The proximity detection circuit B (SAE J1772 PD signal) reports a intermittent/erratic condition. Charging cable plug detection is impaired.

t: Battery Charger B Charge Sequence Signal 1 Circuit High
s: The charge sequence signal 1 circuit of battery charger B reports voltage above the expected window. Charging handshake compromised.

t: Battery Charger B Charge Sequence Signal 1 Circuit Low
s: The charge sequence signal 1 circuit of battery charger B reports voltage below the expected window. Charging handshake compromised.

t: Battery Charger B Charge Sequence Signal 2 Circuit High
s: The charge sequence signal 2 circuit of battery charger B reports voltage above the expected window. Charging handshake compromised.

t: Battery Charger B Charge Sequence Signal 2 Circuit Low
s: The charge sequence signal 2 circuit of battery charger B reports voltage below the expected window. Charging handshake compromised.

t: Battery Charger A Internal Power Supply Performance
s: The internal low-voltage power supply of battery charger A does not maintain the expected output. Internal regulator drift or overload.

t: Battery Charger B Internal Power Supply Performance
s: The internal low-voltage power supply of battery charger B does not maintain the expected output. Internal regulator drift or overload.

t: Charge Port Door B Open Request Switch Wake-Up Circuit
s: The wake-up circuit for the charge port door B open request switch reports a fault. Switch press may not wake the BMS for charge port unlocking.

t: Charge Port Door B Open Request Switch Circuit
s: The charge port door B open request switch reports a circuit condition. Operator request for charge port unlocking is unreliable.

t: Charge Port Door B Open Request Switch Circuit Performance
s: The charge port door B open request switch reports a circuit performance condition. Operator request for charge port unlocking is unreliable.

t: Charge Port Door B Open Request Switch Circuit Low
s: The charge port door B open request switch reports a circuit low condition. Operator request for charge port unlocking is unreliable.

t: Charge Port Door B Open Request Switch Circuit High
s: The charge port door B open request switch reports a circuit high condition. Operator request for charge port unlocking is unreliable.

t: Charge Port Door B Open Request Switch Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The charge port door B open request switch reports a circuit intermittent/erratic condition. Operator request for charge port unlocking is unreliable.

t: Charge Port Door B Position Sensor Circuit
s: The charge port door B position sensor reports a circuit condition. Door state cannot be verified reliably.

t: Charge Port Door B Position Sensor Circuit Performance
s: The charge port door B position sensor reports a circuit performance condition. Door state cannot be verified reliably.

t: Charge Port Door B Position Sensor Circuit Low
s: The charge port door B position sensor reports a circuit low condition. Door state cannot be verified reliably.

t: Charge Port Door B Position Sensor Circuit High
s: The charge port door B position sensor reports a circuit high condition. Door state cannot be verified reliably.

t: Charge Port Door B Position Sensor Circuit Intermittent/Erratic
s: The charge port door B position sensor reports a circuit intermittent/erratic condition. Door state cannot be verified reliably.

t: Charge Port Door B Unlock Control Circuit
s: The charge port door B unlock control reports a circuit condition. Door cannot be commanded to unlock reliably.

t: Charge Port Door B Unlock Control Circuit Performance
s: The charge port door B unlock control reports a circuit performance condition. Door cannot be commanded to unlock reliably.

t: Charge Port Door B Unlock Control Circuit Low
s: The charge port door B unlock control reports a circuit low condition. Door cannot be commanded to unlock reliably.

t: Charge Port Door B Unlock Control Circuit High
s: The charge port door B unlock control reports a circuit high condition. Door cannot be commanded to unlock reliably.

t: Exhaust Gas Temperature Sensor Circuit Bank 1 Sensor 6
s: The exhaust gas temperature sensor on bank 1 sensor 6 reports a circuit fault. EGT measurement at this position is unreliable.

t: Exhaust Gas Temperature Sensor Circuit High Bank 1 Sensor 6
s: The exhaust gas temperature sensor on bank 1 sensor 6 reports a circuit high fault. EGT measurement at this position is unreliable.

t: Exhaust Gas Temperature Sensor Circuit Low Bank 1 Sensor 6
s: The exhaust gas temperature sensor on bank 1 sensor 6 reports a circuit low fault. EGT measurement at this position is unreliable.

t: Exhaust Gas Temperature Sensor Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1 Sensor 6
s: The exhaust gas temperature sensor on bank 1 sensor 6 reports a circuit range/performance fault. EGT measurement at this position is unreliable.

t: Turbocharger Compressor Outlet Temperature Sensor Circuit
s: The temperature sensor at the turbocharger compressor outlet reports a generic fault. Charge air temperature post-compressor is unreliable.

t: DC/AC Converter A Input Voltage Too Low
s: The DC/AC converter A reports a "input voltage too low" condition. AC output for vehicle 12V loads is impaired.

t: DC/AC Converter A Input Voltage Too High
s: The DC/AC converter A reports a "input voltage too high" condition. AC output for vehicle 12V loads is impaired.

t: DC/AC Converter A Over Temperature
s: The DC/AC converter A reports a "over temperature" condition. AC output for vehicle 12V loads is impaired.

t: Active Radiator Air Flow Control Module A Control Circuit Performance
s: The active radiator air flow control module A reports a control circuit performance condition. Active grille shutter operation is impaired.

t: Active Radiator Air Flow Control Module A Over Temperature
s: The active radiator air flow control module A reports a over temperature condition. Active grille shutter operation is impaired.

t: Active Radiator Air Flow Control Module A Performance
s: The active radiator air flow control module A reports a performance condition. Active grille shutter operation is impaired.

t: Reductant Metering Unit 2 Performance
s: The reductant metering unit 2 does not deliver the expected DEF/AdBlue dosing within the calibrated profile. Pump aging or pressure regulator drift.

t: Reductant Injection Valve Circuit Range/Performance Bank 1 Unit 2
s: The reductant injection valve on bank 1 unit 2 reports a range/performance fault. Dosing accuracy on this unit is impaired.

t: Reductant Injection Valve Stuck Closed Bank 1 Unit 2
s: The reductant injection valve on bank 1 unit 2 is stuck closed. No DEF/AdBlue can be dosed on this unit.

t: Throttle Valve Intake Air Pressure Sensor Circuit
s: The ECM detects a circuit fault on the throttle valve intake air pressure sensor circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Throttle Valve Intake Air Pressure Sensor Circuit Low
s: The ECM detects a circuit low fault on the throttle valve intake air pressure sensor circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Throttle Valve Intake Air Pressure Sensor Circuit High
s: The ECM detects a circuit high fault on the throttle valve intake air pressure sensor circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Throttle Valve Intake Air Pressure Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The ECM detects a range/performance fault on the throttle valve intake air pressure sensor circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Inductive Charger High Voltage Interlock Circuit/Open
s: The inductive charger reports a high voltage interlock circuit/open condition. Inductive charging cannot proceed reliably.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Inductive Charger Input Voltage Low
s: The inductive charger reports a input voltage low condition. Inductive charging cannot proceed reliably.

t: Hybrid/EV Battery Inductive Charger Input Voltage High
s: The inductive charger reports a input voltage high condition. Inductive charging cannot proceed reliably.

t: Replace 12V Battery
s: The vehicle electrical system reports that the 12V auxiliary battery has reached end-of-life and should be replaced. State-of-health below threshold.

t: Reductant Injection Valve Circuit Range/Performance Bank 2 Unit 2
s: The reductant injection valve on bank 2 unit 2 reports a range/performance fault. Dosing accuracy on this unit is impaired.

t: Crankcase Temperature Sensor Circuit
s: The ECM detects a circuit fault on the crankcase temperature sensor circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Crankcase Temperature Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The ECM detects a range/performance fault on the crankcase temperature sensor circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Crankcase Temperature Sensor Circuit High
s: The ECM detects a circuit high fault on the crankcase temperature sensor circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Crankcase Temperature Sensor Circuit Low
s: The ECM detects a circuit low fault on the crankcase temperature sensor circuit. Reading cannot be used reliably for control.

t: Battery Charging Mode Switch Stuck
s: The battery charging mode switch is mechanically stuck in one position. Driver cannot change between charging modes.

t: Turbocharger Compressor Outlet Temperature Sensor Circuit Low
s: The turbocharger compressor outlet temperature sensor reports a circuit low fault. Charge air temperature post-compressor is unreliable.

t: Turbocharger Compressor Outlet Temperature Sensor Circuit High
s: The turbocharger compressor outlet temperature sensor reports a circuit high fault. Charge air temperature post-compressor is unreliable.

t: Turbocharger Compressor Outlet Temperature Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
s: The turbocharger compressor outlet temperature sensor reports a circuit range/performance fault. Charge air temperature post-compressor is unreliable.

t: Cylinder Deactivation System Bank 1
s: The engine control module has detected a fault in the cylinder deactivation system on bank 1. Variable displacement engines shut off cylinders under low load to save fuel — a fault here disables the function.

t: Cylinder 1 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open circuit at the cylinder 1 deactivation or intake valve control solenoid. Without a valid circuit, cylinder 1 cannot be deactivated.

t: Cylinder 1 Deactivation Performance/Stuck Off
s: The engine control module has detected that cylinder 1 remains deactivated when activation is commanded — the cylinder does not contribute to combustion when expected.

t: Cylinder 1 Deactivation Stuck On
s: The engine control module has detected that cylinder 1 remains deactivated when activation is commanded — the deactivation hardware is stuck in the off state. Symptoms include rough running and reduced power.

t: Cylinder 1 Deactivation Circuit Low Voltage
s: The engine control module has detected low voltage or a short to ground in the cylinder 1 deactivation control circuit.

t: Cylinder 1 Deactivation Circuit High Voltage
s: The engine control module has detected high voltage or a short to power in the cylinder 1 deactivation control circuit.

t: Cylinder 1 Deactivation Stuck Off
s: The engine control module has detected that cylinder 1 deactivation hardware is stuck in the off state. The deactivation cannot be released when full-cylinder operation is commanded.

t: Cylinder 1 Deactivation Stuck On
s: The engine control module has detected that cylinder 1 deactivation hardware is stuck in the active state. Cylinder 1 stays deactivated when full-cylinder operation is commanded.

t: Cylinder 1 Deactivation Circuit Low Voltage
s: The engine control module has detected low voltage on the cylinder 1 deactivation control circuit. Variant of P3404 reported on platforms with separate diagnostic ranges.

t: Cylinder 1 Deactivation Circuit High Voltage
s: The engine control module has detected high voltage on the cylinder 1 deactivation control circuit. Variant of P3405 reported on platforms with separate diagnostic ranges.

t: Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit Bank 1
s: Generic electrical fault on the cylinder deactivation/intake valve control circuit on bank 1. The mechanism that switches off intake valves on selected cylinders cannot be commanded.

t: Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit Bank 2
s: Generic electrical fault on the cylinder deactivation/intake valve control circuit on bank 2.

t: Deactivation/Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Bank 1
s: Generic electrical fault on the cylinder deactivation/exhaust valve control circuit on bank 1. The exhaust control valve modulates exhaust backpressure or routing (EGR feed, sound, regen support); a malfunction shifts torque mapping and may trigger limp mode.

t: Deactivation/Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Bank 2
s: Generic electrical fault on the cylinder deactivation/exhaust valve control circuit on bank 2. The exhaust control valve modulates exhaust backpressure or routing (EGR feed, sound, regen support); a malfunction shifts torque mapping and may trigger limp mode.

t: Cylinder Deactivation System Bank 2
s: The engine control module has detected a fault in the cylinder deactivation system on bank 2. The system disables fuel and valve operation on selected cylinders under low load to reduce fuel consumption; a fault disables further deactivation requests on this bank.

t: Cylinder 2 Deactivation Performance
s: The engine control module has detected that cylinder 2 deactivation does not perform as expected — the actual cylinder behavior does not match the commanded state.

t: Cylinder 2 Deactivation Stuck Off
s: The engine control module has detected that cylinder 2 deactivation hardware is stuck in the off state. The deactivation cannot be released when full-cylinder operation is commanded.

t: Cylinder 2 Deactivation Stuck On
s: The engine control module has detected that cylinder 2 deactivation hardware is stuck in the active state. Cylinder 2 stays deactivated when full-cylinder operation is commanded.

t: Cylinder 2 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit/Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open or out-of-range circuit at the cylinder 2 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid. Without a valid circuit the exhaust valve of cylinder 2 cannot be commanded for cylinder deactivation.

t: Cylinder 2 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Low
s: The engine control module has detected a low-side voltage fault (short to ground) on the cylinder 2 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid circuit. The system disables further cylinder deactivation requests until the circuit is repaired.

t: Cylinder 2 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit High
s: The engine control module has detected a high-side voltage fault (short to power) on the cylinder 2 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 3 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit/Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open or out-of-range circuit at the cylinder 3 deactivation or intake valve control solenoid. Cylinder 3 cannot be deactivated until the circuit is restored.

t: Cylinder 3 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Performance
s: The engine control module has detected a performance fault on the cylinder 3 deactivation or intake valve control system — the response of the actuator does not match the commanded state.

t: Cylinder 3 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit Low
s: The engine control module has detected a low-side voltage fault (short to ground) on the cylinder 3 deactivation or intake valve solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit Performance Bank 1
s: The intake valve deactivation system on bank 1 is electrically active but the mechanical response does not match commanded state. Often hydraulic pin sticking from sludge or low oil pressure.

t: Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit Performance Bank 2
s: The intake valve deactivation system on bank 2 is electrically active but the mechanical response does not match commanded state.

t: Deactivation/Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Performance Bank 1
s: The exhaust valve deactivation system on bank 1 is electrically active but the mechanical response does not match commanded state.

t: Deactivation/Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Performance Bank 2
s: The exhaust valve deactivation system on bank 2 is electrically active but the mechanical response does not match commanded state.

t: Cylinder 3 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit High
s: The engine control module has detected a high-side voltage fault (short to power) on the cylinder 3 deactivation or intake valve solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 3 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit/Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open or out-of-range circuit at the cylinder 3 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid. Cylinder 3 cannot be deactivated on the exhaust side until the circuit is restored.

t: Cylinder 3 Exhaust Valve Control Performance
s: The engine control module has detected a performance fault on the cylinder 3 exhaust valve deactivation system — the actuator response does not match the commanded state.

t: Cylinder 3 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Low
s: The engine control module has detected a low-side voltage fault (short to ground) on the cylinder 3 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 3 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit High
s: The engine control module has detected a high-side voltage fault (short to power) on the cylinder 3 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 3 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open circuit at the cylinder 3 deactivation or intake valve control solenoid. Without a valid circuit, cylinder 3 cannot be deactivated.

s: The engine control module has detected a high-side voltage fault (short to power) on the cylinder 4 deactivation or intake valve solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 4 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit/Open

t: Cylinder 4 Exhaust Valve Control Performance

t: Cylinder 4 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Low
s: The engine control module has detected a low-side voltage fault (short to ground) on the cylinder 4 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 4 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit High
s: The engine control module has detected a high-side voltage fault (short to power) on the cylinder 4 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 4 Deactivation Performance

t: Cylinder 4 Deactivation Stuck On

s: The engine control module has detected an open or out-of-range circuit at the cylinder 6 deactivation or intake valve control solenoid. Cylinder 6 cannot be deactivated until the circuit is restored.

t: Cylinder 5 Deactivation Performance
s: The engine control module has detected that cylinder 5 deactivation does not perform as expected — the actual cylinder behavior does not match the commanded state.

s: The engine control module has detected a low-side voltage fault (short to ground) on the cylinder 6 deactivation or intake valve solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 6 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit High
s: The engine control module has detected a high-side voltage fault (short to power) on the cylinder 6 deactivation or intake valve solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

s: The engine control module has detected an open or out-of-range circuit at the cylinder 6 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid. Cylinder 6 cannot be deactivated on the exhaust side until the circuit is restored.

s: The engine control module has detected a low-side voltage fault (short to ground) on the cylinder 6 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

s: The engine control module has detected a high-side voltage fault (short to power) on the cylinder 6 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 7 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit/Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open or out-of-range circuit at the cylinder 7 deactivation or intake valve control solenoid. Cylinder 7 cannot be deactivated until the circuit is restored.

t: Cylinder 6 Deactivation Performance
s: The engine control module has detected that cylinder 6 deactivation does not perform as expected — the actual cylinder behavior does not match the commanded state.

t: Cylinder 7 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit Low
s: The engine control module has detected a low-side voltage fault (short to ground) on the cylinder 7 deactivation or intake valve solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 7 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit High
s: The engine control module has detected a high-side voltage fault (short to power) on the cylinder 7 deactivation or intake valve solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 7 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit/Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open or out-of-range circuit at the cylinder 7 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid. Cylinder 7 cannot be deactivated on the exhaust side until the circuit is restored.

t: Cylinder 5 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Low
s: The engine control module has detected a low voltage condition in the control circuit of the cylinder 5 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid. A short to ground or low-side driver fault prevents proper actuation of the exhaust valve during cylinder deactivation events on bank-relevant cylinder 5.

t: Cylinder 6 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Low
s: The engine control module has detected a low voltage condition in the control circuit of the cylinder 6 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid. A short to ground or low-side driver fault prevents proper actuation of the exhaust valve during cylinder deactivation events on cylinder 6.

t: Cylinder 7 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Low
s: The engine control module has detected a low voltage condition in the control circuit of the cylinder 7 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid. A short to ground or low-side driver fault prevents proper actuation of the exhaust valve during cylinder deactivation events on cylinder 7.

t: Cylinder 8 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit/Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open or out-of-range circuit at the cylinder 8 deactivation or intake valve control solenoid. Cylinder 8 cannot be deactivated until the circuit is restored.

t: Cylinder 7 Deactivation
s: The engine control module has detected a fault in the cylinder 7 deactivation system. Cylinder 7 cannot reliably switch between active and deactivated state.

t: Cylinder 8 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit Low
s: The engine control module has detected a low-side voltage fault (short to ground) on the cylinder 8 deactivation or intake valve solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 8 Deactivation/Intake Valve Control Circuit High
s: The engine control module has detected a high-side voltage fault (short to power) on the cylinder 8 deactivation or intake valve solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 8 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit/Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open or out-of-range circuit at the cylinder 8 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid. Cylinder 8 cannot be deactivated on the exhaust side until the circuit is restored.

t: Cylinder 8 Exhaust Valve Control Performance
s: The engine control module has detected a performance fault on the cylinder 8 exhaust valve deactivation system — the actuator response does not match the commanded state.

t: Cylinder 8 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Low
s: The engine control module has detected a low-side voltage fault (short to ground) on the cylinder 8 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 8 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit High
s: The engine control module has detected a high-side voltage fault (short to power) on the cylinder 8 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid circuit. Cylinder deactivation is disabled while the fault is present.

t: Cylinder 8 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit High
s: The engine control module has detected a high voltage condition in the control circuit of the cylinder 8 exhaust valve deactivation solenoid. A short to voltage or open low-side path causes the driver to read above the expected range, disabling cylinder deactivation requests for cylinder 8.

t: Cylinder 8 Deactivation
s: The engine control module has detected a fault in the cylinder 8 deactivation system. Common in V8 engines with active fuel management — cylinder 8 cannot reliably switch state.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Hydraulic Circuit Bank 1 Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open hydraulic circuit in the cylinder deactivation system on bank 1. The system uses pressurized engine oil routed through dedicated lifters and oil control valves to collapse valves; an open circuit indicates loss of oil flow or a failed pressure switch.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Hydraulic Circuit Bank 1 Performance
s: The engine control module has detected a performance fault in the bank 1 hydraulic circuit used for cylinder deactivation. Oil pressure response or timing is outside the expected window during deactivation events, often caused by viscosity issues, internal leaks or worn lifters.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Hydraulic Circuit Bank 1 Low
s: The engine control module has detected low oil pressure in the bank 1 hydraulic circuit responsible for cylinder deactivation. Below threshold pressure the lifters cannot collapse and the system disables further deactivation requests on bank 1.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Hydraulic Circuit Bank 1 High
s: The engine control module has detected an excessively high oil pressure or stuck-on signal in the bank 1 hydraulic circuit for cylinder deactivation. Either a stuck oil control valve, a blocked relief path, or a shorted pressure switch is reporting permanently high pressure.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Hydraulic Circuit Bank 2 Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open hydraulic circuit in the cylinder deactivation system on bank 2. Loss of oil flow, a failed oil pressure switch or a disconnected oil control valve prevents the system from deactivating cylinders on bank 2.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Hydraulic Circuit Bank 2 Performance
s: The engine control module has detected a performance fault in the bank 2 hydraulic circuit used for cylinder deactivation. Oil pressure response or actuation timing falls outside the expected window during deactivation events on bank 2, often caused by viscosity issues, internal leaks or worn switching lifters.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Hydraulic Circuit Bank 2 Low
s: The engine control module has detected low oil pressure in the bank 2 hydraulic circuit responsible for cylinder deactivation. Below the calibrated threshold the switching lifters cannot collapse and cylinder deactivation on bank 2 is disabled until pressure recovers.

t: Cylinder 9 Deactivation
s: The engine control module has detected a fault in the cylinder 9 deactivation system. Found on V10/V12 engines with cylinder deactivation — cylinder 9 cannot reliably switch state.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Hydraulic Circuit Bank 2 High
s: The engine control module has detected an excessively high oil pressure or stuck-on signal in the bank 2 hydraulic circuit for cylinder deactivation. A stuck oil control valve, a blocked relief path or a shorted pressure switch is reporting permanently high pressure on bank 2.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Oil Pressure Switch Bank 1 Circuit
s: The engine control module has detected an electrical fault in the cylinder deactivation oil pressure switch circuit on bank 1. The switch confirms that hydraulic pressure has reached the level required to collapse the switching lifters; without a valid signal the system disables deactivation.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Oil Pressure Switch Bank 1 Performance
s: The engine control module has detected a rationality fault between commanded oil pressure and the bank 1 cylinder deactivation oil pressure switch state. The switch fails to change state at the expected pressure threshold during deactivation transitions.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Oil Pressure Switch Bank 2 Circuit
s: The engine control module has detected an electrical fault in the cylinder deactivation oil pressure switch circuit on bank 2. Without a valid signal the module cannot confirm that hydraulic pressure has reached the level needed to deactivate cylinders on bank 2.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Oil Pressure Switch Bank 2 Performance
s: The engine control module has detected a rationality fault between commanded oil pressure and the bank 2 cylinder deactivation oil pressure switch state. The switch fails to change state at the expected pressure threshold during deactivation transitions on bank 2.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Solenoid Bank 1 Control Circuit Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open circuit at the bank 1 cylinder deactivation solenoid. The solenoid routes oil pressure to the switching lifters; an open circuit prevents activation and the system disables deactivation requests on bank 1.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Solenoid Bank 2 Control Circuit Open
s: The engine control module has detected an open circuit at the bank 2 cylinder deactivation solenoid. The solenoid routes oil pressure to the switching lifters on bank 2; an open circuit prevents activation and the system disables deactivation requests on bank 2.

t: Cylinder 10 Deactivation
s: The engine control module has detected a fault in the cylinder 10 deactivation system. Found on V10/V12 engines — cylinder 10 cannot reliably switch state.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Solenoid Bank 1 Control Circuit Low
s: The engine control module has detected a low voltage condition at the bank 1 cylinder deactivation solenoid control circuit. A short to ground or low-side driver fault prevents proper actuation of the bank 1 deactivation hydraulic path.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Solenoid Bank 1 Control Circuit High
s: The engine control module has detected a high voltage condition at the bank 1 cylinder deactivation solenoid control circuit. A short to power or open low-side path causes the driver to read above the expected range, disabling deactivation requests on bank 1.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Solenoid Bank 2 Control Circuit Low
s: The engine control module has detected a low voltage condition at the bank 2 cylinder deactivation solenoid control circuit. A short to ground or low-side driver fault prevents proper actuation of the bank 2 deactivation hydraulic path.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Solenoid Bank 2 Control Circuit High
s: The engine control module has detected a high voltage condition at the bank 2 cylinder deactivation solenoid control circuit. A short to power or open low-side path causes the driver to read above the expected range, disabling deactivation requests on bank 2.

t: Cylinder 11 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Performance
s: Per-cylinder exhaust valve control on cylinder 11 does not respond as commanded. Mechanical issue at the deactivation actuator for that cylinder.

t: Cylinder 11 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit Low
s: Cylinder 11 exhaust valve control voltage below expected range — short to ground. The exhaust control valve modulates exhaust backpressure or routing (EGR feed, sound, regen support); a malfunction shifts torque mapping and may trigger limp mode.

t: Cylinder 11 Exhaust Valve Control Circuit High
s: Cylinder 11 exhaust valve control voltage above expected range — short to power or open ground. The exhaust control valve modulates exhaust backpressure or routing (EGR feed, sound, regen support); a malfunction shifts torque mapping and may trigger limp mode.

t: Cylinder 11 Deactivation
s: The engine control module has detected a fault in the cylinder 11 deactivation system. Found on V12 engines with cylinder deactivation.

t: Cylinder Deactivation System Performance Bank 1
s: The engine control module has detected that the cylinder deactivation system on bank 1 is not performing as expected. Closed-loop monitoring of pressure response, valve timing or misfire counters during transitions indicates the system fails to deactivate or reactivate cylinders within calibrated limits.

t: Cylinder Deactivation System Performance Bank 2
s: The engine control module has detected that the cylinder deactivation system on bank 2 is not performing as expected. Closed-loop monitoring of pressure response, valve timing or misfire counters during transitions indicates the system fails to deactivate or reactivate cylinders within calibrated limits.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Position Sensor Bank 1
s: The engine control module has detected an implausible signal from the bank 1 cylinder deactivation position sensor. Modern systems use a Hall-effect or inductive sensor to confirm switching lifter position; a fault prevents safe deactivation transitions.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Position Sensor Bank 2
s: The engine control module has detected an implausible signal from the bank 2 cylinder deactivation position sensor. The sensor confirms switching lifter position; without a valid signal the module cannot safely command deactivation transitions on bank 2.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Position Sensor Bank 1 Range/Performance
s: The engine control module has detected the bank 1 cylinder deactivation position sensor signal is within electrical limits but does not change as expected during commanded deactivation transitions. Mechanical drag, sensor drift or contamination is the typical cause.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Position Sensor Bank 2 Range/Performance
s: The engine control module has detected the bank 2 cylinder deactivation position sensor signal is within electrical limits but does not change as expected during commanded deactivation transitions. Mechanical drag, sensor drift or contamination is the typical cause.

t: Cylinder 12 Deactivation
s: The engine control module has detected a fault in the cylinder 12 deactivation system. Found on V12 engines with cylinder deactivation.

t: Cylinder Deactivation System Bank 2
s: The engine control module has detected a fault in the cylinder deactivation system on bank 2. Same as P3400 but for the second cylinder bank in V-engines.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Pressure Sensor Bank 1
s: The engine control module has detected a fault with the bank 1 cylinder deactivation pressure sensor. Newer variable displacement systems use an analogue oil pressure sensor in addition to or in place of the legacy switch; a fault disables closed-loop deactivation control on bank 1.

t: Cylinder Deactivation Pressure Sensor Bank 2
s: The engine control module has detected a fault with the bank 2 cylinder deactivation pressure sensor. The analogue oil pressure sensor is used to verify the deactivation hydraulic state; a fault disables closed-loop deactivation control on bank 2.

t: Cylinder 3 Deactivation/Intake Valve Performance
s: Per-cylinder intake valve deactivation on cylinder 3 does not respond as commanded.

t: Cylinder 4 Deactivation/Intake Valve Performance
s: Per-cylinder intake valve deactivation on cylinder 4 does not respond as commanded.

t: Cylinder 5 Deactivation/Intake Valve Performance
s: Per-cylinder intake valve deactivation on cylinder 5 does not respond as commanded.

t: Cylinder 6 Deactivation/Intake Valve Performance
s: Per-cylinder intake valve deactivation on cylinder 6 does not respond as commanded.

t: Cylinder 7 Deactivation/Intake Valve Performance
s: Per-cylinder intake valve deactivation on cylinder 7 does not respond as commanded.

t: Cylinder 8 Deactivation/Intake Valve Performance
s: Per-cylinder intake valve deactivation on cylinder 8 does not respond as commanded.

t: Cylinder 9 Deactivation/Intake Valve Performance
s: Per-cylinder intake valve deactivation on cylinder 9 does not respond as commanded.

t: Cylinder 10 Deactivation/Intake Valve Performance
s: Per-cylinder intake valve deactivation on cylinder 10 does not respond as commanded.

t: Cylinder 11 Deactivation/Intake Valve Performance
s: Per-cylinder intake valve deactivation on cylinder 11 does not respond as commanded.

t: Cylinder 12 Deactivation/Intake Valve Performance
s: Per-cylinder intake valve deactivation on cylinder 12 does not respond as commanded.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Circuit Bank 1
s: Generic electrical fault on the temperature sensor of the intake camshaft position actuator on bank 1 (used by ECU to thermally protect the VVT actuator from overheating).

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Range/Performance Bank 1
s: Temperature sensor of the intake camshaft actuator on bank 1 reads implausibly compared to engine warm-up state or other temperatures.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Circuit Low Bank 1
s: Cam actuator A temperature sensor on bank 1 signal voltage below expected range — short to ground. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Circuit High Bank 1
s: Cam actuator A temperature sensor on bank 1 signal voltage above expected range — short to power or open.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Circuit Bank 2
s: Generic electrical fault on the temperature sensor of the intake camshaft position actuator on bank 2. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Range/Performance Bank 2
s: Cam actuator A temperature sensor on bank 2 reads implausibly compared to engine warm-up state. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Circuit Low Bank 2
s: Cam actuator A temperature sensor on bank 2 signal voltage below expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Circuit High Bank 2
s: Cam actuator A temperature sensor on bank 2 signal voltage above expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Circuit Bank 1
s: Generic electrical fault on the position sensor of the intake camshaft actuator on bank 1 (closed-loop feedback for VVT angle).

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Range/Performance Bank 1
s: Position sensor of intake cam actuator on bank 1 reads implausibly compared to commanded actuator position.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Circuit Low Bank 1
s: Cam actuator A position sensor on bank 1 signal voltage below expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Circuit High Bank 1
s: Cam actuator A position sensor on bank 1 signal voltage above expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Circuit Bank 2
s: Generic electrical fault on the position sensor of the intake cam actuator on bank 2. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Range/Performance Bank 2
s: Position sensor of intake cam actuator on bank 2 reads implausibly. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Circuit Low Bank 2
s: Cam actuator A position sensor on bank 2 signal voltage below expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Circuit High Bank 2
s: Cam actuator A position sensor on bank 2 signal voltage above expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Circuit Bank 1
s: Generic electrical fault on the temperature sensor of the exhaust camshaft position actuator on bank 1. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Range/Performance Bank 1
s: Temperature sensor of exhaust cam actuator on bank 1 reads implausibly. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Circuit Low Bank 1
s: Cam actuator B temperature sensor on bank 1 signal voltage below expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Circuit High Bank 1
s: Cam actuator B temperature sensor on bank 1 signal voltage above expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Circuit Bank 2
s: Generic electrical fault on the temperature sensor of the exhaust camshaft position actuator on bank 2. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Range/Performance Bank 2
s: Temperature sensor of exhaust cam actuator on bank 2 reads implausibly. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Circuit Low Bank 2
s: Cam actuator B temperature sensor on bank 2 signal voltage below expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Temperature Sensor Circuit High Bank 2
s: Cam actuator B temperature sensor on bank 2 signal voltage above expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Circuit Bank 1
s: Generic electrical fault on the position sensor of the exhaust cam actuator on bank 1. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Range/Performance Bank 1
s: Position sensor of exhaust cam actuator on bank 1 reads implausibly compared to commanded position. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Circuit Low Bank 1
s: Cam actuator B position sensor on bank 1 signal voltage below expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Circuit High Bank 1
s: Cam actuator B position sensor on bank 1 signal voltage above expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Circuit Bank 2
s: Generic electrical fault on the position sensor of the exhaust cam actuator on bank 2. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Range/Performance Bank 2
s: Position sensor of exhaust cam actuator on bank 2 reads implausibly. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Circuit Low Bank 2
s: Cam actuator B position sensor on bank 2 signal voltage below expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Position Sensor A Circuit High Bank 2
s: Cam actuator B position sensor on bank 2 signal voltage above expected range. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit Driver Current/Temperature Too High Bank 1
s: The driver stage controlling the intake cam position actuator on bank 1 reports overcurrent or overtemperature, triggering thermal shutdown to protect the electronics.

t: A Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit Driver Current/Temperature Too High Bank 2
s: Driver stage controlling the intake cam position actuator on bank 2 reports overcurrent or overtemperature. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit Driver Current/Temperature Too High Bank 1
s: Driver stage controlling the exhaust cam position actuator on bank 1 reports overcurrent or overtemperature. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: B Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit Driver Current/Temperature Too High Bank 2
s: Driver stage controlling the exhaust cam position actuator on bank 2 reports overcurrent or overtemperature. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: Camshaft Position Control Module Performance
s: The dedicated camshaft position control module reports an internal performance fault — self-diagnostic flagged an issue with internal computation, drivers or sensor interfaces.

t: High Speed CAN Communication Bus
s: A general fault on the high-speed CAN bus has been detected. This bus typically connects powertrain modules (ECM, TCM, ABS) at 500 kbit/s and a fault disables communication between them, often triggering multiple secondary DTCs in other modules.

t: High Speed CAN Communication Bus Performance
s: The high-speed CAN bus is functional but performance is degraded — frame errors, bit errors or excessive bus load have been detected. Communication may be intermittent rather than completely lost, leading to sporadic warning lamps.

t: High Speed CAN Communication Bus (+) Open
s: The CAN-High line of the high-speed bus is open. Without the differential pair, the controller cannot decode any traffic and reports loss of all participating modules at once. Often follows a wiring repair, accident damage, or a corroded connector pin.

t: High Speed CAN Communication Bus (+) Low
s: The CAN-High signal stays below its valid voltage window. Common cause is a short to ground on the CAN-H wire or a transceiver that pulls the line down. The bus stops working until the offending node is isolated.

t: High Speed CAN Communication Bus (+) High
s: The CAN-High line is held above its valid voltage range, usually because of a short to battery voltage. Bus communication collapses for every connected module.

t: High Speed CAN Communication Bus (-) Open
s: The CAN-Low wire of the high-speed bus is open. The differential signal can no longer be evaluated correctly, so all bus participants report communication loss simultaneously.

t: High Speed CAN Communication Bus (-) Low
s: The CAN-Low line is shorted to ground or held below its valid recessive level. The differential pair cannot transmit data and the bus is effectively dead.

t: High Speed CAN Communication Bus (-) High
s: The CAN-Low signal is held above its valid range, typically because of a short to battery voltage on the CAN-L wire. Bus communication is lost for every node sharing this segment.

t: High Speed CAN Communication Bus (-) Shorted to Bus (+)
s: CAN-High and CAN-Low are shorted together. The differential signal collapses to zero and the bus controller can no longer separate dominant from recessive bits, so all communication stops.

t: Medium Speed CAN Communication Bus
s: A fault on the medium-speed CAN bus has been detected. This bus (typically 125 kbit/s) connects body and comfort modules — BCM, climate, doors, infotainment. A failure usually disables comfort functions but leaves the powertrain operational.

t: Medium Speed CAN Communication Bus Performance
s: The medium-speed CAN bus is operating but with degraded performance — frame errors or excessive error counters. Comfort and infotainment functions may operate intermittently.

t: Medium Speed CAN Communication Bus (-) Open
s: The CAN-Low wire on the medium-speed comfort/body bus is open. Body modules such as door modules, climate control or seat modules lose communication, but powertrain functions usually stay alive.

t: Medium Speed CAN Communication Bus (-) Low
s: The CAN-Low signal of the medium-speed bus is shorted to ground. Comfort and body modules can no longer exchange messages and trigger several user-visible faults at once (windows, locking, lighting).

t: Medium Speed CAN Communication Bus (-) High
s: CAN-Low on the medium-speed bus is shorted to battery voltage. The differential bus cannot work, body and comfort modules drop out of the network.

t: Medium Speed CAN Communication Bus (-) Shorted to Bus (+)
s: CAN-High and CAN-Low on the medium-speed bus are shorted together. The differential signal collapses; body and comfort modules drop offline.

t: Medium Speed CAN Communication Bus Off
s: A controller on the medium-speed comfort bus has shut its transceiver off after too many consecutive transmit errors. Comfort and body functions stop responding until the bus recovers or is repaired.

t: Medium Speed CAN Communication Bus (+) Open
s: The CAN-High wire of the medium-speed bus is open. Body and comfort modules drop offline; the cluster typically displays multiple comfort warnings simultaneously.

t: Medium Speed CAN Communication Bus (+) Low
s: CAN-High on the medium-speed bus is shorted to ground. The differential pair cannot work, all body and comfort nodes drop offline at the same time.

t: Medium Speed CAN Communication Bus (+) High
s: CAN-High on the medium-speed bus is shorted to battery voltage. The bus is non-functional and all comfort modules drop offline.

t: Low Speed CAN Communication Bus (+) Open
s: The CAN-High wire of a low-speed comfort bus (e.g. ISO 11898-3 / fault-tolerant CAN) is open. Single-wire fallback may keep some functions alive but a fault is logged because the bus is no longer fault-tolerant.

t: Low Speed CAN Communication Bus (+) Low
s: CAN-High on the low-speed comfort bus is shorted to ground. Bus signaling is impaired or lost; affected comfort modules drop offline.

t: Low Speed CAN Communication Bus (+) High
s: CAN-High on the low-speed bus is shorted to battery. Single-wire fallback is also disturbed; comfort modules report communication faults.

t: Low Speed CAN Communication Bus (+) Shorted to Bus (-)
s: CAN-High and CAN-Low on the low-speed comfort bus are shorted together. Both differential and single-wire modes fail; comfort modules drop offline.

t: Low Speed CAN Communication Bus Off
s: The low-speed CAN controller has gone bus-off after repeated error frames. The fault-tolerant single-wire fallback is exhausted and the bus stops carrying traffic.

t: Low Speed CAN Communication Bus (-) Open
s: CAN-Low on the low-speed comfort bus is open. The bus runs in single-wire fallback only; a fault is set because fault-tolerance is lost.

t: Low Speed CAN Communication Bus (-) Low
s: CAN-Low on the low-speed bus is shorted to ground. Differential signaling is no longer reliable, modules drop offline.

t: Low Speed CAN Communication Bus (-) High
s: CAN-Low on the low-speed bus is shorted to battery. Bus signaling is no longer possible; comfort modules drop offline.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus A
s: Generic fault on vehicle communication bus A. The controller flags an unspecified electrical or protocol fault on this bus segment that does not match the more specific U0xxx subcodes.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus B
s: Generic fault on vehicle communication bus B. Used as a catch-all for unspecified problems on a secondary CAN/comfort segment.

t: Powertrain Sensor Data Bus
s: A fault has been detected on the dedicated powertrain sensor data bus (a sub-bus used for high-speed sensor data such as crank, knock or wheel-speed). Symptoms range from rough running to limp-home, depending on which sensor stream is missing.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus C (+) Open
s: The plus line of vehicle bus C is open. Modules sharing this segment drop offline. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus A (+) High
s: The communication bus A high-side line (CAN-H or equivalent) is at a higher voltage than allowed. Likely short to power or a transceiver fault that pulls the line high.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus A (-) Open
s: The communication bus A low-side line (CAN-L) is open — broken wire, unplugged connector, or terminator missing.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus A (-) Low
s: Bus A low-side line voltage below the expected range. Short to ground or transceiver pulling line low.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus A (-) High
s: Bus A low-side line voltage above the expected range. Short to power or transceiver fault. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus A (-) shorted to Bus A (+)
s: The two lines of differential bus A are shorted together. Communication impossible — both lines see the same voltage.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus B
s: Generic fault on vehicle communication bus B (second physical bus, e.g. body CAN, comfort CAN, or LIN). One or more nodes cannot communicate.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus B Performance
s: Bus B is electrically OK but message rate or error counters are outside spec. Often intermittent wiring or a degraded transceiver.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus B (+) Open
s: Bus B high-side line is open — broken wire or disconnected. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Communication Bus Off
s: A control module has counted enough transmit errors to enter the bus-off state defined by ISO 11898 and stops transmitting on the bus. Multiple modules typically lose communication and several warning lamps illuminate at once.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus B (+) High
s: Bus B high-side line voltage above expected range — short to power or transceiver fault. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus D (+) Open
s: The plus line of vehicle bus D is open. The fault is reported by the gateway when traffic from this segment disappears.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus B (-) Low
s: Bus B low-side line voltage below expected range. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus B (-) High
s: Bus B low-side line voltage above expected range. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus B (-) shorted to Bus B (+)
s: The two lines of differential bus B are shorted together. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus C
s: Generic fault on bus C (third bus, often chassis CAN or diagnostic CAN). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus C Performance
s: Bus C is electrically OK but message rate or error counters are outside spec. Often intermittent wiring or a degraded transceiver.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus C (+) Open
s: Bus C high-side line open. Same diagnostic as U0039 on bus C. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus C (+) Low
s: Bus C high-side line voltage below expected range. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus E (+) Open
s: The plus line of vehicle bus E (often a secondary safety or chassis CAN) is open. Loss of this segment usually disables related convenience or chassis features.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus C (-) Open
s: Bus C low-side line open. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus C (-) Low
s: Bus C low-side line voltage below expected range. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus C (-) High
s: Bus C low-side line voltage above expected range. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus C (-) shorted to Bus C (+)
s: Bus C lines shorted together. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus D
s: Generic fault on bus D (fourth bus, often dedicated subsystem like infotainment or driver assistance).

t: Vehicle Communication Bus D Performance
s: Bus D error counters or message rate out of spec. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus D (+) Open
s: Bus D high-side line open. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus D (+) Low
s: Bus D high-side line voltage below expected range. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus D (+) High
s: Bus D high-side line voltage above expected range. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus F (+) Open
s: The plus line of vehicle bus F is open. Used by some vehicles for body or infotainment subnets; loss disables those features.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus D (-) Low
s: Bus D low-side line voltage below expected range. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus D (-) High
s: Bus D low-side line voltage above expected range. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus D (-) shorted to Bus D (+)
s: Bus D lines shorted together. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus E
s: Generic fault on bus E (fifth bus). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus E Performance
s: Bus E error counters or message rate out of spec. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus E (+) Open
s: Bus E high-side line open. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus E (+) Low
s: Bus E high-side line voltage below expected range. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus E (+) High
s: Bus E high-side line voltage above expected range. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus E (-) Open
s: Bus E low-side line open. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Communication Bus "B" Performance
s: The secondary communication bus (Bus B, often a chassis or comfort sub-bus) shows reduced performance — high error rate, intermittent dropouts, or framing errors. Bus is still alive but unreliable.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus A (+) Bus Off
s: The CAN controller for bus A has shut its plus driver after exceeding the SAE J2284 / ISO 11898 transmit error threshold. The node disconnects from the bus until error counters recover.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus A (-) Bus Off
s: The CAN controller for bus A has gone bus-off on the minus side. Common after a CAN-L short or a faulty transceiver pulling the bus low.

t: Control Module Communication Bus "A" Off
s: A control module has detected that the communication bus is non-functional and has shut its bus interface off. Affects multiple modules at once because none can communicate; on most vehicles the dashboard lights up with several warning lamps simultaneously.

t: Control Module Communication Bus "B" Off
s: A control module has shut its bus B interface off because the bus is non-functional. Bus B is typically a secondary CAN segment (often body or chassis) and a failure disables all modules connected to it.

t: Control Module Communication Bus "B" Performance
s: Bus B is operational but performance is degraded — error frames or out-of-spec timing. Modules on this bus may behave erratically.

t: Control Module Communication Bus "C" Off
s: A control module has shut its bus C interface off because the bus is non-functional. Bus C is typically a tertiary CAN or LIN segment used for diagnostic or specialized subsystems.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus B Off
s: A controller on bus B has reached its transmit error limit and shut its transceiver off. All traffic on this segment stops until the cause is removed.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus B (+) Open
s: The plus wire of bus B is open. Modules on this segment lose communication. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Vehicle Communication Bus B (+) Low
s: The plus line of bus B is shorted to ground. The differential signal collapses and bus traffic stops.

t: Control Module Communication Bus H Off
s: The control module communication bus H is off — the gateway has shut down or isolated this bus, often due to repeated bus errors or a "bus off" event in the CAN controller.

t: Control Module Communication Bus I Off
s: The control module communication bus I is off — the gateway has shut down or isolated this bus, often due to repeated bus errors or a "bus off" event in the CAN controller.

t: Control Module Communication Bus J Off

t: Vehicle Communication Bus B (+) High

t: Lost Communication with ECM/PCM "A"
s: A control module that expects to talk to the engine ECU has stopped receiving messages. Most commonly seen by the TCM, instrument cluster, or BCM after the ECU has been disconnected, fitted with a faulty replacement, or the CAN wiring has been damaged. Engine usually will not run while this fault is active in other modules.

t: Lost Communication with TCM
s: The ECU has stopped receiving messages from the transmission control module. The transmission falls into limp-home mode (typically locked in 3rd or 4th gear), the speedometer may stop working, and shift adaptation freezes.

t: Lost Communication with Transfer Case Control Module

t: Lost Communication with Fuel Pump Control Module

t: Lost Communication With Exhaust Gas Recirculation Control Module A
s: ECU has lost CAN communication with the EGR control module A. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Exhaust Gas Recirculation Control Module B
s: ECU has lost CAN communication with the EGR control module B. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Turbocharger/Supercharger Control Module A
s: ECU has lost CAN communication with the turbocharger control module A. Boost control tracks commanded vs. actual manifold pressure; persistent deviation means wastegate, vanes, hose or actuator are not delivering the requested pressure.

t: Lost Communication With Turbocharger/Supercharger Control Module B
s: ECU has lost CAN communication with the turbocharger control module B. Boost control tracks commanded vs. actual manifold pressure; persistent deviation means wastegate, vanes, hose or actuator are not delivering the requested pressure.

t: Lost Communication With Reductant Control Module
s: ECU lost CAN communication with the AdBlue/SCR reductant control module. Emissions affected. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Air Conditioning Control Module
s: ECU lost CAN communication with the A/C control module. Climate functions degraded. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Rocker Arm Control Module A
s: ECU lost CAN communication with rocker arm control module A (variable valve actuation). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Rocker Arm Control Module B
s: ECU lost CAN communication with rocker arm control module B (variable valve actuation). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With All Wheel Drive Control Module
s: ECU lost CAN communication with the AWD control module. AWD function may revert to FWD/RWD. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Throttle Actuator B Control Module
s: ECU lost CAN communication with throttle actuator B control module. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With V2X Module
s: ECU lost CAN communication with the V2X (vehicle-to-everything) module. Connected/cooperative driving features degraded.

t: Lost Communication with Starter/Generator Control Module
s: A control module that expects to talk to the engine ECU has stopped receiving messages. Most commonly seen by the TCM, instrument cluster, or BCM after the ECU has been disconnected, fitted with a faulty replacement, or the CAN wiring has been damaged. Engine usually will not run while this fault is active in other modules.

t: Lost Communication with ABS Control Module
s: The CAN messages from the ABS module are missing. ABS, ESP/ESC and traction control all become inactive (warning lamps illuminate), the speedometer may go to zero on cars that derive vehicle speed from the ABS module, and adaptive cruise control is disabled.
