t: Lost Communication With Chassis Control Module A
s: ECU lost CAN communication with chassis control module A (vehicle dynamics). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Chassis Control Module B
s: ECU lost CAN communication with chassis control module B (vehicle dynamics). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Active Vibration Control Module
s: ECU lost CAN communication with active vibration control module (used in some hybrid drivetrains to dampen vibrations).

t: Lost Communication With Generator A Control Module
s: ECU lost CAN communication with generator (alternator) A control module. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Camshaft Position Control Module
s: ECU lost CAN communication with camshaft position control module. The camshaft position sensor identifies which cylinder is on the compression stroke — without it the ECM cannot run sequential injection or coil-on-plug ignition correctly.

t: Lost Communication With Generator B Control Module
s: ECU lost CAN communication with generator (alternator) A control module. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication with Steering Effort Control Module B
s: A control module that expects to talk to the engine ECU has stopped receiving messages. Most commonly seen by the TCM, instrument cluster, or BCM after the ECU has been disconnected, fitted with a faulty replacement, or the CAN wiring has been damaged. Engine usually will not run while this fault is active in other modules.

t: Lost Communication with Power Steering Control Module
s: A control module that expects to talk to the engine ECU has stopped receiving messages. Most commonly seen by the TCM, instrument cluster, or BCM after the ECU has been disconnected, fitted with a faulty replacement, or the CAN wiring has been damaged. Engine usually will not run while this fault is active in other modules.

t: Lost Communication with Ride Level Control Module
s: A control module that expects to talk to the engine ECU has stopped receiving messages. Most commonly seen by the TCM, instrument cluster, or BCM after the ECU has been disconnected, fitted with a faulty replacement, or the CAN wiring has been damaged. Engine usually will not run while this fault is active in other modules.

t: Lost Communication with Yaw Rate Sensor Module
s: The ESP / ABS controller has lost messages from the yaw-rate sensor module. ESP is deactivated, ABS may stay active. Often caused by a damaged ribbon cable in steering column or a failed sensor cluster after airbag jobs.

t: Lost Communication with Restraints System Sensor (Side Sensor)
s: The airbag control module has stopped receiving data from a side impact sensor. Airbag warning lamp lights up; restraint deployment for side and curtain bags may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication with Parking Brake Control Module
s: The vehicle network has lost the electric parking brake (EPB) controller. Auto-hold is disabled, the EPB warning lamp comes on. EPB cannot be released electrically.

t: Lost Communication with Park Brake Module B
s: A control module that expects to talk to the engine ECU has stopped receiving messages. Most commonly seen by the TCM, instrument cluster, or BCM after the ECU has been disconnected, fitted with a faulty replacement, or the CAN wiring has been damaged. Engine usually will not run while this fault is active in other modules.

t: Lost Communication with Trailer Brake Control Module
s: The vehicle network has lost the integrated trailer brake controller. Trailer braking falls back to passive operation; trailer-related warnings appear in the cluster.

t: Lost Communication with All Terrain Control Module
s: The terrain mode / 4WD mode controller has dropped off the bus. Off-road and traction modes can no longer be selected; vehicle defaults to standard 2WD/AWD behaviour.

t: Lost Communication with Active Roll Control Module
s: The active anti-roll bar / dynamic chassis controller has dropped off the bus. The vehicle behaves as if the system is disabled; rolling motion is not actively counteracted.

t: Lost Communication With Accelerator Pedal Module
s: ECU lost CAN communication with the accelerator pedal module. Throttle response severely affected. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Vacuum Sensor A
s: ECU lost CAN communication with vacuum sensor A (often a smart sensor with bus interface). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Vacuum Sensor B
s: ECU lost CAN communication with vacuum sensor B (often a smart sensor with bus interface). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Pedestrian Alert Control Module
s: ECU lost CAN communication with the pedestrian alert (acoustic vehicle alerting) module — required by law on EVs/hybrids at low speed.

t: Lost Communication with Body Control Module
s: CAN messages from the body control module (BCM) are missing. Effects depend on what the BCM controls — typically interior lights, wipers, central locking, immobiliser handshake. On some cars the engine refuses to start because the immobiliser handshake fails.

t: Lost Communication with Body Control Module A
s: A second body control module (BCM "A") has dropped off the bus. Several comfort, lighting and locking functions stop working until the BCM is back online.

t: Lost Communication with Body Control Module B
s: A control module that expects to talk to the engine ECU has stopped receiving messages. Most commonly seen by the TCM, instrument cluster, or BCM after the ECU has been disconnected, fitted with a faulty replacement, or the CAN wiring has been damaged. Engine usually will not run while this fault is active in other modules.

t: Lost Communication with Body Control Module C
s: A third body control module (BCM "C") has dropped off the bus. Comfort, lighting or door functions on the affected zone stop working.

t: Lost Communication with Personalization Module "B"
s: A control module that expects to talk to the engine ECU has stopped receiving messages. Most commonly seen by the TCM, instrument cluster, or BCM after the ECU has been disconnected, fitted with a faulty replacement, or the CAN wiring has been damaged. Engine usually will not run while this fault is active in other modules.

t: Lost Communication with Restraints Control Module Auxiliary
s: A secondary or auxiliary airbag/restraints controller (e.g. for rear or curtain bags) has stopped responding. The airbag warning lamp lights up and parts of the restraint system may be disabled.

t: Lost Communication with Gateway "A"
s: A control module has lost communication with the central gateway. The gateway routes messages between separate CAN/Ethernet segments — when it falls silent, large parts of the vehicle simultaneously go offline and many secondary DTCs are stored.

t: Lost Communication with Gateway "B"
s: A control module has lost communication with the central gateway. The gateway routes messages between separate CAN/Ethernet segments — when it falls silent, large parts of the vehicle simultaneously go offline and many secondary DTCs are stored.

t: Lost Communication with Body Control Module "C"
s: A control module that expects to talk to the engine ECU has stopped receiving messages. t: Lost Communication With Battery Energy Control Module C
s: ECU lost CAN communication with battery energy control module C (hybrid/EV high-voltage battery management).

t: Lost Communication With Hybrid/EV Battery Inductive Charging Module
s: ECU lost CAN communication with the inductive charging module (wireless charging of HV battery). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Battery Energy Control Module D
s: ECU lost CAN communication with battery energy control module D (hybrid/EV high-voltage battery management).

t: Lost Communication with Mirror Control Module B
s: ECU lost CAN communication with mirror control module B. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Multi-axis Acceleration Sensor Module B
s: ECU lost CAN communication with multi-axis acceleration sensor module B (used by stability and airbag systems).

t: Lost Communication with Instrument Panel Cluster
s: CAN messages from or to the instrument cluster are missing. The cluster is the user-facing interface — speedometer, tachometer, warning lamps, fuel level, odometer — so failure is immediately visible. On some cars warning lamps from other modules can no longer illuminate either.

t: Lost Communication with Cruise Control Front Distance Range Sensor
s: Adaptive cruise control has lost the front radar / distance sensor. ACC is deactivated, fixed-speed cruise may stay available, and forward collision warning is disabled.

t: Lost Communication with Steering Angle Sensor Module
s: The ESP / power steering controller has lost the steering angle sensor. ESP is deactivated and lane-keeping assistance is disabled.

t: Lost Communication with Sunroof Assembly Control Module
s: The body bus has lost the sunroof assembly module. Pinch protection and tilt/slide functions cannot be commanded.

t: Lost Communication With Digital Audio Control Module D
s: ECU lost communication with digital audio control module D (amp or zone audio controller). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Entrapment Control Module A
s: ECU lost communication with entrapment control module A (anti-pinch protection on windows/sunroof/doors).

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Module A
s: ECU lost communication with image processing module A (camera ECU for ADAS, e.g. front camera). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Module B
s: ECU lost communication with image processing module B (camera ECU for ADAS, e.g. front camera). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Module C
s: ECU lost communication with image processing module C (camera ECU for ADAS, e.g. front camera). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Front Distance Range Sensor - Left
s: ECU lost communication with the front distance range sensor (radar/lidar) on the left. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Front Distance Range Sensor - Right
s: ECU lost communication with the front distance range sensor (radar/lidar) on the left. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Entrapment Control Module B
s: ECU lost communication with entrapment control module B (anti-pinch protection on windows/sunroof/doors).

t: Lost Communication With Headlamp Control Module A
s: ECU lost communication with the headlamp control module A (matrix LED, adaptive headlights). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Headlamp Control Module B
s: ECU lost communication with the headlamp control module B (matrix LED, adaptive headlights). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Parking Assist Control Module B
s: ECU lost communication with parking assist control module B (additional ultrasonic/visual parking system).

t: Lost Communication With Running Board Control Module A
s: ECU lost communication with running board (power side step) control module A. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Entertainment Control Module - Front
s: ECU lost communication with the front entertainment control module (head unit, infotainment). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Seat Control Module E
s: ECU lost communication with seat control module E (driver seat with memory/heating/ventilation). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Seat Control Module F
s: ECU lost communication with seat control module F (driver seat with memory/heating/ventilation). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Remote Accessory Module
s: ECU lost communication with the remote accessory module (remote start, key fob). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Entertainment Control Module - Rear B
s: ECU lost communication with the rear entertainment control module B. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Lost Communication With Interior Lighting Control Module A
s: ECU lost communication with the interior lighting control module A (ambient lighting, courtesy lights).

t: Lost Communication With Seat Control Module G
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with seat control module G. Power-seat positions, memory presets and heating/ventilation requests routed through this node are no longer answered.

t: Lost Communication With Seat Control Module H
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with seat control module H. Power-seat positions, memory presets and heating/ventilation requests routed through this node are no longer answered.

t: Lost Communication With Seat Control Module J
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with seat control module J. Power-seat positions, memory presets and heating/ventilation requests routed through this node are no longer answered.

t: Lost Communication With Side Obstacle Detection Control Module C
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with side obstacle detection module C. Blind-spot warnings and lane-change assist for the corresponding zone may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Side Obstacle Detection Control Module D
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with side obstacle detection module D. Blind-spot warnings and lane-change assist for the corresponding zone may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Impact Classification System Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the impact classification module. Decisions on airbag deployment force, seat-belt pretensioning and post-crash actions can no longer use the inputs from this node.

t: Lost Communication With Running Board Control Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with running board control module B. Power-deploying running boards on the corresponding side stop responding to door-open requests.

t: Lost Communication With Lighting Control Module Rear B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the rear lighting control module B. Tail, brake, turn and reverse lamps controlled by this node may fall back to a default state or stay off.

t: Lost Communication With Accessory Protocol Interface Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the accessory protocol interface module (APIM). Infotainment, voice control and connected services that route through APIM stop responding.

t: Lost Communication With Remote Start Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the remote-start module. Engine pre-start by key fob, app or scheduled timer is no longer executed.

t: Lost Communication With Front Display Interface Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the front display interface module. Center-stack screen, climate readout and infotainment menus stop updating.

t: Lost Communication With Front Controls Interface Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the front controls interface module A. Hard-keys, rotary knob and steering-wheel switches that route through this module stop being recognised.

t: Lost Communication With Front Controls/Display Interface Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the combined front controls/display interface module. Both center screen and hard-key inputs that route through this node fail at once.

t: Lost Communication With Radio Transceiver
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the radio transceiver. Tuner, satellite radio and DAB reception that route through this transceiver stop responding.

t: Lost Communication With Special Purpose Vehicle Control Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with special-purpose vehicle (SPV) control module A. Upfit functions like emergency lighting, sirens, fleet relays or PTO/auxiliary systems controlled by this node stop responding.

t: Lost Communication With Special Purpose Vehicle Control Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with special-purpose vehicle (SPV) control module B. Upfit functions like emergency lighting, sirens, fleet relays or PTO/auxiliary systems controlled by this node stop responding.

t: Lost Communication With Special Purpose Vehicle Control Module C
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with special-purpose vehicle (SPV) control module C. Upfit functions like emergency lighting, sirens, fleet relays or PTO/auxiliary systems controlled by this node stop responding.

t: Lost Communication With Special Purpose Vehicle Control Module D
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with special-purpose vehicle (SPV) control module D. Upfit functions like emergency lighting, sirens, fleet relays or PTO/auxiliary systems controlled by this node stop responding.

t: Lost Communication With Front Controls Interface Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the front controls interface module B. Hard-keys, rotary knob and steering-wheel switches that route through this module stop being recognised.

t: Lost Communication With Electronic Brake Booster Control Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with electronic brake booster module A. Boost assist, brake-by-wire pedal feedback and regenerative-brake blending coordinated by this node may revert to a degraded mode.

t: Lost Communication With Electronic Brake Booster Control Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with electronic brake booster module B. Boost assist, brake-by-wire pedal feedback and regenerative-brake blending coordinated by this node may revert to a degraded mode.

t: Lost Communication With Seat Control Switch Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the seat control switch module A. Buttons for seat adjustment, lumbar and memory recall on this position stop responding.

t: Lost Communication With Seat Control Switch Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the seat control switch module B. Buttons for seat adjustment, lumbar and memory recall on this position stop responding.

t: Lost Communication With Audio Amplifier B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with audio amplifier B. Speakers driven by this amplifier go silent or fall back to a head-unit-only output.

t: Lost Communication With Speech Recognition Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the speech recognition module. Voice commands for navigation, phone or media stop being recognised.

t: Lost Communication With Camera Module Rear
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the rear camera module. The reverse-camera image, dynamic guide lines and any cross-traffic alert from this camera are unavailable.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Sensor A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with image processing sensor A. ADAS functions that depend on this camera (lane keeping, traffic-sign recognition, forward collision warning) may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Sensor B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with image processing sensor B. ADAS functions that depend on this camera (lane keeping, traffic-sign recognition, forward collision warning) may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Sensor C
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with image processing sensor C. ADAS functions that depend on this camera (lane keeping, traffic-sign recognition, forward collision warning) may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Sensor D
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with image processing sensor D. ADAS functions that depend on this camera (lane keeping, traffic-sign recognition, forward collision warning) may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Sensor E
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with image processing sensor E. ADAS functions that depend on this camera (lane keeping, traffic-sign recognition, forward collision warning) may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Sensor F
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with image processing sensor F. ADAS functions that depend on this camera (lane keeping, traffic-sign recognition, forward collision warning) may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Sensor G
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with image processing sensor G. ADAS functions that depend on this camera (lane keeping, traffic-sign recognition, forward collision warning) may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Sensor H
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with image processing sensor H. ADAS functions that depend on this camera (lane keeping, traffic-sign recognition, forward collision warning) may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Sensor I
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with image processing sensor I. ADAS functions that depend on this camera (lane keeping, traffic-sign recognition, forward collision warning) may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Sensor J
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with image processing sensor J. ADAS functions that depend on this camera (lane keeping, traffic-sign recognition, forward collision warning) may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Sensor K
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with image processing sensor K. ADAS functions that depend on this camera (lane keeping, traffic-sign recognition, forward collision warning) may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Image Processing Sensor L
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with image processing sensor L. ADAS functions that depend on this camera (lane keeping, traffic-sign recognition, forward collision warning) may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Seat Control Switch Module C
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the seat control switch module C. Buttons for seat adjustment, lumbar and memory recall on this position stop responding.

t: Lost Communication With Seat Control Switch Module D
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the seat control switch module D. Buttons for seat adjustment, lumbar and memory recall on this position stop responding.

t: Lost Communication With Lighting Driver Control Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with lighting driver control module A. LED matrix segments and adaptive headlamp functions driven by this node may default to a fallback beam pattern.

t: Lost Communication With Lighting Driver Control Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with lighting driver control module B. LED matrix segments and adaptive headlamp functions driven by this node may default to a fallback beam pattern.

t: Lost Communication With Lighting Driver Control Module C
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with lighting driver control module C. LED matrix segments and adaptive headlamp functions driven by this node may default to a fallback beam pattern.

t: Lost Communication With Lighting Driver Control Module D
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with lighting driver control module D. LED matrix segments and adaptive headlamp functions driven by this node may default to a fallback beam pattern.

t: Lost Communication With Seat Control Module K
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with seat control module K. Power-seat positions, memory presets and heating/ventilation requests routed through this node are no longer answered.

t: Lost Communication With Seat Control Module L
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with seat control module L. Power-seat positions, memory presets and heating/ventilation requests routed through this node are no longer answered.

t: Lost Communication With Haptic Control Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the haptic control module. Vibration feedback in steering wheel, seat or pedals (lane-departure, blind-spot, parking) stops being delivered.

t: Lost Communication With Battery Charger Control Module C
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with battery charger control module C. AC/DC charging coordination for the high-voltage battery via this node is suspended.

t: Lost Communication With Battery Charger Control Module D
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with battery charger control module D. AC/DC charging coordination for the high-voltage battery via this node is suspended.

t: Lost Communication With Off-Board Charger Control Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the off-board charger module. Negotiation with external DC fast-charge stations (CCS, CHAdeMO) and the resulting charging session cannot start.

t: Lost Communication With Active Grille Air Shutter Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with active grille shutter module A. The aerodynamic flaps in front of the radiator stay in their default position, increasing drag and possibly affecting cooling.

t: Lost Communication With Active Grille Air Shutter Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with active grille shutter module B. The aerodynamic flaps in front of the radiator stay in their default position, increasing drag and possibly affecting cooling.

t: Lost Communication With Radiator Anti-Tamper Device
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the radiator anti-tamper device. Detection of unauthorised changes to the cooling system can no longer be confirmed by this node.

t: Lost Communication With Transmission Fluid Pump Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with transmission fluid pump module A. Auxiliary ATF pumping during start/stop or hybrid coast cannot be commanded, which may delay shifts or impair cooling.

t: Lost Communication With DC/AC Converter Control Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with DC/AC inverter module A. Drive-motor torque commands or AC-outlet supply that route through this inverter are unavailable, putting the EV/hybrid drivetrain into a degraded mode.

t: Lost Communication With DC/AC Converter Control Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with DC/AC inverter module B. Drive-motor torque commands or AC-outlet supply that route through this inverter are unavailable, putting the EV/hybrid drivetrain into a degraded mode.

t: Lost Communication With Transmission Fluid Pump Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with transmission fluid pump module B. Auxiliary ATF pumping during start/stop or hybrid coast cannot be commanded, which may delay shifts or impair cooling.

t: Lost Communication With Active Grille Air Shutter Module C
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with active grille shutter module C. The aerodynamic flaps in front of the radiator stay in their default position, increasing drag and possibly affecting cooling.

t: Lost Communication With Active Grille Air Shutter Module D
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with active grille shutter module D. The aerodynamic flaps in front of the radiator stay in their default position, increasing drag and possibly affecting cooling.

t: Lost Communication With Active Aerodynamic Feature Control Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with active aerodynamic feature module A. Powered spoilers, air-dams or wheel-arch closures controlled by this node default to a fixed position.

t: Lost Communication With Active Aerodynamic Feature Control Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with active aerodynamic feature module B. Powered spoilers, air-dams or wheel-arch closures controlled by this node default to a fixed position.

t: Lost Communication With Active Aerodynamic Feature Control Module C
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with active aerodynamic feature module C. Powered spoilers, air-dams or wheel-arch closures controlled by this node default to a fixed position.

t: Lost Communication With Active Aerodynamic Feature Control Module D
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with active aerodynamic feature module D. Powered spoilers, air-dams or wheel-arch closures controlled by this node default to a fixed position.

t: Lost Communication With Gear Shift Control Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with gear shift control module B. Shift-by-wire requests for park/reverse/drive coordinated through this node are no longer answered.

t: Lost Communication With Drive Motor Control Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with drive motor control module B. Torque commands for the second EV/hybrid traction motor cannot be coordinated, forcing the powertrain into a degraded mode.

t: Lost Communication with Hybrid Vehicle Control System
s: The hybrid vehicle network has lost the hybrid control unit (HCU). The vehicle drops out of hybrid operation, often forced into a fail-safe mode that limits torque.

t: Lost Communication with Powertrain Control Module B
s: A second powertrain control module (e.g. on dual-engine, twin-turbo bank-specific or range-extender vehicles) has dropped off the network. Engine power is reduced or limp-home mode is forced.

t: Lost Communication with Power Steering Control Module
s: The vehicle network has lost the electric power steering (EPS) control module. Power assist drops out, the steering becomes heavy and the EPS warning lamp lights up.

t: Lost Communication With AC/DC Converter Control Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with AC/DC converter (on-board charger) module A. Charging from AC sources cannot be controlled and the high-voltage battery does not refill via this path.

t: Lost Communication with Hybrid Powertrain Control Module
s: The hybrid CAN has lost the hybrid powertrain control module. Hybrid operation is disabled and the vehicle defaults to combustion-only mode if available.

t: Lost Communication With DC/DC Converter Control Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with DC/DC converter module A. The converter that supplies the 12-V system from the high-voltage battery cannot be commanded; 12-V load shedding may begin.

t: Lost Communication With DC/DC Converter Control Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with DC/DC converter module B. The converter that supplies the 12-V system from the high-voltage battery cannot be commanded; 12-V load shedding may begin.

t: Lost Communication With Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Sensor Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the high-voltage battery pack sensor module. Cell-voltage and temperature data needed for state-of-charge and state-of-health calculation are missing.

t: Lost Communication With Drive Motor Control Module C
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with drive motor control module C. Torque commands for this EV/hybrid traction motor cannot be coordinated, forcing the powertrain into a degraded mode.

t: Lost Communication With Drive Motor Control Module D
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with drive motor control module D. Torque commands for this EV/hybrid traction motor cannot be coordinated, forcing the powertrain into a degraded mode.

t: Lost Communication With NOx Sensor A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with NOx sensor A (smart sensor with own ECU). The SCR catalyst control loop loses its actual NOx feedback and may switch to substitute values.

t: Lost Communication With NOx Sensor B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with NOx sensor B (smart sensor with own ECU). The SCR catalyst control loop loses its actual NOx feedback and may switch to substitute values.

t: Lost Communication With Evaporative Emission System Leak Detection Control Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the EVAP leak detection module. Tank-pressure / vacuum diagnostics for fuel-vapour leaks cannot be commanded.

t: Invalid Data Received From Hybrid/EV Battery Interface Control Module D
s: A receiving module detected implausible data from hybrid/EV battery interface module D. Cell-voltage, current or temperature values fail plausibility checks against other inputs and are rejected.

t: Lost Communication With NOx Sensor C
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with NOx sensor C (smart sensor with own ECU). The SCR catalyst control loop loses its actual NOx feedback and may switch to substitute values.

t: Lost Communication With Reductant Quality Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the reductant (DEF/AdBlue) quality sensor module. The SCR system cannot verify urea concentration; emissions countermeasures may activate.

t: Lost Communication With Particulate Matter Sensor
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the particulate matter (PM) sensor (smart sensor with own ECU). DPF post-cat soot monitoring loses its primary feedback signal.

t: Lost Communication With NH3 Sensor
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the ammonia (NH3) slip sensor downstream of the SCR. Detection of urea over-dosing is impaired.

t: Lost Communication With Reductant Heater Control Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the reductant heater module. AdBlue lines and tank can no longer be thawed at low temperatures, blocking SCR operation in winter.

t: Lost Communication With Safety Integration Control Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the safety integration module that arbitrates between ADAS, restraint, brake and steering systems. Cross-system safety functions may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Engine Sound Control 1
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with engine sound control unit 1. Active sound design (interior synthetic engine sound or exterior pedestrian alert) for this channel stops.

t: Lost Communication With Engine Sound Control 2
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with engine sound control unit 2. Active sound design (interior synthetic engine sound or exterior pedestrian alert) for this channel stops.

t: Lost Communication With Charge Air Cooler Coolant Pump A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with charge air cooler (intercooler) coolant pump A. Intake-charge cooling on indirect intercooler systems cannot be controlled, raising knock risk and reducing power.

t: Lost Communication With Engine Disconnect Clutch
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the hybrid engine disconnect clutch actuator. Switching between EV-only and combustion drive can no longer be commanded smoothly.

t: Lost Communication With Vehicle Thermal Management Control Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the vehicle thermal management module. Coordination of engine, battery and cabin cooling/heating loops is lost; sub-modules fall back to local control.

t: Lost Communication With Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost Control A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with boost control module A (electric wastegate or VTG actuator). Boost pressure cannot be commanded; the engine reverts to a low-power fallback.

t: Lost Communication With Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost Control B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with boost control module B (electric wastegate or VTG actuator). Boost pressure cannot be commanded; the engine reverts to a low-power fallback.

t: Lost Communication With Hybrid/EV Battery Coolant Pump
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the high-voltage battery coolant pump. Active battery cooling cannot be regulated; charging/discharging power may be limited to protect the cells.

t: Lost Communication With Turbocharger Turbine Inlet Valve Position Sensor
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the turbocharger turbine inlet valve position sensor. Position feedback for variable inlet geometry on twin-scroll/sequential turbo systems is missing.

t: Lost Communication With Turbocharger/Supercharger Boost Control B Position Sensor
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the boost control B position sensor. Closed-loop control of the wastegate/VTG actuator on this turbo loses its actual feedback signal.

t: Lost Communication With Comfort and Convenience Display A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with comfort and convenience display A (typically a rear-seat or passenger screen for climate, audio, ambient lighting). It freezes or goes blank.

t: Lost Communication With Comfort and Convenience Display B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with comfort and convenience display B (typically a rear-seat or passenger screen for climate, audio, ambient lighting). It freezes or goes blank.

t: Lost Communication With Driver Monitoring System Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the driver monitoring system (DMS) module. Eye/head-pose tracking for drowsiness, distraction and hands-on detection is unavailable, and dependent ADAS handover features may be inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Front Radar Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with front radar module A. Adaptive cruise control, automatic emergency braking and forward collision warning lose their primary range/closing-speed input.

t: Lost Communication With Front Radar Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with front radar module B. Adaptive cruise control, automatic emergency braking and forward collision warning lose their primary range/closing-speed input.

t: Lost Communication With Rear Radar Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with rear radar module A. Blind-spot warning, lane-change assist and rear cross-traffic alert dependent on this sensor are inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Rear Radar Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with rear radar module B. Blind-spot warning, lane-change assist and rear cross-traffic alert dependent on this sensor are inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Rear Radar Module C
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with rear radar module C. Blind-spot warning, lane-change assist and rear cross-traffic alert dependent on this sensor are inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Rear Radar Module D
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with rear radar module D. Blind-spot warning, lane-change assist and rear cross-traffic alert dependent on this sensor are inhibited.

t: Lost Communication With Hybrid/EV Electronics Coolant Pump B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the hybrid/EV power-electronics coolant pump B. Inverters and DC/DC stages on this loop lose active cooling and may derate.

t: Lost Communication With EVAP System Purge Pump
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the EVAP purge pump (used on systems with electric purging instead of intake-vacuum). Vapour purge from the carbon canister cannot be commanded.

t: Lost Communication With Localization and Mapping Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the localization and mapping module (HD map / SLAM ECU for ADAS and autonomous functions). Lane-level localization and map-based predictions are unavailable.

t: Lost Communication With Night Vision Control Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the night vision module. The infrared camera image and pedestrian highlighting in the head-up or cluster display are unavailable.

t: Lost Communication With Trailer Backup Assist Control Module
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the trailer backup assist module. Steer-by-knob trailer reverse guidance, hitch angle sensing and trajectory planning are unavailable.

t: Lost Communication With Interior Lighting Control Module B
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with interior lighting control module B. Ambient lighting zones, reading lamps and door courtesy lights driven by this node fall back to a default state.

t: Lost Communication With Active Radiator Air Flow Control Module A
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with active radiator air flow control module A. Active grille shutters and electric cooling-fan staging on this circuit cannot be commanded.

t: Lost Communication With Hybrid/EV Electronics Coolant Pump C
s: The vehicle bus reports loss of communication with the hybrid/EV power-electronics coolant pump C. Inverters and DC/DC stages on this loop lose active cooling and may derate.

t: Internal Control Module Software Incompatibility
s: A control module has detected that its software version is incompatible with another module on the bus. Most often after a partial update, mismatched coding, or fitting a used module from a different vehicle.

t: Software Incompatibility with ECM/PCM
s: A control module has detected that its software/calibration is incompatible with the engine ECM/PCM. Typically appears after a partial flash, mismatched coding, or fitting a used ECM from a different vehicle.

t: Software Incompatibility with TCM
s: A control module has detected that its software/calibration is incompatible with the transmission control module. Often appears after a TCM swap or partial flash.

t: Software Incompatibility with Transfer Case Control Module
s: A control module has detected that its software/calibration is incompatible with the transfer case control module. AWD/4WD modes may not work correctly until matching software is flashed.

t: Invalid Data Received from Transmission Control Module
s: The ECU receives messages from the TCM but their content is implausible (e.g. invalid gear, impossible speed). The transmission may switch to limp-home mode and shift quality is degraded.

t: Software Incompatibility with Cruise Control Module
s: A control module has detected that its software/calibration is incompatible with the cruise control module. Cruise control may be disabled until matching software is flashed.

t: Software Incompatibility with Fuel Injector Control Module
s: The injector driver module identifies itself with a software version that does not match what the engine ECU expects. Engine may not start or runs roughly until both modules are reflashed to compatible versions.

t: Software Incompatibility with Glow Plug Control Module
s: The glow plug controller and the engine ECU run incompatible software versions. Cold-start preheating may be inhibited and the glow plug warning lamp can stay on.

t: Software Incompatibility with Body Control Module
s: The BCM software does not match what other vehicle modules expect. Comfort or lighting features can behave erratically until reflashing or coding is performed.

t: Software Incompatibility With Alternative Fuel Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the alternative fuel (CNG/LPG/H2) control module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Evaporative Emission System Leak Detection Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the EVAP leak detection module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Glow Plug Control Module 2
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with glow plug control module 2 — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Reductant Quality Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the reductant quality module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With NOx Sensor A
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with NOx sensor A (smart sensor with own ECU) — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With NOx Sensor B
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with NOx sensor B (smart sensor with own ECU) — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Reductant Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the reductant control module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Fuel Pump Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the fuel pump control module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Drive Motor Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the drive motor control module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Battery Energy Control Module A
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with battery energy control module A — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Battery Energy Control Module B
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with battery energy control module B — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Four-Wheel Drive Clutch Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the 4WD/AWD clutch control module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility with ABS Control Module
s: A control module has detected that its software/calibration is incompatible with the ABS module. ABS, ESP/ESC and traction control may be disabled until matching software is flashed.

t: Software Incompatibility with Park Brake Control Module
s: A control module has detected that its software version does not match the parking brake module. Most often after replacing the EPB unit with one from a different model year or coding variant.

t: Software Incompatibility with Brake System Control Module
s: A control module has detected that its software version does not match the ABS/brake system module. Typically after a partial flash or fitting an unmatched ABS unit.

t: Software Incompatibility with Power Steering Control Module
s: A control module has detected that its software version does not match the electric power steering module. Steering may stay functional but become heavy if the EPS goes into a fallback mode.

t: Software Incompatibility with Anti-Lock Brake System Control Module
s: The ABS controller advertises a software version not supported by the rest of the vehicle network. ABS, ESP and ASR functions are usually disabled until coding is corrected.

t: Software Incompatibility With Transmission Range Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the transmission range control module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With DC/DC Converter Control Module A
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with DC/DC converter control module A — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Brake System Control Module B
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with brake system control module B — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Parking Assist Control Module A
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with parking assist module A — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Parking Assist Control Module B
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with parking assist module B — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Accessory Protocol Interface Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the accessory protocol interface module (APIM) — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Power Steering Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the power steering control module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Suspension Control Module A
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with suspension control module A — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility with Body Control Module B
s: A second body control module (BCM "B") is running software that other modules do not accept. Some comfort and convenience functions stop working until coded correctly.

t: Software Incompatibility With Instrument Panel Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the instrument panel control module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility with HVAC Control Module
s: The HVAC controller is running software incompatible with the rest of the network. Climate functions may default to a basic operating mode.

t: Software Incompatibility with Auxiliary Heater Control Module
s: The auxiliary heater module is running incompatible software. The pre-heater may not start or aborts operation early.

t: Software Incompatibility With Vehicle Immobilizer Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the vehicle immobilizer control module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network. The engine may refuse to start.

t: Software Incompatibility with Powertrain Control Module
s: A control module has detected that its software version does not match the engine/powertrain ECU. Typically after a partial flash, mismatched coding, or fitting a used ECM from a different vehicle.

t: Software Incompatibility With Steering Angle Sensor Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the steering angle sensor module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Steering Column Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the steering column control module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With A/C Compressor Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the A/C compressor control module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Tire Pressure Monitor Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the tire pressure monitor (TPMS) module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Body Control Module A
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with body control module A (BCM) — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Multi-axis Acceleration Sensor Module A

t: Software Incompatibility With Gear Shift Control Module B
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with gear shift control module B — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Radio
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the radio/head unit — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Hybrid/EV Battery Pack Sensor Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the high-voltage battery pack sensor module — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Restraints Control Module
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with the restraints control module (airbag/SRS) — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Battery Charger Control Module A
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with battery charger control module A — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.

t: Software Incompatibility With Body Control Module B

t: Software Incompatibility With Hybrid/EV Battery Interface Control Module J
s: A module reports a software version mismatch with hybrid/EV battery interface module J — typically after replacing one of the modules without programming the new unit to match the rest of the network.
