t: Invalid Data from Ion Sense Module
s: A receiving module detected implausible data from the ion sense module (used for misfire/knock detection via ionisation current at the spark plug). Per-cylinder ion current values fail plausibility checks and are rejected.

t: Control Module
s: Generic fault on the control module itself — internal self-diagnostic flagged a problem with computation, memory, drivers or other internal subsystems.

t: Control Module Improper Shutdown Performance
s: The control module did not complete its shutdown sequence cleanly — power was lost before housekeeping (saving learned values, freeze frames, adaptations) finished.

t: Vehicle Identification Number
s: The control module reports a problem with the stored Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) — missing, mismatched between modules, or invalid format.

t: Battery Voltage
s: The control module detected battery voltage outside its acceptable operating window. Either too low (drained battery, weak charging) or too high (charging system overvoltage).

t: Accessory Power Relay
s: Fault on the accessory power relay that switches power to comfort/accessory loads (radio, infotainment, etc.) when the key is in ACC position.

t: Retained Accessory Power
s: Fault on the retained accessory power (RAP) function that keeps comfort loads (windows, radio) powered for a short time after the key is removed.

t: Control Module Input Power A Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on the input power line A of the control module. The module is missing or losing its main supply on this rail.

t: Control Module Input Power B Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on input power line B of the control module (second supply rail, e.g. permanent +12V vs switched battery feed).

t: Control Module Ground A
s: The control module detects a problem on its ground reference A — open ground, high resistance to chassis, or floating ground potential.

t: Control Module Ground B
s: The control module detects a problem on its ground reference A — open ground, high resistance to chassis, or floating ground potential.

t: Ignition Switch
s: The control module detects an inconsistent or implausible ignition switch state — multiple positions reported simultaneously, or the position contradicts other inputs.

t: Ignition Input Accessory/On/Start
s: Fault on the combined ACC/ON/START ignition input line of the control module. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Ignition Input Off/On/Start
s: Fault on the OFF/ON/START ignition input line. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Ignition Input On/Start
s: Fault on the ON/START ignition input line — module sees inconsistent ON or START signal. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Ignition Input On
s: Fault on the ignition ON input — module does not see ON state when other inputs say ON, or vice versa.

t: Ignition Input Accessory
s: Fault on the ignition ACC (accessory) input. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Ignition Input Start
s: Fault on the ignition START input — module does not see start request when cranking, or sees one when not cranking.

t: Ignition Input Off
s: Fault on the ignition OFF input — module does not detect OFF state correctly. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Control Module Improper Wake-up Performance
s: The control module did not wake up cleanly from sleep mode — wake event timing or sequence outside spec.

t: Control Module Input Power C Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on input power line C of the control module (third supply rail, e.g. for redundant power on safety-critical modules).

t: Control Module Input Power D Circuit/Open
s: Open circuit on input power line D of the control module (fourth supply rail). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Control Module Ground C
s: Fault on the third ground reference of the module. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Control Module Ground D
s: Fault on the fourth ground reference of the module. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Control Module Timer/Clock Performance
s: The control module's internal timer or clock has drifted or stopped — affects time-stamped diagnostic events, scheduled tasks and security challenges.

t: Control Module Input Power Circuit A/B Correlation
s: Voltages on input power lines A and B do not correlate as expected — one is significantly different from the other.

t: Control Module Input Power Circuit B/C Correlation
s: Voltages on input power lines B and C do not correlate as expected. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Control Module Wake-up Circuit A/Open
s: Open circuit on the wake-up signal line A — module cannot be woken from sleep via this path. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Control Module Wake-up Circuit A Low
s: Wake-up signal A voltage below expected range — short to ground. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Control Module Wake-up Circuit A High
s: Wake-up signal A voltage above expected range — short to power. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Control Module Wake-up Circuit A Performance
s: Wake-up signal A is electrically OK but timing or pattern does not match expected behavior. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Control Module Wake-up Circuit B/Open

t: Control Module Wake-up Circuit B Low

s: Wake-up signal B voltage above expected range. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Control Module Input Power Circuit A - Ignition Input On/Start Circuit Correlation
s: Implausible relationship between input power line A and the ON/START ignition signal — power present without ignition active, or vice versa.

t: Control Module Input Power Circuit B - Ignition Input On/Start Circuit Correlation
s: Implausible relationship between input power line A and the ON/START ignition signal — power present without ignition active, or vice versa.

t: Control Module Security Certificate Missing/Invalid
s: The control module's security certificate (used for authenticated communication, secure boot or vehicle anti-tamper) is missing or fails validation.

t: Communication Authentication Key/Certificate Missing/Invalid
s: The cryptographic key or certificate used to authenticate inter-module communication is missing or invalid.

t: Communication Authentication Signal Invalid Data
s: The received authentication signal contains invalid data — message authentication code does not match expected value, suggesting tampering or corruption.

t: Communication Authentication Signal Performance
s: Authentication signal arrives within tolerances but timing or content drifted from expected behavior.

t: High Voltage System Interlock Circuit B

t: Hydrogen Tank High Pressure Sensor 1 Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the high-pressure hydrogen tank pressure sensor 1 (typical operating range up to 700 bar on FCEVs).

t: Hydrogen Tank A Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on temperature sensor 1 of hydrogen tank A. Temperature is needed to compute mass from pressure (PV=nRT).

t: Hydrogen Tank A Temperature Sensor 2 Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on temperature sensor 2 of hydrogen tank A. Temperature is needed to compute mass from pressure (PV=nRT).

t: Hydrogen Tank B Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on temperature sensor 1 of hydrogen tank A. Temperature is needed to compute mass from pressure (PV=nRT).

t: Hydrogen Tank B Temperature Sensor 2 Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on temperature sensor 2 of hydrogen tank A. Temperature is needed to compute mass from pressure (PV=nRT).

t: Hydrogen Tank C Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on temperature sensor 1 of hydrogen tank A. Temperature is needed to compute mass from pressure (PV=nRT).

t: Hydrogen Tank C Temperature Sensor 2 Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on temperature sensor 2 of hydrogen tank A. Temperature is needed to compute mass from pressure (PV=nRT).

t: Hydrogen Tank A Valve Control Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the control circuit of the hydrogen tank A shut-off valve. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Hydrogen Tank A Valve Enable Circuit
s: Fault on the enable line that authorises the hydrogen tank A valve to open. Used as redundant safety release.

t: Hydrogen Tank A Valve Performance
s: Hydrogen tank A valve does not open or close as commanded — mechanical failure detected via downstream pressure feedback.

t: Hydrogen Tank B Valve Control Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the control circuit of the hydrogen tank A shut-off valve. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Hydrogen Tank B Valve Enable Circuit
s: Fault on the enable line that authorises the hydrogen tank A valve to open. Used as redundant safety release.

t: Hydrogen Tank B Valve Performance
s: Hydrogen tank A valve does not open or close as commanded — mechanical failure detected via downstream pressure feedback.

t: Hydrogen Tank C Valve Control Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the control circuit of the hydrogen tank A shut-off valve. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Hydrogen Tank C Valve Enable Circuit
s: Fault on the enable line that authorises the hydrogen tank A valve to open. Used as redundant safety release.

t: Hydrogen Tank C Valve
s: Generic fault reported on hydrogen tank C valve — combines circuit and mechanical performance issues into one code.

t: Air Inlet Pressure Sensor Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the air inlet pressure sensor of the fuel cell air supply (cathode side, before compressor).

t: Charge Air Cooler Outlet Pressure Sensor 1 Circuit
s: Generic fault on charge air cooler outlet pressure sensor 1 (after CAC, before stack on FCEV air path).

t: Charge Air Cooler Outlet Pressure Sensor 2 Circuit
s: Generic fault on charge air cooler outlet pressure sensor 2 (after CAC, before stack on FCEV air path).

t: Charge Air Cooler Outlet Temperature Sensor Circuit
s: Generic fault on the charge air cooler outlet temperature sensor in the FCEV cathode supply path. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Expander Inlet Pressure Sensor 1 Circuit
s: Generic fault on the inlet pressure sensor 1 of the expander (turbine recovering energy from the cathode exhaust on FCEVs).

t: Expander Inlet Pressure Sensor 2 Circuit
s: Generic fault on the inlet pressure sensor 2 of the expander (turbine recovering energy from the cathode exhaust on FCEVs).

t: Cathode Backpressure Valve Control Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the cathode back-pressure valve control. The valve regulates pressure on the cathode (air) side of the fuel cell stack.

t: Cathode Backpressure Valve Position Sensor Circuit
s: Generic fault on the position sensor of the cathode back-pressure valve (closed-loop position feedback).

t: Cathode Bypass Valve Control Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the cathode bypass valve control. Bypass routes air around the stack during startup or low-load operation.

t: Cathode Bypass Valve
s: Generic fault on the cathode bypass valve — combines circuit and mechanical performance. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Cathode Bypass Valve Position Sensor Circuit
s: Generic fault on the position sensor of the cathode bypass valve. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Hydrogen Supply Line A Pressure Sensor 1 Circuit
s: Generic fault on the pressure sensor 1 in hydrogen supply line A — feeds the fuel cell anode after the tank's pressure regulator.

t: Hydrogen Supply Line A Pressure Sensor 2 Circuit
s: Generic fault on the pressure sensor 2 in hydrogen supply line A — feeds the fuel cell anode after the tank's pressure regulator.

t: Anode Inlet Pressure Sensor 1 Circuit
s: Generic fault on the anode inlet pressure sensor 1 (hydrogen pressure into the stack). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Anode Inlet Pressure Sensor 2 Circuit
s: Generic fault on the anode inlet pressure sensor 2 (hydrogen pressure into the stack). A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Anode Multifunction Valve Control Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the anode multifunction valve. This valve combines shut-off, pressure regulation and recirculation control on the hydrogen side.

t: Anode Multifunction Valve
s: Generic fault on the anode multifunction valve — combines circuit and mechanical issues. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Anode Drain Valve Control Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the anode drain valve control. The drain valve releases liquid water and inert gases that accumulate in the anode loop.

t: Anode Drain Valve
s: Generic fault on the anode drain valve — combines circuit and mechanical issues. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Anode Header Drain Valve Control Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the control of the anode header drain valve (drains the manifold/header line).

t: Anode Header Drain Valve
s: Generic fault on the anode header drain valve. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Anode Drain Valve Heater Relay Control Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the heater relay for the anode drain valve. The heater prevents drain water from freezing in cold weather.

t: Anode Header Drain Valve Heater Relay Control Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the heater relay for the anode drain valve. The heater prevents drain water from freezing in cold weather.

t: Stack Inlet Coolant Temperature Sensor Circuit
s: Generic fault on the coolant temperature sensor at the fuel cell stack inlet (deionized coolant cools the membrane electrode assemblies).

t: Stack Outlet Coolant Temperature Sensor 1 Circuit
s: Generic fault on coolant temperature sensor 1 at the fuel cell stack outlet (used to monitor stack thermal output).

t: Stack Outlet Coolant Temperature Sensor 2 Circuit
s: Generic fault on coolant temperature sensor 2 at the fuel cell stack outlet (used to monitor stack thermal output).

t: Coolant Mixing Valve Control Circuit
s: Generic electrical fault on the coolant mixing valve control. The valve blends hot and cold coolant streams to maintain stack temperature.

t: Coolant Mixing Valve
s: Generic fault on the coolant mixing valve — combines circuit and mechanical issues. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Coolant Mixing Valve Position Sensor Circuit
s: Generic fault on the position sensor of the coolant mixing valve. A communication loss with this module breaks any function relying on its data — diagnostic tools may show 'no response' and dependent modules can fall into limp mode.

t: Stack Differential Pressure Sensor Circuit
s: Generic fault on the differential pressure sensor across the fuel cell stack — measures the pressure drop between cathode and anode (used to detect membrane damage or flooding).
